InfoNet Back Office / Overview

Official Documentation

InfoNet Back Office
User Manual

The complete manager's operations guide — inventory, reports, fleet card management, policies, and end-of-day procedures.

Chapter 1

🚀 Getting Started

CHAPTER 1

Overview

Back Office was designed as a means of accessing, maintaining, and monitoring store business. The general Back Office user is a store manager, assistant manager, or system administrator. Occasionally, store employees may have access to time maintenance functions. From one central server, you can access and manage employees’ status and roles, items, pricing, and store transactions. You can also perform basic C-Store Commander Back Office such as Inventory Management, POS Screen Setup, Accessing Reports, Routine Maintenance and Data Maintenance for advanced POS operations. This chapter provides an introduction to Back Office, including navigational tools, screen region functions, and an overview of the Back Office features, which allows you to successfully access and manage C-Store Commander Back Office. Topics in this chapter are:

• “Getting Started”

• “User Interface”

• “Security and Errors”

Getting Started

Starting the Application

To access Back Office, open a Back Office Computer by double clicking the Back Office application icon on your desktop. See BackOffice icon below.

Logging In/Out

You must log in to Back Office before any of the functions can be accessed. Logging in is done from the Login Screen that appears when Back Office is launched or after a screen timeout. You must also log out of Back Office when you wish to terminate use of the application.

Logging In

Enter your personal User ID and password on the Login Screen and click Login. The application displays the Home page if it accepts your ID and password. Before signing in to the back-office Web site, you will have registered with us and received the following identification information:

  • User name
  • Password

📷 Figure 1-1 Login Screen

Logging Out

Click the Exit button on the top right hand corner of the screen. The application logs out the current user and displays the window screen.

User Interface

Screen Regions

Back Office contains several screen regions listed in the following table.

Screen RegionDescription
Title barDisplays name of the application.
Menu barLocated below Content Window having distinct image. Displays the main function.
Content WindowThe main part of the screen; it displays dynamic information and action buttons.

Navigation

Knowing the screen regions and how to access them makes Back Office easy to navigate. To select a tab, link, or button, left-click (single-click with a Mac mouse) the object. Selecting one of these objects accesses its functionality.

📷 Figure 1-2 Navigation

Main Tabs

The main tabs provide the main functions of Back Office. The following features are accessible from the main Tabs.

Screen RegionDefinition
InventoryAllows you to update and track Inventory items entered into the database.
POS Screen SetupAllows you to have better POS user experience
ReportsAllows you to view various embedded and generate reports.
Data MaintenanceAllows you to program configuration of your POS.
PolicyAllows you to change business rules that has action on your POS.
Routine MaintenanceAllows you to view and operate day-to-day business operations.
FreezeAllows you to temporarily lock the computer
ExitAllows you to exit in using the application.

Security and Errors

User Messages

A message is displayed to notify you of an error or task completion. Common error messages include not entering specified fields or an incorrect User ID or password. You must correct the specified error before continuing.

Screen Timeout

If you attempt to perform a task after several minutes of inactivity, the screen defers back to the login screen for security purposes. Based on policy settings the screen timeout can be configured as per user’s request. If the screen returns to the login screen, enter your User ID and password. The Home page or the last screen viewed is displayed.

Chapter 2

📦 Inventory

CHAPTER 2

Inventory

Back Office provides the functionality to maintain the store database of stock items and kits that are available in the store. A kit is a collection of items grouped under one price whereas stock item is single individual item. You can search for and modify existing items or kits as well as add stock items or kits to the database. You can also add or modify the stock items included in a kit. The Promotional option allows you to setup promotional items menu option where you canedit promotional item price, and promotional rule options. Promotional items are temporary price reductions for a set period of time while price changes are permanent changes in the price of an item or group of items. Promotional rules are used to apply promotion to combinations of items or multiple items for a temporary time period. Topics in this chapter are:

• “Overview”

• “Stock Items”

• “Stock Receiving”

• “Stock Takes”

• “Purchasing and Receiving”

• “Promotional Items”

• “Kits”

• “Product Categories”

• “Price Book Management”

• “Verify Stock Files”

Overview

The key to successfully managing your inventory is to maintain an up-to-date, comprehensive database. As you adjust your inventory, you should continually update your database as a standard practice to get better reports. From the Inventory menu options, where all inventory is ordered, received and accounted for.

📷 Figure 2-1 Inventory Menu

Stock Items

Searching for Stock Item

You can search for items by item number or description etc. A search must be made before you can add an item or kit to the database to avoid duplication. There are various options available in Stock Item to search the product. Below is the list of options available.

Feature NameDescription
Stock CodeSearch by stock code or PLU number. A stock item is annotated by stock code, also

referred as PLU and stands for “Price Look-up”. When the barcode of an inventory item is scanned or a cashier enter its product code into the system, C-Store look up its price in the database.

DescriptionSearch by short or long description of stock item. Example: Coca-Cola
KeywordSearch by keyword of stock item. Example: Canadian Classics
Vendor PartSearch by additional vendor supplier information.
Department, Sub-DepartSearch by department the stock item belongs to. Example: Beverages
VendorSearch by supplier’s information. Example: Coremark
Alternate CodeSearch by entering additional information that describes the stock item code.
Price TypeSearch by selecting list of options available after choosing Price Type.
Sale Items OnlySearch by items on sale. Stock Items listed after selecting this option display special

pricing in the details tab of pertinent stock item.

Tax Exempt Items OnlySearch by Tax Exempt Items; This products have differential pricing based Tax

Exemption settings applied on details tab of pertinent stock item.

Scalable Items OnlySearch by the item where capacity to be changed in size or scale.
Inactive Items OnlySearch by the inactive item. Marking an item as inactive means that you can't select

it on new transactions.

To search for a stock item:

  1. 1.On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items.
  2. 2.Click on the information fields to see the available choice according to the selected searching criteria or enter the information manually (Example: Stock Code, Description, Keyword, Vendor Part etc.). Search Criteria descriptions is discussed in detail in the above table.
  3. Once the search criteria have been entered, the search results will show at the bottom of the screen.
  4. Click Details button to get Cost, Price and Quantity Available for each product.

📷 Figure 2-2 Inventory Search Window

  • Click inventory > Stock Items > Add button.
  • Enter or Scan the Stock Code followed by entering the description (max-35 characters in length). If the item is a package, write the quantity and the format of each unit. Example: Juice box 3X155ml.

    • 📷 Figure 2-3 Product Details Window

  • Choose the type of item you want to create for Product Type
  • V - Variable – standard used, covers all items
  • F - Fixed – sell by item, non-inventory based
  • C - Charge – not normally used, cab set-up for charges and deposits
  • O - One time - special item use, or for reducing inventory
    1. I - Inactive – not active, cannot be sold
    2. G - Gift certificate – in store or other defined gift certificate
    3. P - Coupon
    4. The product detail window contains various items properties. Use the various tabs in the Product Details window to enter all the item’s properties (i.e. Stock code, Description, Category, Sale Code, Brand, Available Quantity, Packing, Eligible for Tax Exempt).
    FeatureDescription

    DescriptionCategory/Sub/DetailFamilyAvailabilityEligible for LoyaltyLabelSale CodeShelf LabelScalableBrandGenericCharacteristicFormatUnit of MeasureProduct Discount CodeOrder UnitAvailable QuantityPackingReorder PointReorder MaximumQualified for Tax RebateEligible for Tax RebateEligible for Tax Exempt

    1. While modifying item is simple by double clicking the item you wish you change the details followed by entering appropriate information.
    2. When you finish entering all the item information, click save to save the information.

    📷 Figure 2-4 Price Window

    📷 Figure 2-5 Details Window

    Note: Generally, you should not delete items after they are associated with sales transaction. Deleting items will invalidate data associated with items. You should be fully aware of its consequences.

    To delete an item

    1. Click Inventory > Stock Items > Select the item > Delete.
    2. Select the item you want to remove, and then click Delete. Back Office displays a remainder that deleting the selected item cannot be undone.
    3. To delete the items, Click ‘Yes’. To cancel and return to the stock item window, click ‘No’.

    Copying a Stock Item

    You can add a new product by using Copy button when all the information of the product is identical. Example: Jell-O-raspberry, same vendor, same category, same prices and same tax but with a different description, Jell- O-Strawberry.

    To Copy a Stock Item (s):

    1. 1.On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items.
    2. 2.Select the item that will be copied. Click Copy button.
    3. Enter or scan the item’s UPC and enter a description and make other changes as appropriate.
    4. Click on save button.

    Note: All of the information is copied in the price and detail tab. If the item has the same price and tax you don’t need to click on price or details and make changes.

    Adding an Items to Kit

    A Kit is a set of items grouped under one promotional price. You can modify existing kits or add a new kit to the database. Kit can include many products and when rung at the POS will update the quantity of all products included in a kit. Example: Moring special that includes 12 oz. Coffee, Donut and Newspaper for $2.29. To add an item to Kit:

    1. 1.On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items > Add.
    2. 2.Add a new inventory item (Example: Morning special) and enter a stock number, description, price etc. followed by click Save > Make it a Kit. Once an item is selected as “Make it a Kit”, this enable to popup on Kit menu of Inventory.
    3. 3.Again On the Inventory menu, click Kits > Add; Select the items that you wish to be in Kit.

    📷 Figure 2-6 Item to Kit Window (Step 1)

    📷 Figure 2-7 Item to Kit Window (Step 2)

    Modifying an Item to Inactive

    You can make items inactive rather than deleting them. This preserves sales data associated with the items while excluding them from the item list and reports. Inactive items can be sold, ordered, and will appear in reports. You can also prevent the sale or ordering of inactive items.

    To make an item inactive

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items.
    2. Select the item you want to make inactive, and then click Make Inactive button. Make Inactive button will be turned to Red once product is set to inactive.

    To make an item active

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items.
    2. Select the item you want to make inactive, and then click Make Active button. Make Active button will be will be normal after this step.

    📷 Figure 2-9 Modifying an Item to Inactive Window

    Note:

    Getting Details for an Items

    “Details” buttons enables you to view the inventory details like Purchased Cost, Selling Price, and Quantity Available.

    To get details of an Item

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items > Details button.
    2. It displays Stock Code, Description, Cost, Price and Quantity Available.

    📷 Figure 2-10 Details for an Item Window

    Getting Reports for an Items

    You can use Reports button to generate active reports to display any type of information you need about the inventory items of your store. Once the inventory button is selected, when report button is clicked then it displays Stock Item Report window about the Sales, Purchases, Reports subtabs where you can sort the data per your request. In Reports subtab of Stock Item Report you can compare the Historical Report and do Sales Analysis of product, also by Vender Purchase of product. To get report of an Item

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items > select Stock Code > Report button.
    2. It displays Stock Item Report window > Sales, Purchases, Reports subtabs. Click [Sales] subtab allows you to view on quantity sales per year/monthly basis, revenue in amount dialog box.
    1. Click [Purchase] subtab is similar to Sales subtab of Stock Item Report window.
    2. Click [Reports] subtab allows to view Inventory Stock History Report, Sales Analysis by Product, and Vendor Purchase by Product by range of date’s selection. Click Run Report to view and save the information.

    📷 Figure 2-11 Stock Item Report Window

    Stock Receiving

    You can manage and track your inventory by continuously updating stock receiving option. If you have a big store you might do it by handheld scanners, single store can be done manually. You can receive products related to purchase orders and purchase orders are updated accordingly. Purchase order status is updated as items are received against a purchased order. Stock receiving is discussed in detail below.

    Note: The import from file and the export selected stock options can be programmed to receive or send data to head office (if that option is so available).

    To perform a stock receiving:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Takes button.
    2. Click List Stock dialog box, [Filter by All Stock] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock codes (leave unticked to stock take all items in the store).
    3. Click List Stock dialog box, [Filter by Vendor] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of Vendor (leave unticked to stock take all items in the store).
    4. Click List Stock dialog box, [Filter by Category] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock categories and sub-categories (leave unticked to stock take all items in the store).
    5. Click List Stock dialog box, select the [Filter by Month].
    6. Click the stock item you want to update, followed by entering Quantity, Cost and Invoice. Click ‘Accept’ button to update the information.

    📷 Figure 2-12 Stock Receiving Window

  • Click Delete button of Update Stock dialog box to delete the stock item you wish to change the quantity.
  • Click DeleteAll button of Update Stock dialog box to delete all the stock item you wish to change the quantity.
    1. Click Suspend button of Update Stock dialog box allows you to suspend updating stock receiving temporarily.
    2. Click Batch Report button of Update Stock dialog box allows you to view the stock receiving report that displays stock item Quantity, Unit Cost, Taxes, Charges, Total estimated cost.
    3. Click Process button of Update Stock dialog box allows you to synchronize your database with latest inventory changes.
    4. Click Unsuspend button of Update Stock dialog box allows you to unsuspend updating stock receiving.
    5. Click Export Selected Stocks button allows you to upload all the stock information to the handheld device.
    6. Click Import from File button allows you to import stock receiving file from external handheld device to update the inventory.

    Stock receiving using Handheld Scanner (MC50)

    Handheld device expedite the process of stock receiving option of inventory management. Using a handheld on a regular basis as well as utilizing it for receiving and inventory writing offs will aid in the identification and elimination of shrinkage. In addition, it will speed the flow of product control ensuring accurate product receipt and management. By following a strict guideline for inventory management a store owner/manager is able to see which particular products are missing from the inventory and then apply the appropriate investigation techniques to determine the cause of the discrepancy.

    Note: Before beginning the installation of the inventory control handheld; please contact InfoNet @ 1‐888‐925‐8125 MON – FRI, 9am – 5pm.

    What you need :

    1. Motorola MC50 or MC55 Handheld Computer
    2. Cradle point for the Handheld
    3. Copy of Microsoft Active Sync 4.5
    4. Infonet Back Office Software
    5. Infonet Inventory Control Software
    6. .NET 3.5 Software Upgrade

    Know your inventory :

    In any retail outlet, all products are marked with a barcode or PLU. Our handheld supports 3 types of barcode’s. UPC‐A, the most commonly used barcode consists of 12 digits often starting with a 0 and ends with a check digit. UPC‐E is another common barcode containing 8 digits, typically starting with a 0 and ending with a check digit. The last barcode is an EAN‐13 which contains 13 digits. The scanner also accepts manual entries of PLU codes which range from 4 to 5 digits.

    Before getting started take a look at how your inventory is entered into your system and determine if your products have all of the digits for UPC‐A and UPC‐E, this is important for the proper configuring of the handheld to read these barcodes properly and accurately.

    To perform inventory receiving using handheld:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Receiving button.
    2. On Stock Receiving window, click [Select All] followed by Export Selected Stocks button on top right corner of same window. This process will create a database for you. Copy the database to the handheld device. Using ActiveSync program, you can synchronize the database and load that in the handheld device.
    3. Run the “Handheld” application in the Pocket PC and use the “Inventory Receiving” option. There is an option to create invoice number and choose that invoice number to process the receiving.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Stock Receiving Window

  • You can use the “Scan” option to scan the items or use the “Manual” option to manually enter the items. You can enter the quantity for each item that you have received. System will display the cost of each

    • item and the total cost for all items. Using the “Received Items” option, you can verify all the items that you have received.

    📷 Figure 2-14 Handheld Product Receiving Window

  • Use the “Create Export File” option to upload the information to the back office system. After creating the file, synchronize the device. Use the “Import from File” option in the Stock Receiving screen to download the data. The screen will display the invoice number, stock code, description, cost and total amount. You will have the option to print the receiving report. Click on the “Process” button to complete the receiving.

    • Stock Takes

    A stock take can be performed anytime through the year (or several times if needed). Most stores may choose to do a stock take at end of the year (or start of the year), however you can do a stock take each term / month. You can manage and track your inventory by continually recording changes to increases or decreases in your on-hand quantities. You can perform physical counts in your store, compare the counts to the Back Office computer, and then update the database. If you have a big store you might do it by Categories.

    Note: The import from file and the export selected stock options can be programmed to receive or send data to head office (if that option is so available).

    To perform a stock take:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Takes button.
    2. Click List Stock dialog box, [Filter by All Stock] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock codes (leave unticked to stock take all items in the store).
    3. Click List Stock dialog box, [Filter by Vendor] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of Vendor (leave unticked to stock take all items in the store).
    4. Click List Stock dialog box, [Filter by Category] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock categories and sub-categories (leave unticked to stock take all items in the store).
    5. Click List Stock dialog box, select the [Filter by Month].
    6. Click the stock item you want to update that will move the right pane of same window, followed by entering Count after physical count is made per each stock item. System Count displays individual stock item count available in your back office.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Stock Takes window

    Stock Take using Handheld Scanner (MC50)

    With the C‐Store Commander Inventory control handheld module you do not need to close your store to do an inventory count. The handheld timestamps all counts and will deduct any sales that happen after that product is counted. It is best practice to count your inventory in sections. Always start from bottom left and work across to the right and upward. Most store shelving is 4 to 8 feet long. Always consider these as your section before moving on to another part of the store. If you are using multiple handhelds be sure to only count specific departments on each handheld, as this will prevent double counting. If you do end up counting the same item on more than one device the last device to be synchronized data will over write the first device that was synchronized causing a possible incorrect stock count.

    To perform stock take using handheld:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Stock Take button.
    2. Next, click [Select All] box and wait for all the products to appear on the right hand side of the screen. Once done click on Export Selected Stocks on top right corner to create a text file for ActiveSync in order to transfer stock data to your handheld. Wait for ActiveSync to complete the transfer to the handheld.
    3. On the handheld device press Start button and then go to Inventory Control menu. The inventory control program has four menu options.1. Inventory Count, 2. Product Receiving, 3. Price Check, 4. Encoding Settings.
    4. The first setup is to setup the Encoding Settings to determine if the scanner needs to remove the first or last digit of the barcode in order for it to match the store inventory barcode setup. This allows you to proceed to next step to touch Reload button under inventory count.

    Note: If you press Reload button at any time all inventory counts will be erased from the handheld system, thereby resetting the entire count to the beginning count total.

    1. Once the program is complete then inventory counting can begin. Handheld device has physical buttons on the side of the device in order to scan products as well there is an onscreen software scan control button.
    2. Once an item has been scanned, enter the count into Store Count. Store Count is the quantity you got on hand whereas System Count is nothing but the quantity that is available on your back office database.
    3. Once the counts are entered, touch ‘Done’ button to be able to enter the next product. Note: if a product is not in the store database a message will appear on the screen saying “Product Not Found”. This product will need to be entered into the Price Book before it can be counted. Once entered any missing item can then be counted at the end of the count. To be able to then count missing items the handheld will need to be reloaded after the count in process has been completed. Only do this at the end of the count.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Stock Takes Handheld window

  • For items that have a damaged barcode, you need to look them under Uncounted Items. After selecting, click on Edit Product to enter the count quantity.
    1. When the inventory count is complete, press the Create Export File button and wait for the Message “Create Export File Done Successfully”. Next click on Back and exit out of the program.
    2. Dock the handheld in its cradle/USB cable and wait for ActiveSync to synchronize the data.
    3. Next in the Back Office under Stock Takes Click on the Import from File button. Click Process button to commit the data. Once complete the handheld is ready to be reloaded for the next set of counting.
    4. At any time before the processing the stock take variance report can be run to see if there are any differences between the system count and the inventory count.

    Purchasing and Receiving

    You can manage and track your inventory by continually recording changes to increases or decreases in your on-hand quantities. Here you can specify the ordering frequency (order per week) for each vendor in the vendor definition screen. Use the purchasing and receiving module to automatically create the orders. Order quantity in the PO will be based on the MAX quantity/ordering frequency minus quantity in stock.

    You can add a scheduled task to run an application called “BOInventoryLevel.exe”. This application will create a file with the following details (sales for the last 8 weeks, average sale per day, average sales per week, current MIN and MAX values and new MIN and MAX value). New MIN and MAX value is calculated based on a formula (MIN = stock ratio * sales per week, MAX = MIN value + pack size) and the inventory table will be updated with the new values. You can specify the stock ratio based on department/stock in the policy settings (1.5 for cigarettes and 1.0 for other products etc.).

    To create a purchasing and receiving order:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Purchasing and Receiving button.
    2. Click Status dialog box, [Filter by Open] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock codes.
    3. Click Status dialog box, [Filter by Closed] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock codes.
    4. Click Status dialog box, [Filter by Send] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock codes.
    5. Click Status dialog box, [Filter by Hold] list: you can choose to only perform a stock take on a range of stock codes.
    6. Upon Open selection, click Create button to create a new purchasing and receiving order. Select the vendor from which you are going to be ordering from upon new window popups.
    7. Click Detail button on the bottom to complete the purchase order information. See purchase order information window below that displays. Enter the appropriate information to complete PO.

    📷 Figure 2-10 Purchasing and Receiving window

    📷 Figure 2-10 Purchase Order Information Window

    📷 Figure 2-10 Add Item to the PO Window

  • Click Search Items button if you wish to search an item from selected list.

    • 📷 Figure 2-10 Add Item to the PO Window

    Note: Please note the output path and file name. If you were to open the file you will see a comma delimited file that can be used by the vendor to place an order for you.

    1. Once the order has been received then go back to the main screen. Upon selecting Status > Send > Select the PO > Post button. You can only post once. Once the invoice has been posted the inventory will be updated with the latest Quantities.
    2. When you are posting you will need to enter the Shift ID and Shift Number. Enter the value 1 for both of these. Next choose your Payment Type, Charge or Cash. Then press OK.

    📷 Figure 2-10 Add Item to the PO Window

  • Then you will find the finished sale in the closed section of the purchase and receiving tab. Make sure that the system PO is written on the packing slip /invoice from the vendor so that you can cross reference if necessary.

    • Promotional Items

    You may be getting ready to promote your grand opening or holiday sale, and you want to set up all your sales prices days in advance. When you put items on sale, you should specify the sales start data and end date, as well as the sale price of the items. When customers purchase an item on sale, they pay the sale price unless they have a discount that specifies a lower price. At the end of the sale, the sale price returns to its normal price.

    You can change the price at the point of sale, provided that the cashier has access rights to override the price. To ensure that the cashier does not sell an item below its lowest price, you can define a price range for the item. It allows you to set a special promotion for single product or group of products. The special promotion price will apply when all items are purchased in one single transaction. To set an items on promotion:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Promotional Items > Add button.
    2. Use the various tabs in the Promotional Items window to enter all the item’s properties (i.e. Promotion ID, Description, Promo Items, Amount, Start Date, End Date, Select a day). Select a day will allow you to set the promotional price on particular day of week.

    📷 Figure 2-12 Promotion Item window

  • While selecting Discount:
    • % percentage:Enter the % to be applied when all of the products are purchased together.
    $ Dollar:Enter the $ discount to be applied when all of the products are purchased together
    P Unit price:Enter the price for a group of products. Ex: (Kit 3/$2.19. The price will be divided into

    three)

    B By item:Enter the $ discount given for each products
    1. Click the Stock Code of the items you wish to set on Promotion list; this can be done by scanning the items or entering the stock codes manually followed by Clicking “Save” button.

    Promotion Scenarios

    1. 1.Multi-Link Promotion:

    Here if you buy all three items, then you will get 50% for each item.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Multi-Link Promotion window

    📷 Figure 2-13 2 and 1 package for $7.00 window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Beer and Wine Combo window

    📷 Figure 2-13 2 for $4.00 Sweet Chilly window

    📷 Figure 2-13 2 for $9.00 only Saturdays window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Dollar Discount window

    📷 Figure 2-13 By Item Discount window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Dollar Discount for 2 of each window

    📷 Figure 2-13 any item 10% off window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Fuel Discount for 10 L or more window

    📷 Figure 2-13 P Type 3 Items window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Mix and Max window

    Note: You can set the Combine sale line policy to either “YES” or “NO”. With the previous version, you had to set this policy to “YES”. Figure 2-13 Mix and Match window

  • 1.On the Inventory menu, click Stock Items > Add.
  • 2.Add a new inventory item (Example: Morning special) and enter a stock number, description, price etc. followed by click Save > Make it a Kit. Once an item is selected as “Make it a Kit”, this enable to popup on Kit menu of Inventory.

    • 📷 Figure 2-13 Kit window

  • 3.Again On the Inventory menu, click Kits > Select previously created item > Add; Select the items that you wish to be in Kit.

    • Product Categories

    C-Store Commander Back Office enables you to organize your inventory into departments and categories, which will facilitate cleaner report output. You can modify your departments and categories on one window and see all your data right in front of you.

    When you set multiple categories, remember that category names should be unique so as not to create confusion when assigning products to the categories. A suggestion for having similar category names for same product should be defined by its quantity etc.

    To add a department or category

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Product Categories > New Category.
    2. Enter Code, Catg. Name, Details, Count Detail’s followed by ‘Complete’ button to save the information.
    3. Click Delete Category button if you wish to remove any product category saved in the database.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Product Classification Window

    Price Book Management

    You may have already set up your initial pricing structure. However, as you continually add to your inventory, you should define the item price and cost at the same time you add the item. You can adjust item pricing a couple of ways. If you want to change the pricing of one item, you can find it in the database and manually modify its pricing. The Price Book Management has 3 different options available as follows:

    • Price Change
    • Cost Change
    • Label Printing

    To Perform Price Change:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Price Book Management.
    2. On the top, select [Price Change] > Price Change Selection.
    3. In this Price Change selection tab we can select the Product Categories button or Vendors for which we need to do the price change. If you select Product Categories, all the product information (Dept.\Sub Dept.\ Sub Detail) will be displayed. Once product items are available select the Dept.\Sub Dept.\ Sub Detail. You can select one or more selections from any category. Vendors buttons will allow you to do a

    vendor price change. These options are just to filter and display only products matching with the selected criteria.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Price Change Selection Window

  • Click Price Change View tab to do actual price change programming. In the Price Setup dialog box, it allows you to select various options.

    • a.Regular Price is a price of item when it is sold to the customers for consumption. Sale Pricing is

    the price of a product that is being offered at a discount or promotion.

    b.First Unit Pricing is for product that includes all extra costs incidental to the product. c.Unit Price for Gas Container is 15.00 (Includes regular price, tax, environmental fee etc.). d.Incremental pricing also referred to as marginal cost, is the encompassing change a company

    experience within its balance sheet or income statement due to the product and sale of one additional unit of production. It is calculated by analyzing the additional charges incurred based on the change in a certain activity.

    e.X for Pricing is a price that applies to customer based on the quantity purchased. When more

    number of same product are on sale line then X-Pricing applies to that sale as discount or promotion.

    1. Previous selected items from Price change Selection will be displayed here to apply price change. We can select all products by using < Select/Unselect All> option or can select only the required one from the list by individually clicking on the product’s select column.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Price Change Selection Window

  • If you need to do display all products selecting Show All Products button will overwrite all other previous list. Also there is an option to search a particular product by name or by description. Search button to search specific product which is missing in the list.
  • You have option to select Current price or Future Price (for up to 3 Future Price) from this screen.
  • Price can be changed in the increment\decrement of $ or % or Price or Margin %. You can set a Price for all selected Products. Also you can change the Margin % for the products. Clicking Apply is not changing the price; it will change only the display.
  • In this screen you can remove the selected product from price change by unselecting them by clicking the ‘Sell’ column. Once the price change selection is programmed, the system will display the current cost, new price and margin.
  • Clicking the New Price column for a product will open a new screen with additional information about the price change as follows: (For this selected product Price increased by 10% to the current Price)

    • 📷 Figure 2-13 Price Change Selection Window

  • After changing the price use the ‘Save as Pending’ option to save it temporarily. This means price change is not applied to the inventory. Still they got option to review the price change by running the reports. After review it we can change it or remove all of them or individual products until we Finalize.

    • 📷 Figure 2-13 Price Change List Window

  • Click Price Change list screen, we can delete the all pending price changes (Delete All) or can remove the individual items (by clicking on Products ‘Del’ column). Also report is available to review it before finalizing the change. Once you finished reviewing and ready to apply the changes, click on ‘Finalize’ and that will update Stock price tables with the new information, which is not irreversible ( To reverse we need to do another price change)

    • To Perform Cost Change:

    1. On the Inventory menu, click Price Book Management.
    2. On the top, select [Cost Change] > Cost Change Selection.
    3. Cost change Selection tab is almost similar to the Price Change Selection tab. In this screen we can select the Categories (Dept.\Sub Dept.\ Sub Detail) for which we need to do the cost change. Also we got the option to selector the vendor to do a vendor cost Change. These options are just to filter and display only products matching with the selected criteria.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Cost Change Selection Window

  • This screen is for setting up the cost change for current and future costs. Costs can be set by increment\decrement of $ or % or by Cost. When changing the Cost based on policy setting we can change the price or margin automatically. Also we can change the cost only without changing the price (This is based on Policy settings for each individual business requirement).
  • In this screen also we got option to review the new cost, price and margin for each product. Once finished the change, save the same changes as pending, which will save the information in a temporary table.

    • 📷 Figure 2-13 Cost Change View Window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Cost Change List Window

    To Perform Label Printing:
    1. On the Inventory menu, click Price Book Management.
    2. On the top, select [Label Printing] > Label Printing Selection.
    3. Label Printing Selection tab is almost similar to the Price Change Selection tab. In this screen we can select the Categories (Dept.\Sub Dept.\ Sub Detail) for which we need to do the cost change. Also we got the option to selector the vendor to do a vendor cost Change. These options are just to filter and display only products matching with the selected criteria.

    📷 Figure 2-13 Label Printing Selection Window

  • Label Printing displays previous selected items along with Product ID, Product Name, and Reg. Price. Once you click [Sell] column product information will be displayed. Click [Select/Unselect All] to choose all items displayed. Click Save as Pending button to proceed further.

    • 📷 Figure 2-13 Label Printing View Window

    📷 Figure 2-13 Label Printing List Window

    Verify Stock Files

    Verify Stock Files is used only after importing data from an excel file (during a site installation). This will clean up any unwanted data in the inventory tables. It will also add missing records in required tables.

    Chapter 3

    🔘 Hot Buttons & POS

    CHAPTER 3

    POS Screen Setup

    The POS Screen Setup section provides you information about screen layouts for the C-Store Commander for retail point of sale experience. C-Store Commander for retail point of sale user interface can be configured using a below list of options and screen layouts, assigned to stores, registers and users. It allows better user experience in improving the sale process faster. Topics in this section

    • “Hot Buttons”

    • “Bottle Returns”

    • “Message Buttons”

    • “Cash Draws”

    📷 Figure 3-1 POS Screen Setup menu window

    Hot Buttons

    Hot Buttons prompts you to expedite the sales process apart from selecting the product that does not have barcode to scan into the system. These products are listed as per users request using Back Office Programming. These Hot Buttons contains up to 10 pages of buttons and each page contains up to 36 Hot Buttons. To add an item to the sale line start off by selecting the product you wish to sell. By selecting a product multiple times will

    allow you to add to the quantity of items sold. If you need to remove an item from sale items then you have to remove it from sale list.

    To add a Hot button

    1. On the Back Office menu, click POS Screen Setup > Hot Buttons.
    2. If you wish to change the name of the menu, click Change Page button of Hot Buttons dialog box followed by entering information for Page ID, Page Name. For example in the below image, the ChangePage is set to “Miscellaneous”, Click Complete button to save the information. C-Store Commander allows you to have 10 pages with each page contains up to 36 Hot Buttons.
    3. While setting up Hot Buttons, click the specific button of Hot Button dialog box and select the inventory by Code, Description
    4. Enter FirstLine, Second Line, and Default Quantity followed by Define Button to setup hot buttons. When entering the any information on First Line will be the description of item on your POS.
    5. Changing color for already defined buttons is done by clicking Hot Button > Change Color > Choose Color > Ok button.
    6. Reassigning already defined button done by clicking Hot Button > Undefine Button.

    📷 Figure 3-2 Set Hot Buttons window

  • On the Back Office menu, click POS Screen Setup > Bottle Return Buttons.
  • Choose the button of Bottle Returns Buttons dialog box, enter appropriate information like Product, FirstLine, Second Line, Default Quantity, Price followed by DefineButton to complete the task.
  • Reassigning already defined button done by clicking Bottle Return Button > Undefine Button.

    • Note: This setup will be done prior to a store opening and normally this is a onetime set-up.

    📷 Figure 3-2 Set Bottle Return Buttons window

  • On the Back Office menu, click POS Screen Setup > Message Buttons.
  • Choose the button of Message Buttons dialog box, enter appropriate information like FirstLine, Second Line, Second Line, Message followed by DefineButton to complete the task.
  • Changing color for already defined buttons is done by clicking Message Button > Change Color > Choose Color > Ok button.
  • Reassigning already defined button done by clicking Message Button > Undefine Button.

    • 📷 Figure 3-2 Set Message Buttons window

  • On the Back Office menu, click POS Screen Setup > Cash Draws.
  • Choose the button you wish to change of Cash Loans dialog box, enter Name on Button, Amount Added per Click followed by Update button.
  • In the event when you want to disable the button, choose the cash loans button followed by click ‘Disablebutton’.

    • 📷 Figure 3-2 Cash Draw Setup window

    Chapter 4

    📊 Reports

    CHAPTER 4

    Reports

    The Reports feature allows you to view, export, print, and save all of the reports in Back Office. The reports provide summaries of store activity, performance, and records. Reports can be viewed over a specific time period or added to a schedule. You can generate a number of reports about your customers and day to day business operations that can help you communicate with customers and gather useful information at the point-of-sale. Detailed information will be available in next sections of this chapter. Topics in this chapter are:

    • “Overview”

    • “Crystal Reports”

    • “Text Reports”

    • “Electronic Journal”

    • “Excel Reports”

    • “Till Close Report”

    • “Close Batch Reports”

    • “Cards Reports”

    • “AR Statement”

    • “Group Reports”

    Overview

    From the Reports main tab, you can access summary reports on the status of sales, returns, employees, item performance, and other data collected through the Point-of-Sale. Reports can be viewed and exported. These reports are all available under the Reports main tab. A report can be accessed by choosing the appropriate subtab and left navigation link. Reports can be viewed for hourly, weekly, monthly, or yearly activity. The following table describes the available reports and their features. For further information on the purpose and screens for each report, refer to the Reports Overview document.

    📷 Figure 4-1 Report Menu Tab Window

    Crystal Reports

    Crystal Reports is a business intelligence application used to design and generate reports from a wide range of data sources. Crystal Reports allows users to graphically design data connections and report layout. In the Database Expert, users can select and link tables from a wide variety of data sources, including Microsoft Excel spreadsheets and SQL databases.

    📷 Figure 4-2 Crystal Report Window

    Filters : Report filters are used to break down the data into a more manageable chunk. There are many filters that can be set up on a report. If any report needs a specific filter for your needs it can be added. Filters that can be part of a report are as follows:

    • Date Range • Till # • Shift # • Employee • Time • Department •Sub Department •Sub Detail •Stock Code •Vendor •Customer •Fleet Card Number

    Filters often get mixed up with what is to be displayed on a report. All reports have fixed column names and will display only the data that should be in those columns. The filters are used to narrow down the search to give the exact information that you require. Please have a look through this catalogue and see where the filters can help in your search for the perfect report.

    TermDefinition
    Date RangeThe sales activity by range of date from store open to the current time.
    Till #Physical money tray at a workstation
    Shift #Sales by shift # up to the current date and time
    EmployeeEmployee at a specific workstation
    Time Sales by time up to the current date and time
    DepartmentSales by department up to the current date and time
    Sub DepartmentSales by sub department up to the current date and time
    Sub DetailSales by sub detail up to the current date and time
    Stock CodeSales by stock code up to the current date and time
    VendorSales by vendor up to the current date and time
    CustomerSales by customer up to the current date and time
    Fleet Card NumberSales by fleet card number up to the current date and time

    Functions : Crystal Reports allows a user to print and export data into either Excel or HTML format. To print a report, simply click on the printer icon on the Crystal Report header. To export, click on the envelope with the red down arrow to export to either Excel or HTML format. If you would like to print to PDF, you will need to have a PDF writer application installed on the computer and set it up as a Printer. Crystal also has a find feature to help you look for specific information inside a report. Simply click on the binoculars and type in the word, phrase or number you are looking for.

    Tax Sales Detail

    AC2019 – Tax Sales Detail: This is a summary report and is used to retrieve sale tax information based upon date range. You can also view the sales tax total for the current date, week-to-date, month-to-date, and year- to-date.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Crystal Reports menu, point to All Reports, point to Tax Sales Detail,

    and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-3 Tax Sales Details Filter Window

    📷 Figure 4-4 Tax Sales Details Crystal Report

  • On the Back Office > Reports > Crystal Reports menu, point to All Reports, point to Credit Card

    • Reconsolidation, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    Note: Please note that card numbers are MASKED/ENCRYPTED in this report.

  • On the Back Office > Reports > Crystal Reports menu, point to All Reports, point to Third Party Card,

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Crystal Reports menu, point to All Reports, point to BC Tax Exempt

    Sales - Fuel Crystal, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-131 BC Tax Exempt Sales - Fuel Crystal Report

    Note: report has been abridged to fit this screen: report as printed will match to manual paper report.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Crystal Reports menu, point to All Reports, point to BC Tax Exempt

    Sales - Tobacco, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-157 Text Report Window

  • 1.On the Back Office menu, click Report > Text Report > Choose the list from text report menu.
  • Apply the filters from Select Report Filters dialog box. See Text Report Table for detailed description.
    • TermDescription
    StoreWhere products is passed from the seller to the customer.
    Reading DateDate when reading of point of sales date captured.
    Sale DateDate when sale begins.
    Start Date Date when batch begins
    End DateDate when batch ends
    Start Card #The ways in which your company communicates and deals with existing cards.

    End Card #

    Start Customer #The ways in which your company communicates and deals with existing customers.
    End Customer #The ways in which your company communicates and deals with existing customers
    Start DepartmentDate when sales start by department up to the current date and time
    End DepartmentDate when sales end by department up to the current date and time
    EmployeeEmployee at specific work station
    Terminal IDTerminal Identification Number, also known as a TID or TIN, is a unique number

    assigned and linked to a specific point-of-sale (POS) terminal or workstation that can be used to identify the merchant operating the terminal during credit card sales transaction processing.

    1. Click Run Report button, if you wish to generate report.
    2. Click Print Report button, if you wish to print the generated report.
    3. Click Email button, if you wish to email to a recipient.

    Totalizer Reading Report

    T0001 – Totalizer Reading Report: This report is made to be printed out on a receipt printer but can also be printed out on the back office printer as well. The report shows each pump and all of its positions. This data comes from the pumps themselves and cannot be manipulated by the system. A totalizer report can be taken at any point in the day as long as no one is pumping. Typically this will be done as part of the End of Day process.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Text Reports menu, point to Total Reading Report, apply the report

    filters, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-158 Totalizer Reading Text Report

  • On the Back Office > Reports > Text Reports menu, point to Card Detail Report, apply the report filters,

    • and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-159 Card Detail Report Text Report

  • On the Back Office > Reports > Text Reports menu, point to FleetCard Fuel Report, apply the report

    • filters, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-160 Fleet Card Fuel Report

    Credit Card Summary Report

    T0004 – Credit Card Summary Report: Gives a summary of all credit and debit cards transaction totals for each till and by each card type.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Text Reports menu, point to Credit Card Summary Report, apply the

    report filters, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-161 Credit Card Summary Report

    • report filters, and then click the Run Report > Save.

    Note: By default, electronic journal uses the built in function Sum to aggregate values that belong to the same group into an individual data point in the series.

    📷 Figure 4-175 Electronic Journal Search Wizard Window

  • 1.On the Back Office menu, click Report > Electronic Journal > Select specified list.
  • 2.The Left side of the above screen contains the search groups. When you select a group, the corresponding report filters (parameters) will be displayed in the right. By default, start and end date will be available for each group. Other filters are configurable by the user. Select one of list of options. Example: Date, TransactionType, Stock Item, Group, Client, Operator, Gas Pump, Loyalty. If you have selected “Transaction Type” (or Function) as the group, you will get the following filters:
    • Transaction TypeSearch by transaction type. Ex: Sale, Void, Refund, Payout etc.
    Invoice NumberSearch by Invoice Number. Ex: AX123YZ
    UserSearch by user, user code. Ex: Manager, Administrator, Cashier etc.
    ShiftSearch by shift. Ex: Morning, Afternoon, Evening.
    TillSearch by till. Ex: #1, #2 etc.
    CustomerSearch by customer name. Ex: AR Customer, Loyalty Customer.
    AmountSearch by amount. Ex: $20, $50, $100
    1. Apply filters by Start Date and End Date. The filters may change depending upon your selection.
    2. In expression, select the expression for the field to filter. AND expression allows you to perform a logical conjunction on two expressions. AND expression returns a value of true if both its operands are true, and false otherwise. OR expression returns a value of true if either (or both) of its operands is true.
    SymbolMnemonicDefinitionExample
    <LtLess thannum<8
    <=LeLess than or equal tosales<=100
    >GtGreater thannum<5
    >=GeGreater than or equal tosales>=300
    =EqEqual toA = 3
    !=NeNot equal to.A != 3
    1. After entering all the necessary values, use the “Search” option to get the results. For any text-based filters, you can use the wild cards (*? etc.) for search.
    2. The electronic journal will be displayed in a new window. In the left side, you will see the search results in a grid. You can click on the result to get the detailed journal report on the right side.
    3. Click on the “Summary” button, to get a summary report based on the entire search results. Click on the “Back” button to go back to the Electronic Journal screen. Use the Print option to print the report.

    Note: User will have an option to add additional filters in the search wizard screen. Maximum of 10 filters are allowed. By default, you will get the filters that are configured in the database. Click on the “Add” button to

    add additional filters. This will be useful to search the records based on a range (Starting and ending Invoice number etc.).

    📷 Figure 4-175 Electronic Journal Search Wizard Window

    Note: A new look up option is available in the Electronic Journal screen to narrow down the search. You will get the option to select any one of the filters defined for the corresponding group in the look up screen. After entering the search criteria, you can click on the search button to get the records based on your selection.

    Note: This report is a summary of all the records displayed on the screen based on your selection. This will have the breakdown of sales by department, totals and tenders.

    Excel Reports

    You can export a list reports as an excel spreadsheet by clicking any list of options available under reports dialog box of excel window followed by selecting the criteria by applying filter Start Date, Department > Generate Report.

    Fuel Litres Summary Report

    See a full summary of your fuel purchased. View fuel purchase data by fuel grade for the current date, week- to-date, month-to-date, and/or year-to-date. This report is available for Tax Exempt/First Nation sales; segregates exempt and non-exempt sales sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Fuel Litres Summary Report, apply filters

    Start Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Fuel Dollars Summary

    See a quick summary of your daily fuel sales in dollars. View sales data by sales rep, cashier, grade for the current date, week-to-date, month-to-date, and/or year-to-date. This report is available for Tax Exempt/First Nation sales; segregates exempt and non-exempt sales sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Fuel Dollars Summary, apply filters Start

    Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Department Sales Summary

    If you organized your items into departments and categories, you can generate the Department Sales Report to view the total sales, cost, and profit margin information for each department. This report is available for Tax Exempt/First Nation sales; segregates exempt and non-exempt sales sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Department Sales Summary, apply filters

    Start Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Sales Summary Family Group

    See a quick summary of your daily sales. View sales data by sales rep, cashier, supplier, register, category, and department for the current date, week-to-date, month-to-date, and/or year-to-date. This report is available for Tax Exempt/First Nation sales; segregates exempt and non-exempt sales sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Sales Summary Family Group, apply

    filters Start Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Fuel Daily Sales Report

    See a quick summary of your fuel sales. View sales data by sales rep, cashier, supplier, register, category, and department for the current date, week-to-date, month-to-date, and/or year-to-date. This report is available for Tax Exempt/First Nation sales; segregates exempt and non-exempt sales sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Fuel Daily Sales Report, apply filters

    Start Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Sales Detail Family Group

    See a quick summary of sales summary of each product family. View sales data by product family name, Quantity, Dollar, Cost and Quantity of non-status customers, members, non-members and total sales of each category by current date to reference date, week-to-date, month-to-date, and/or year-to-date. This report is available for Tax Exempt/First Nation sales; segregates exempt and non-exempt sales sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Sales Detail Family Group, apply filters

    Start Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-175 Sales Detail Family Group Report

    Sales Summary Fuel Products

    This report is available for QITE - Quebec Indian Tax Exemption specific report sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Sales Summary Fuel Product, apply

    filters Start Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Register

    See all your pertinent register information. The report displays everything from register, sales information and item information to quantity and price levels. This report is available for QITE - Quebec Indian Tax Exemption specific report sites.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Register, apply filters Start Date,

    Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Fleet Card Report.

    See all pertinent information of your fleet card customer information. This report displays your customer fleet card number, credit slip #, transaction date, litres, dollar amount of fuel, type of fuel purchase, discount amount, and total amount owing after discount.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Fleet Card Report, apply filters Start

    Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    Sales by Hour Report

    See a quick summary of your hourly sales. View sales data by current date to reference date, week-to-date, month-to-date, and/or year-to-date.

    • On the Back Office > Reports > Excel Reports menu, point to Sales by Hour Report, apply filters Start

    Date, Department, and then click the Generate Report > Save.

    📷 Figure 4-175 Sales by Hour Report

    📷 Figure 4-26 Till Closes Report

    Close Batch Report
    📷 Figure 4-26 Close Batch Report

    Card Report

    You can see all pertinent sales information charged on credit/debit etc. card using card report menu. This report displays everything from date and sale number to amount purchased.

    To generate a card report

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Card Report.
    2. Use Enter Card Number option when you would like to search pertinent card number of your customer.
    3. Start Date allows you to select the start date range you wish you to generate the card report.
    4. End Date allows you to select the end date range you wish you to generate the card report.
    5. Click Generate Report button when you would like to generate after entering necessary information.
    6. If printer is connected, click Print Report button to print generated report.

    📷 Figure 4-180 Card Report Window

    Note: Should be generated at the end of every month. The statement is the companies invoice to the customer. This report can be combined with text report T0005.

    1. On the Back Office > Reports > AR Statement menu, choose dropdown list of Select a Month First dialog box, and then click the Print Statement when you want to print the pertinent statement.
    2. Click Print All Statement button, when you would like to print all the AR Statements of all your AR Customers.
    3. Once Print All Statement is clicked, Send to Printer button will be enable you to get print.
    4. Click Monthly Closing button,
    5. Click Monthly Closing with Statement Period button,

    📷 Figure 4-25 AR Statement Window

    📷 Figure 4-25 Daily Sales Report

  • Automatically populates daily sales and tender information
  • Customizable product sales details
  • Tracks summary sales and tenders
  • Calculates and displays required daily bank deposit
    • Fuel sales details
    • Fuel Inventory summary
    • Detailed entry of vacuum, car wash and other fuel sales

    Benefits:

    • Reduced day end cash out time requirement
    • Accurate bank deposit calculation
    • Eliminates human error
    • Reports fuel variance/shortage
    • Track and compare sales details of non‐integrated product sales: car wash, vacuum, propane, natural

    gas etc.

    📷 Figure 4-25 Carwash Entry Screen You can track all car wash sales using the car wash entry screen, by to entering the closing car wash system supplied water meter readings and entry meter changes.

    📷 Figure: 4-25 Vacuum Sales Entry Screen As with car wash sales, you can enter meter data from your vacuum system readings.

    📷 Figure: Natural Gas Sales

    Group Reports

    You can customize to enhance reporting capabilities with more fields in group report. For example if you would like to add Kit Number variable in your inventory reports, you can modify the report by adding it to group report followed by assigning the variable to pertinent report.

    To add a new report group

    1. On the Back Office > Reports > Group Report menu.
    2. Click New Report Group, enter Group Name, Position of variable.
    3. Click ‘Complete’ button to save the information.
    4. Once the group is created,

    📷 Figure 4-25 Group Report

    Chapter 5

    💳 Fleet Card Mgmt

    CHAPTER 5

    Data Maintenance

    Data Maintenance provide communication and activity between Point-of-Sale and Back Office and allows you to perform daily functions associated with store opening and closing and register and till maintenance. The operations performed in Back Office affect the status of the store as well as the registers and tills at Point-of- Sale. Below is the list of options displayed and discussed in details.

    📷 Figure 5-1 Data Maintenance Menu Tab window

    Note: This setup/configuration must be done prior to the store opening or using the POS. Do not modify these without first checking with your Supervisor/Manager.

    To add a new tender type

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Tender.
    2. Click Add;
    3. On Tender window, type a code, class, short name, exchange rate, full name and sequence.
    4. In the Display order box, you can specify the order in which tender types appear at the register.
    5. Click the Complete button below after you enter the applicable information.

    📷 Figure 5-2 Tender window

  • On the Back Office menu, click Tender.
  • In the Tender Types dialog box, select a tender type, and then click Edit Card Definition complete changes in the properties of the tender type. Click Edit button to change tender type details. Click ResetSequence button to change the sequence of tender type on point of sale.
  • Click Save button finish the task.

    • To delete a tender type

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Tender Types.
    2. In the Tender Types dialog box, select the tender type you want to remove, and then click Delete. Back Office asks to confirm the deletion.

    Masking credit card numbers

    For security, Edit Card Information > Check Digit Mask window allows you to masks credit cards numbers in the database and on all receipts, as in the example below.

    Example: Original credit card number 1111 2222 3333 4444

    Masked credit card number XXXX XXXX XXXX 4444

    Fleet Card Management

    Fleet Card Management prompts you to track fuel purchases off or your customers and helps control, monitor and analyse fuel expenses. Simply fleet management is the management of a transportation fleet. Fleet management includes commercial motor vehicles such as cars, vans, trucks. Fleet management is a function which allows to remove or minimize the risks associated with vehicle investment, improving efficiency, productivity and reducing their overall transportation and staff cost. This option is used to link certain items or departments to a specific card. This will ensure that only approved products are able to be charged to a particular card.

    To add a fleet card

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Fleet Card Management.
    2. Click Add;
    3. Enter the appropriate information like Bank Card ID, Card Production Code, Description, and Usage.
    4. Once card been entered into the database then card needs to link to product category for purchase. Click Card Product Link button on right side of window that opens new Card Product Link window there select Bank Card ID, Card Product Code, Stock Code, Name, Department, Sub Department, Sub Details.
    5. Click ‘Complete’ button to save the information.

    📷 Figure 5-4 Fleet Card Management window

  • On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Fleet Card Management.
  • Click Delete button, then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous menu.

    • Creating a Fleet Card Track Data Private Fleet cards consist of track 2 data. Your bin range should start in a range that is not occupied by major card companies. The fuel bin range is (7****) It is recommended that you pick 5 unique digits to start your bin range. For example: 78123. Next you will have to choose how long of an account number you will require for future use. For example if you use 2 digit account number you will have 100 unique card numbers. If you use 3 you will have 1000 unique card numbers. If you pick 8 digits you will have 100 Million unique card numbers. This may sound like overkill, but when you print your cards and put expiry dates on them, every 3 to 5 years you will

    need to roll your cards over, plus hand out replacement cards. Please read this document carefully, as failure to do so will result in cards not working.

    Track 2

    The Track 2 structure is specified as:

    • STX Start sentinel ";"
    • PANPrimary Account Number, up to 19 digits, as defined in ISO/IEC 7812-1
    • FS Separator "="
    • ED Expiration date, YYMM or "=" if not present
    • SC Service code, 3 digits or "=" if not present
    • DD Discretionary data, balance of available digits
    • ETX End sentinel "?"
    • LRC Longitudinal redundancy check, calculated according to ISO/IEC 7811-2

    The maximum record length is 40 numeric digits.

    Example:

    ; 7812300012345678=1508=1234567890123456?=

    STX PAN FS ED SC DD ETX

    The account number including bin range on average is approx. 16 digits to a maximum of 19. Typically the filler uses ‘0’ as the default digit. The Discretionary data is numbers you can use to fill up the total length of 40 digits. Please note that track 2 supports only numeric characters and not alpha. If you want Alpha you will need to add track 1 data.

    For recommendations of available card printers, we normally have our customer source out to Dolphin Printing.

    http://www.dolphinprinting.com/ refer the track data as ISO/IEC 7812-1 standard. It is also notable that you

    should have the card number printed completely on the card itself.

    Next make sure you know how you want to manage your cards before you start handing them out. If you would like to use a positive card file, meaning you must activated the card prior to issuing, this prevents a person from making a card using the first 5 digits of the bin range from making a valid transaction. The other is a negative card file. This will allow any card in the bin range to get approval and you only manage the cards that are over limit or stolen or has problems with the account. The most secure way is the positive card file. If you have any questions please contact Lee Barter at 1-888-925-8125 Ext 235.

    Here are the most common Fleet Card bin ranges:

    CompaniesBin Ranges

    ARIBin Range(s)Bin Range(s)

    7080200 - 70802097825330 - 7825339 6080000 - 6080009

    ARI RCMP: www.arifleet.ca/6015480 - 6015489
    Corp-Rate: www.corp-rate.ca6005020 - 60050296005040 - 6005049

    GE Capital: www.gecapital.ca/7078590 - 70785997085450 - 7085459

    PHH: www.elementfleet.com7440030 - 740039

    TVL (Triathlon): www.tvl.com70779010-70779019

    JPL: www.jimpeplinski.ca/7080160 - 7080169

    Telus: www.telus.ca/8912580 - 8912589
    Bell Canada: www.bell.ca/8917890 - 8917899
    Jim Pattison Lease: www.jimpattisonlease.com6008370 - 60083796008770 - 6008779
    Wilsons Fuel: wilsons.ca/789723 - 789723

    Francis Fuel: www.francisfuels.com60333 - 60333
    Wex: www.wexinc.com690046 - 690046707138 - 707138

    Card Restriction Profile

    Card Restriction Profile allows you to setup the profile with the required restrictions. Instead of setting up same restrictions for individual cards, define a profile and link that profile to different card. Once the profile is linked to a customer card all the rules for that profile will be applied to the customer card during the transaction process. To make any changes to restriction, you need to change that once in the card restriction profile only. The change will be effective for all the customer cards that are linked to the profile.

    Note: card restriction profile is not implemented for pay @ pump sales.

    Steps for using Card Restriction profile setup:

    • Define different Card Restriction Profiles.
    • Link Product Restrictions to the Profile
    • Define Customer
    • Define Customer Cards for the Customer
    • In Customer card setup screen link the profile to the customer card.

    Notes:

    • Product restriction can be linked to the profile using department/subdepartment/sub-detail/product

    level configuration.

    • Products specified in the product restriction screen is allowed or denied depends on the restriction setup

    in the profile.

    • Prompts can be selected from a list including Driver ID, Odometer, Vehicle #, User ID etc.….
    • You can specify the prompts as required by adding the min length in the table CSCMASTER.

    CardFuelprompts. (We don’t have any entry screen) ‘Dispalytext’ is the caption showing in the receipt. Following are the default prompts available.

    To add a card restriction profile to the database:

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Card Restriction Profile > Add.
    2. Enter Profile ID; Profile ID is annotation of Profile Name. Enter Profile Name; Profile Name must be the user who operates C-Store Commander POS system. Example: Cashier, Manager.
    3. Enter Single Transaction in dollar amount; if your account is verified, there is no limit on the total amount of money you can send from your account. You can send up to $10,000 in a single transaction. (This amount can vary though).
    4. Enter Daily Transaction in dollar amount;
    5. Enter Monthly Transaction in dollar amount;
    1. Enter Transactions per day.

    📷 Figure 5-6 Card Restriction Profile Window

  • Check Ask for PO #?, If you would like to
  • Check Prompt for Fuel?, If you would like to
  • In the Restriction Setup, check ‘Deny Product’ if you would the user not sell the products, check ‘Allow Products’.
  • Click Add Prompt button to enter Sequence and Prompt Message;
  • Click ‘Complete’ button to save the information.
  • Click Delete button to remove the card profile from your database. Edit button will allows you to update card profile information at a later date. Find button allows you to search from list of card profiles. Next button will prompt you to see the next card profile setup in your database. Previous button will allows to return to previous card profile page. Copy button prompts you to copy the information from desired profile to create a new one with same settings.
  • Upon clicking Product Restriction button displays another window where you can restrict the user to buy certain products from the store. This can be done by click Card Product Restriction > Add button. Select Dept., Sub Department, Sub Detail, Stock Code, Description.

    • 📷 Figure 5-7 Optional Fleet Card Data Profiles window

    Transactions Payment Flow for cards linked to Profile ID

    When swiping the private at the tender screen system will check the following card (if policy is yes and card type is ‘F’ and Card is linked to a valid profile id):

    1. If Ask for PO _ popup a screen to enter the PO number
    2. If need to Limit Time of Sale then, system will check the transaction day & time and if not within a valid

    time slot popup a message and won’t allow the card to be used for that transaction.

    • If pass the time limit, will check the product restriction settings for the same profile. Based on the setting

    allow or deny products system will identify the products can be charged on the same card. If there is no products are qualified to be charged on the card system will show a message and decline the processing of card process request. {Allow products- Only allow the products specified in the restriction screen; Deny products – Allow all other products in the system except specified products. For a card if you don’t need any product restriction profile setting should be ‘Deny products’ and do not enter any products in the restriction screen}.

    • If card pass the product restriction, now need to check the daily and monthly and single transaction limit

    also need to check the number of transactions per day.

    1. Check single transaction limit. If valid purchase amount is more than the limit adjust the amount only

    up to the single transaction limit Rest of the amount should be charged to another mode of payment. (zero means no single transaction limit);

    1. Check daily transaction count limit. If total number of transactions (if there is a refund it will balance

    out a sale i.e. Total # of transactions per day per card = Sale Count per card – Return count per card) reach the daily transactions count, system will show a message and the same card purchase will be declined.

    1. Check daily transaction amount limit. If total purchase amount using this card on the day (Total amount

    per day = Daily purchase amount – Daily refund amount) reach the daily transaction amount limit, system will show a message and the same card purchase will be declined.

    1. Check daily transaction amount limit. If total purchase amount using this card for the month (Total

    amount per month = Monthly purchases – Monthly refunds) reach the monthly transaction amount limit, system will show a message and the same card purchase will be declined.

    1. If successfully pass all the above restrictions, if fuels prompts are linked to the profile, system will

    pop up the prompts in the same order as the sequence setup in the profile screen. If the prompt is required, cancelling the prompts without entering the value will cancel the card transactions.

    1. If successfully passed all the above steps, system will charge the valid purchase amount to the card

    \ account

    Note: Card profile restriction will have the priority than tender product restriction, if any.

    Optional Data Profile

    Optional data profile prompts you to use the fleet card profile screen. Users have the option the select the data fields (pre-configured) from the dropdown list and specify the starting and ending position. Length specifies the total length of the field including decimals. Set the number of decimals in the “Decimals” field. “Alignment” can be configured as Right justified or Left justified.

    “Format” is an optional attribute for fields like date and time. Padded specifies if the field is padded or not, CharPad specifies the character used for padding (0 or space). Total length of optional data can be set up by a policy (maximum length of optional data - default value is 80).

    Product Code Setup

    Product code is normally defined as a 2 digit code. You can define a 2 digit code for each stock code. You can also configure one specific product code for a group of products (By Department, Sub-Department etc.). Use the

    “Product Link” option in the “Fleet Card Profile” screen to define a product code based on the profile ID and stock code.

    📷 Figure 5-8 Optional Fleet Card Data Profiles window

    📷 Figure 5-10 Optional Fleet Card Data Profiles window

    Negative/Positive Cards Input

    This option is used to validate a customer’s fleet card for having sufficient credit to complete a purchase (a positive card). This feature could also be used to cash a customer’s cheque or to identify a customer. When the cashier enters the card # the system looks in the database and validates if the card is valid (Positive) or not valid (Negative). Positive cards are a list of valid cards. You can add new positive cards by selecting the button ‘New Positive Card’. It will open a screen where we can enter the card number and also the status of the card.

    To add a negative/positive card input to the database

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Negative / Positive Cards Input. You will see the following screen with the options to setup Negative cards and positive cards;

    📷 Figure 5-11 Negative/Positive Card Input/output Window

    Ryymmdd.NCF(Replace entire data)-,* (This will delete all the records from the negative cards table)

    +, 3081380067621849, 1 (+ means add, 3081380067621849 is the card number, 1 is the message code)

    +, 3081380067661191, 1

    +, 3081380068109430, 1

    Uyymmdd.NCF (Update data)

    -, 6005040053158515 (Remove card number 6005040053158515)
