    +, 6005040053334314, 1 (Add card number 6005040053334314 with message code 1)

    -, 6005040053358651

    NCLatest.txt (Another format to load data)

    “6005040053334314”, A (Add card number 6005040053334314)

    “6005040053358651”, D (Delete card number 6005040053358651)

    Similarly use the “Read Positive Card” button to load the positive cards from the file. File for positive cards should be in the “Uyymmdd.NCF’” or “Ryymmdd.NCF” format. There is also a stand-alone program available, which can be scheduled to run at a specific time to load the negative or positive card files automatically.

    Customer Group

    You can define fuel discounts by setting up specific customer groups and then in turn link the customer group to a customer/loyalty account numbers. In the situation when a cashier manually selects the customer or swipes the customer’s loyalty card, the customer will receive a fuel discount based upon that particular customer group that the customer is a member of. When utilizing a customer/loyalty card, that card (or group of cards id a parent account is being utilized) is linked to that particular customer. This allows a retailer to define different customer groups for different types and amounts of fuel discounts:

    % Percentage - Use the % discount type to specify the discount by percentage of total $ fuel purchase amount, which immediately comes of the fuel purchase.

    $ dollar - Use the $ discount type to specify the discount by cents off on a per litre basis.

    Note: For transactions at the pump the discount will be applied on the receipt only. Due to the patents owned by Excentus Corporation we are not able to drop the price at the pump.

    Coupon -This option prints a coupon at the bottom of the receipt with a $ dollar/cents value. Coupon can then be redeemed for in‐store purchases. The coupon contains a valid coupon number and the $ amount. Customer can then use the coupon to purchase in‐store items. In addition C‐Store Commander validates the coupon number at the time of redemption to ensure that it is a valid coupon.

    Note: Coupon discount is available for inside fuel sales only.

    Cash Bonus - Cash Bonus allows a retailer to award Loyalty Cash Bonus Coupons (equivalent to Cash) to customers for Fuel Sales. (Example: CO‐OP Cash, Canadian Tire Money). Cash Bonus Coupons are based upon total litres sold in a transaction. The amount of Cash Bonus awarded is configurable by way of using a Cash Bonus Chart. Different thresholds can be defined in the Cash Bonus Chart.

    The following is an example Cash Bonus Chart used to define Cash Bonus thresholds for different fuel transaction volume levels. You can create different Cash Bonus customer groups with different cash bonus awards.

    Discount Chart -This option allows for the definition of different customer groups with different levels of fuel discounting based upon fuel grades. This will give you the ability to define multilevel discounting for customers depending upon sales volume and grade purchased.

    You also have the option to specify the loyalty footer, which prints on the receipt.

    Alert: Be careful with customer groups and members of those groups as this ability to give fuel discounts is both

    very powerful and can also be very expensive with today’s low fuel margins.

    Note: You can use customer groups to drive more sales from your c-store by offering a store coupon versus a direct $ discount.

    To add a customer group to the database

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Customers.
    2. Click Add.
    3. Use the tabs on the Customer window dialog box to enter the customer information you want.
    4. Once customers Basic Customer Data, Address, Phone & Fax, Loyalty Program, AR Information, Customer Group, Fuel Rebate is entered.
    5. Click “Complete” button to save the information in the database.

    📷 Figure 5-12 Customer Group window

  • Setting up new customer accounts

    • Setting up New Customer Accounts

    You can view, create, and maintain a detailed and up-to-date customer list. Many retailers find this information essential for contacting customers and for gathering useful information at the point-of-sale.

    📷 Figure 5-13 Customer window

    Note: Be careful not to lose all past related information for an AR/charge account when doing modifications to an existing customer account. We advise that you print all customer information prior to making any changes.

    To add a customer to the database

    1. On the Back Office menu, click Data Maintenance > Customers.
    2. Click Add.
    3. Use the tabs on the Customer window dialog box to enter the customer information you want.
    4. Once customers Basic Customer Data, Address, Phone & Fax, Loyalty Program, AR Information, Customer Group, Fuel Rebate is entered.
    5. Click “Complete” button to save the information in the database.

    📷 Figure 5-14 Customer Card Window

    FeatureDescriptionCompleteIt allows to save the information satisfactorily. CancelIt allows to cancel the information when wrongly entered. EditIt allows to makes changes to the customer card information. SearchIt allows to find the customer from the database. NextIt allows to look up for next customer from the database. Customer CardIt allows to change the definition of customer cards for the customer. Next you then

    link the card restriction profile to that cards. See image 5-1a customer card window.

    Discount Table

    Use Discount Table option to setup and maintain discount table for both customers and products. Please note this is only available if the required Policy Settings are set to allow for discounts.

    Note: This one option is one of your most powerful tools for both attracting and retaining customers.

    Alert: Be careful that you only offer discounts on products, customers and employees that you want to offer

    discounts on and to.

    To setup a discount table

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Discount Table tab.
    2. Enter appropriate rates, please note that all products have a default product discount code of 0 (zero), therefore by setting a discount code of 10% where a customer has been assigned a discount code of 1 will receive a 10% discount on all products with a 0 discount code (unless a product has had the Discount Policy Setting for discounts set at NO).

    📷 Figure 5-15 Discount Table Window

    Alert: This information will be done on the initial configuration of the system. You should NOT change any

    settings in this section as this can cause the need for a new security key to be issued, which will in turn

    make the program in-operable.

    There are various buttons available in Customer window. Table below is list of each button and its function.

    Feature NameDescription
    Company NameThe name of your store. If the customer is representing a chain of stores then it will

    be assigned with appropriate information provided in the site information document. The name can be up to fifty characters long with no special cases. Please be aware that this information will be printed on the receipt of sale.

    AddressYour store's street address. Use the second line for more information.
    CityThe city in which your store is located.
    ProvinceThe province in which your store is located.
    Postal CodeThe postal code for your store.
    CountryThe country where your store is located.
    Store CodeThe store code of your store
    Agent NumberThe agent number of your store.
    Reg. NameThen register name of your store.
    Reg. NumberThe register number of your store.
    Phone NumberThe main phone number for your store.
    Fax NumberThe fax number for your store.
    Security KeyThe security key for your store.
    Initial DateThe date when software gets registered.
    FeaturesUse this button to make changes in the back office features.
    Edit [Change Info]Use this button to make appropriate changes to company information.
    Save [Change Info]Use this button after you make necessary company information.
    Save to FileUse this button to update Datepath.ini file.
    PrintUse this button to print the company page.
    Edit [Change Info]Use this button once security keys are updated.
    Save [Change Info]Use this button after edit button is selected to register company information

    successfully.

    📷 Figure 5-16 Company window

    Tills

    A register must be open before a till can be opened. Tills may be opened in Back Office or Point-of-Sale. This option is used to add or delete tills.

    Note: This will be normally only done during the initial configuration of the system and should NOT be changed without first talking to your Supervisor/Manager or InfoNet Technology Support @ 1-888-431-7944. In the event when new pinpad terminal or new printer is connected to point of sale computer then store manager must contact InfoNet Technology Support to enable appropriate settings to be functional.

    To open a till in Back Office:

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Tills tab.
    2. Click the Till tab. The Till Definition window is displayed.

    📷 Figure 5-17 till window

  • Click Add button to enter the till details by double click the Till you wish to change the details like Till, Active, Processing, Float, Cash, OpenDate, OpenTime, ShiftNumber, ShiftDate, User On, POS ID.
  • Click Delete button if you would like to remove any till which is not existing anymore.
  • Click Flip Active button of Change Till Status dialog box will allow you to change the Till Definitions Active settings to “Yes”.
  • Click Flip Processing button of Change Till Status dialog box will allow you to change the Till Definition Processing settings to “Yes”.
  • Click Add button of Training Mode (91-99) dialog box will allow youto add new user to till in training mode. In the Training mode, the user can process any transaction except fuel.

    • Note: Please note the transaction made during training mode will not be saved in the database.

    1. Click Add button of Pay at the Pump (Till 100) dialog box will allow you to add pay at the pump till.

    Registers

    A register must be opened before a till can be assigned to it. Registers may be opened at any time during the day. Register is small set data holding places that are part of the computer processors. Registers hold an information of point of sale accessories such as Report Printer, Scanner, and Custom Display etc.

    Alert: Register settings will be done during the initial configuration of the system by qualified implementation

    specialist from InfoNet and should not be changed without consulting to your Supervisor/Manager.

    To add a register settings:

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Registers tab.
    2. Click the Register > Add button to create a new register settings. Here is how Register Settings window looks like;

    📷 Figure 5-19 Registers Settings window

    Store Shifts

    Store Shifts is a period of time during which transactions take place in your store. You use register shifts to balance your cash drawer on a regular basis and hold staff accountable for the money they’ve handled.

    📷 Figure 5-20 Store Shifts window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Store Shifts tab.
  • Click the Store Shifts > Add a Shift button to setup a new shifts followed by setting up a Set or Change Start Time. If your store opens 24 hours then most probably our clients prefers to have three shift in a day as see in the below image.
  • Click Delete Shift button if you wish to delete the store shift.
  • Check Shift Active button if you wish to make the store shift active, uncheck if you not;

    • Users

    The users tab allows you to manage employee information. You can add new and temporary users, user group, and edit user’s security roles and changing user passwords. You can also add permanent and temporary employees to the database.

    To add a new user:

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Users tab.
    2. Click New User button to enter User Code, User Name, Password, Date of Birth, User ID, Type of Employee details followed by complete button.
    3. Once the User is added to User’s dialog box, then it will allow you to add a group. Creating and Managing a group purely depends on stores type of business operation. Most of the time customers prefers to have Cashier, Manager, System Administrator, Owners group for ease of business.
    4. Remove User button allows you to delete the user from the database.
    5. New Group button allows you to create a new user group.
    6. Remove group allows you to delete the user group from the database.
    7. On the top of User & User Groups window, Change Users Password button allows you to update your user password settings.

    📷 Figure 5-21 User Password Maintenance window

    📷 Figure 5-22 User and User Groups window

    Commission

    You can set up a flexible commission schedule based on a sales representative or on an item sold, if your store’s sales staff work on commission. You can disallow commission for this item. You can also compute the commission based on the schedule assigned to the sales person or the schedule specified for this particular item.

    To setup a Commission

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Commission tab.
    2. Click Add button, list of options for Employees, Department, Sub-Department, and Sub Details, StockCode followed by percentage of Commission or Dollar value of Commission.
    1. Click complete button to save the information on employee / product commission setup window to apply its settings to employee.

    📷 Figure 5-23 Employee / Product Commission Setup window

    Statutory Holidays

    You can list all the provincial, national and territorial holidays that displays holiday date and its description.

    To add a holiday:

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Statutory Holiday tab.
    2. Click Add button and enter Holiday date and its description followed by complete button to save the information in the database.
    3. Choose appropriate buttons displayed below per your convenience to modify or remove the statutory holiday list from the database.

    📷 Figure 5-24 Statutory Holidays window

    Vendors

    It is very important to keep your vendors contact information up to date to help ensure that your purchase orders are accurate and arrive at the right place at the right time.

    To add a vendor

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Vendors tab.
    2. Click Add button, enter information about the vendor. A name and code are required.
    3. Once the vendor information is entered then click “Complete” button to save the information. If you wish not to save the information click Cancel button.

    To modify a vendor

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Vendors tab.
    2. Click Edit button, enter information about the vendor you wish to change. Please ensure to enter sufficient information for better reporting purpose.
    3. Once the vendor information is entered then click “Complete” button to save the information. If you wish not to save the information click Cancel button.

    To delete a vendor

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Vendors tab.
    2. Click Delete button, then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous menu.

    To search for a vendor

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Vendors tab.
    2. Click Search button, a new search window opens to search vendor details by code, name and phone number.

    📷 Figure 5-25 Vendors window

    Note: Coupon discount is available for inside fuel sales only.

    To add a coupon

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Coupons tab.
    2. Click Add button.
    3. In new coupon dialog box, enter the Code, Description of the Coupon. Choose Tender options. Tick if default coupon for this Tender (Y/N)?
    4. Enter the Serial Number length.
    5. Enter the Value in dollars.
    6. Choose Vender, Department, Sub-Department, Sub-Detail, Stock Code menu.
    7. To save the coupon information, click ‘Complete’ button.

    📷 Figure 5-26 Coupons window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Coupons tab.
  • Click delete button.
  • Then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous window.

    • To modify a coupon

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Coupons tab.
    2. Click edit button.
    3. Modify the appropriate information followed by selecting “Complete” button to save the restriction message.

    Define Import Files

    Use ‘Define Import Files’ option to define file formats for Stock Receiving and Stock Takes files.

    📷 Figure 5-27 Define Import Files window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Define Import Files > Stock Receiving tab.
  • Enter Number and Description of Header dialog box.
  • Use ‘Stock Receiving’ for the Purpose. File type can be ‘Fixed’ or ‘Delimited’. Enter the number of fields in the field count. If the file is delimited file type, then enter the delimiter used (comma, pipe etc.) for the delimiter field.
  • Define the Names, Data Type, Required field should be checked of Field dialog box.
  • Click ‘Complete’ button to save the stock receiving information.

    • Note: Default fields for Stock Receiving file are INV – Invoice number, SC – Stock Code and QTY – Quantity.

    📷 Figure 5-28 Define Import Files window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Define Import Files tab.
  • Enter Number and Description of Header dialog box.
    1. Use ‘Stock Takes’ for the purpose. File type can be ‘Fixed’ or ‘Delimited’. Enter the number of fields

    in the field count. If the file is delimited file type, then enter the delimiter used (comma, pipe etc.) for the delimiter field.

    1. Define the Names, Data Type, Required field should be checked of Field dialog box.
    2. Click ‘Complete’ button to save the stock receiving information.

    Note: Default fields for Stock Take file are SC – Stock Code, QTY – Quantity and ADDQTY – Additional quantity.

    Importing Files

    File Location: Specify the path for the import files in the Datapath.ini file.

    [INVENTORY]

    SETPATH=C: \C-Store Commander\IN

    Policies: Change the policy ‘File Structure to export data to a Handheld Device’ in ‘Inventory Management’ policy class to ’Text Files’.

    📷 Figure 5-29 Define Import Files window

    STOCK RECEIVING :

    Filename – Received.txt: Sample Received.txt file:

    1001, 000010120392,12

    1001, 000010120507,08 Copy ‘Received.txt’ file to the path specified in the Datapath.ini. Click on the ‘Import from File’ button to import the file. Click on the ‘Batch Report’ to review the report. After verifying all the details click on ‘Process’ to complete Stock Receiving. Quantity on hand for all the selected products will be incremented based on the quantity specified in the file.

    STOCK TAKES :

    Filename – Output.txt

    Sample Output.txt file: 068400661160, 29,0,'03/14/16 11:40:05 AM'

    Note: Date and Time field doesn’t have to be defined in the Import file format.

    Copy ‘Output.txt’ file to the path specified in the Datapath.ini. Click on the ‘Import from File’ button to import the file. Click on ‘Variance Report’ to review the report. After verifying all the details, click on the ‘Process’ button to complete Stock Takes. Quantity on hand for all the selected products will be updated based on the count quantity and additional quantity specified in the file. Sales/Refunds will be adjusted based on the time stamp in the ‘Output.txt’ file.

    📷 Figure 5-30 Define Import Files window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Reasons tab.
  • On the left side of the window, select the type of reason code and message you want to list (i.e., Payouts, Void Sales, Refund, Discount, Price Changes, Open Cash Drawer, Mark down Sales, Draws).
  • Click Add.
  • In the Code box, enter a code. In the Description box, describe the reason for this reason code (i.e., Damaged).

    • 📷 Figure 5-31 Reason window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Reasons tab.
  • On the left side of the window, select the type of reason code and message you want to list (i.e., Payouts, Void Sales, Refund, Discount, Price Changes, Open Cash Drawer, Mark down Sales, Draws).
  • Click delete button.
  • Then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous window.

    • To modify a reason code

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Reasons tab.
    2. On the left side of the window, select the type of reason code and message you want to list (i.e., Payouts, Void Sales, Refund, Discount, Price Changes, Open Cash Drawer, Mark down Sales, Draws).
    3. Click edit button.
    4. Modify the appropriate information followed by selecting “Complete” button to save the restriction message.

    Restrictions

    You can create restriction codes that will help you to display the name of the item message that will be displayed at the point of sale when the item is sold. Once the customized message along with age limit is programmed in restriction tab, this allows you to define a policy menu.

    To add a restriction message

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Restrictions tab.
    2. Click Add button.
    3. Enter Code, Restriction message, Age limit of customer. Age limit allows you set 0 if the item has no restrictions with the age.

    To delete a restriction message

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Restrictions tab.
    2. Select a restriction definition list, and then click Delete button.
    3. Then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous window.

    To modify a restriction message

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Restrictions tab.
    1. Click Edit button.
    2. Modify the appropriate information followed by selecting “Complete” button to save the restriction message.

    📷 Figure 5-32 Restriction window

    Taxes

    You can define tax and tax code and enter details regarding any tax you want to apply at your store. After you create the sales taxes, you can then assign the appropriate sale tax to each item. Any Provincial of Federal mandated changes to PST, GST and HST or the addition of new taxes (Carbon, Sugar etc.) as well as the application or removal of tax rates to specific products requires you to do the following.

    It is very important that you organize your products are organized in the appropriate Category/department that allow for the easiest application of PST/GST/HST tax types (please refer to the Provincial and CRA website and documents on sales taxes and the products they are applicable to.

    When it comes time for the required change over, you will be able to quite easily remove, edit and or add taxes to your products based on a rational and consistent Category/department structure.

    Once you are ready to proceed you must add GST & PSTand other required taxes as separate and distinct taxes per Provincial and Federal requirements for those products that require Included taxes (i.e. GST for fuel) and Added taxes (i.e. standard PST on coffee) and ensuring that those products that are Exempt from taxes are appropriately identified.

    To add a new sales tax

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Taxes tab.
    2. Click Add Tax or Add Code button.
    3. Enter the appropriate information followed by selecting “Complete” button to save the tax information.

    📷 Figure 5-33 Taxes window

    Note: Each tax applicable at a retail site has a specific setup requirement per Provincial and Federal requirements. The GST normally has 2 different tax codes that need to be set-up:

    • The first is for adding GST to a product (the GST shows as separate tax on the sales receipt),

    this is applicable to most products you sell except Fuel and those products that are GST exempt.

    • The second is for including GST in the product price, Fuel is the primary product that has GST

    included in its price.

    The PST normally only has 1 tax code that needs to be set-up:

    • This for adding PST to a product (the PST shows as separate tax on the sales receipt), this is

    applicable to most products you sell except Fuel and those products that are PST exempt.

    • For PST exempt products ensure that the PST has not been applied; this is applicable to most

    food for consumption, children’s clothes and other items – you need to verify these items.

    1. Once your taxes are setup and ready to apply to your products navigate in your Back Office > Inventory > Product Categories.
    2. In your Product categories highlight each Category/Department, or if needed go to the appropriate Sub-Categories, Detail or the Product itself and identify the appropriate product(s) that requires the tax change.
    3. You can then pick all or only some of your products that require that the appropriate Tax is either Added or Removed Highlight all appropriate tax options (GST/PST) at the top of the screen and check the box that says Select All.
    4. Next, click on the Add or Remove button, this will automatically apply the required change, if in doubt you can either random check products or reverse the process. Note: By having your products organized by a cohesive department / sub department / sub detail structure, you will be able to remove and implement the taxes very quickly. If the required prep work is not done, then errors in your taxing of products may occur.

    To delete a sales tax

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Taxes tab.
    2. Select a sales tax, and then click Delete button.
    3. Then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous

    window.

    To modify a sales tax

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Taxes tab.
    2. Click Edit button.
    3. Modify the appropriate information followed by selecting “Complete” button to save the tax

    information.

    📷 Figure 5-34 Product Classification window

    Associated Charges

    You may have already set up your initial pricing structure. However, as you continually add to your inventory, if you want to adjust the price of item by applying associated charges using Back Office system. For example associated menu option is used to setup different charges that can be linked to products. Ex. Bottle deposits.

    To add associated charge to an item

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Associated Charges tab.
    2. Click Add Charge button, enter code, description, price of an item followed by clicking “Complete” button to save the information.

    Note: While adding associated charges, if item is allowed to add tax then there is an option where you select the item followed by applying the appropriate tax.

    📷 Figure 5-35 Associated Charges window

  • Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Associated Charges tab.
  • Click Delete Charge button.
  • Then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous window.

    • To modify associated charge to an item

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Associated Charges tab.
    2. Click Edit Charge button.
    3. Modify the appropriate information followed by selecting “Complete” button to save the tax information.

    Stock Description Elements

    The stock description element menu option allow you to generate labels, edit and delete label for selected items. C-Store Commander Back Office enables you to organize your inventory description in brands, groups, and family, which will facilitate cleaner report output. You can modify your brands and groups on one window and see all your data right in front of you. Below is description of each dialog box displayed under stock description elements window, see image figure 5-1.

    Brand – a category of products that are all made by a particular company and all have a particular name. This enables you to quickly recognize all the refreshment items in the stock item. Example: Coke, Pepsi, Player’s

    Characteristics – an item which has the same characteristics of another item. Example hot, large, red.

    Unit of Measure – the quantity of the individual component contained in the assembly item. Example: ml, gr, L.

    Group –.

    Generic – an item referring to whole class or group. Example: water, tobacco, regular

    Family – Example: Coca-Cola, Imperial Tobacco

    Order Unit – Example: box, carton, roll

    To add a Brand / Characteristics / Unit of Measure / Group / Generic / Family / Order Unit

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Stock Description Elements tab.
    2. Click Add button.
    3. Enter Brand ID and Description, click “Complete” button to save the information.

    To modify a Brand / Characteristics / Unit of Measure / Group / Generic / Family / Order Unit

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Stock Description Elements tab.
    2. Click Edit button.
    3. Enter Brand,Characteristics, Unit of Measure, Group, Generic, Family, Order Unit ID and Description, click “Complete” button to save the information.

    Note: For group, make sure to define the family list before selecting family information.

    To delete a Brand / Characteristics / Unit of Measure / Group / Generic / Family / Order Unit

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Stock Description Elements tab.
    2. Click Delete button, then warning message popups for confirmation. Click Yes to confirm or No to return back to previous window.

    📷 Figure 5-36 Stock Description Elements window

    Receipts

    You can add your customized message that you would like to get print on receipt. You will see this message on sales receipt and refund receipt. You can create receipt formats that you can customize for various transaction types, such as sales, refunds. You can print different receipt formats on different registers. Create as many receipt formats as you want. You can display different types of information (i.e., store name, customer address, discounts.

    To create a receipt logo

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Receipts tab.
    2. Enter the customized message in the Sale Receipt Footer and Refund Receipt Footer followed by Save Receipt Footers to create a receipt logo.

    📷 Figure 5-37 Receipts window

    Setup Sound

    You can setup sound for each point of sale and pump activity. To activate the sound your back office computer must be connected to speaker to be able to listen. Various sounds can be configured to play when specific function are performed at the point of sale.

    To setup a sound:

    1. Click the Back Office > Data Maintenance > Setup Sound tab.
    2. Select from the list of options available on Pump Sound dialog box and then click “Edit” button followed choosing appropriate options followed by “Save” button to setup the sound.
    3. Apply the same procedure for Device Sounds and System Sounds list of options. Pumps Sounds are meant for knowing the activity of pump. Each pump activity is setup by distinct pump sound.
    1. Device sounds are meant for knowing the activity of hardware equipment like while scanning an item or while till opens when transaction is processed. System sounds are meant for knowing activity of internal software updates.

    📷 Figure 5-38 Setup Sound window

    Chapter 6

    ⚙️ Policies & Settings

    CHAPTER 6

    Policies

    A “Policy” is simply a rule about how your business operates. C-Store Commander is unique in many of its approaches to point-of-sale but the thing that really sets it apart is its support for management of the system by describing the business practices that are used in your business. C-Store Commander uses that information to configure itself so that you have a point-of-sale system that works the way you want your business to run rather than forcing you to change your business practices to accommodate the point-of-sale system. Most of the system policies will be set before a store opening but some options can be changed by authorized users (manager, owner) as required and needed.

    Note: We advise that any change or contemplated change to policies be carefully documented and analyzed to ensure that the required solution/change is appropriately done.

    Alert: Be very careful while changing options in system policies. Options can have serious effects on C-Store

    Commander Back Office. We advise that any change or contemplated change to policies be carefully

    documented and analyzed to ensure that the required solution/change is appropriately done.

    📷 Figure 6-1 Policies Menu Window

    Screen Region : Policy by Class/Entity window contains several screen regions listed in the following table.

    Screen RegionDefinition
    LevelChoose which level you want to edit. Example: Company, Manager, Cashier etc.
    ValueClick in this column to enter/select a value for the denomination.
    SettingChoose which value to enter/select while applying a policy.

    Policies by Class

    Policy by class sections are composed of the following:

    • Archive / Purge Settings
    • Pricing Policies
    • Business Practices
    • Printing Options
    • Carwash •Sounds
    • Customers •Store Credits
    • Discounts •Tax Exempt Options
    • Features •Third Party Cards
    • Fuel Management •Till End of Day Options
    • Gift and Loyalty Programs •Till Sales Options
    • Gift Certificates • Time Sheets
    • Integrated Loyalty Programs •Unattended Fueling
    • Inventory Management •User Authorizations
    • Loyalty Programs •User System Settings
    • Payouts

    Archive / Purge Settings

    Archiving is the process of backing up the obsolete data that will be deleted during the purge process. During the archive process, data will be moved from the main transactional tables to the backup tables of your SQL database. By default the Back Office application purge scripts will archive data that will be deleted during the purge process. Purging is the process of freeing up space in the database or of deleting obsolete data that is not required by the system. The purge process can be based on the age of the data or the type of data.

    System Shouldn’t Delete Data Less Than Specified No of Days

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides guidelines for the administration of point of sale data to a specified

    period of time. An attempt to delete data newer than the specified value should provide an error message.

    SettingThe value entered in settings column will definethe policy settings. For example ifthe

    value is set to 60 the system will retain last 60 days data and remaining will be erased.

    Business Practices

    Business Practices is an essential feature of process or rule followed by a company needed to effect standard operating procedures in a consistent manner.

    Name of Base Currency

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to set base currency in which the system makes change and to

    which all other currencies are compared in currency exchange conversion.

    SettingSelect the name of base currency you wish to set; Example ‘CASH’.

    Alert: This settings must not be changed once your point-of-sale system has been in operation, changing this

    settings may result in unpredictable changes. The system will assume the base tender is used to make change

    and when modified, reports of historical sales, till closes, etc., will attribute change to the wrong tender.

    Compute Tax On Other Included Taxes

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to compute tax on other included taxes. For instance in certain

    province like Quebec, on fuel products two different taxes are applied.

    YesTaxes are calculated on the total amount that has been taxed already
    NoTaxes are calculated on an amount that has not had other taxes already applied.

    Freezing Interval __(1-10000 Milliseconds)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to set the time-intervalto freeze the computer upon no activity.

    Time in milliseconds for message to be displayed each time on “Freeze” screen.

    Setting Value ranges from 1 – 10000;

    Accounting System

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to get summary of tender and sales data integrating with various

    accounting systems such as Accpac, Simply Accounting to manage accounts. Integrating Accpac with C-Store commander eliminates the need to enter sales and other data from c-store manually into accounting software.

    AccpacIf Accpac,then Accpac tab on Back Office > Routine Maintenance > Accpac will be

    enabled to perform financial activities from C-Store commander to accounting software.

    Simply AccountingIf Simply Accounting, on Back Office > Routine Maintenance > Accpac will be enabled

    to perform financial activities from C-Store commander to accounting software.

    NoneIf None, then C-Store commander will not integrate with any accounting software.

    Note: Prior license is required to use Accpac or Simply Accounting features on C-Store Commander. Upon on integration, if there is no sales data then the message ‘No data available!’ will display, and ‘Last close number was already processed’ will be displayed.

    Support Data Localization

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy is no longer use.

    Ask Password Entry For Fuel Control

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to enter password to login to Fuel Control application. Fuel

    Control allows the pumps to dispense fuel.

    YesIf Yes, login to fuel control application is required.
    NoIf No, no login details required to open fuel control application.

    Version

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to identity the type of banking/payment processing. Version can

    be US or Canadian.

    USIf US, then US banking/payment processing will be enabled.
    CanadianIf Canadian, then Canadian banking/payment processing will be enabled.

    Use Debit Swipe

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support debit swipe on Pinpad system.
    YesIf Yes, debit swipe on pinpad will be enabled.
    NoIf No, debit swipe on pinpad will not be enabled.

    Maximum Times For Debit Swipe

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to set number of times debit swipe is allowed per day.
    Setting Value ranges from 1-10; Set 2 if you want to allow 2 times per day.

    Allow Manual Fuel Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to do manual fuel sale. Manual fuel sale must be enabled if

    there is no connectivity between the fuel control and pumps.

    YesIf Yes, manual fuel sale will be enabled.
    NoIf No, manual fuel sale will be disabled.

    Note: On manual fuel sale, the system cannot track pump sales.

    Support Dip Input

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports manual DIP inputwhen automatic DIP is not available. DIP input

    screen will be available in the POS.

    YesIf Yes, manual DIP button on POS will be enable to enter DIP information.
    NoIf No, manual DIP button on POS will be disabled.

    Dip Input Time

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to setup automatic screen pop up time for DIP input.
    SettingSetup the time when you want to enter DIP input.

    Compute Tax on Taxes

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to compute tax on all other taxes
    YesIf Yes, enable tax on taxes policy.
    NoIf No, disable tax on taxes policy.

    Transfer Data Type Is _

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to transfer data format from stores to head office. It will be

    beneficial if you required to transfer sales, fuel price, and promotion data to each site on frequent basis.

    TextIf Text, text format will be enabled. "Text" option is not supported any more.
    DTSIf DTS, data transfer from store to head office through DTS (Data Transfer System)

    Last Shift Number for Read Totalizer

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to get totalizer report at specified shift number. ‘0’means

    totalizer reading will be taken for all till closes. Otherwise, totalizer reading will be taken based on the shift number specified here.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-5;

    Activate Dealer Change?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy is no longer use.

    Multi Thread Bank Lane (Bank Keys)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports multiple thread bank processing for Pay@Pump on Gilbarco pumps

    as each pump is connected to different payment processing system.

    YesIf Yes, supports multiple thread bank processing for Pay@Pump on Gilbarco pumps.
    NoIf No, doesn’t support multi thread bank processing for Pay@Pump.

    Enter the Maximum Pre-Auth Limits ($)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to set maximum Pre-Auth amount limit for Pay@Pump Debit.
    SettingValue ranges from 1 – 999;

    Allow Pre-set at Dispenser

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to enable pre-set at the pump. Pre-set is an option allowing you

    to quickly select the amount on pump reader to dispense the gas.

    YesIf Yes, enables pre-set at the pumps.
    NoIf No, disables pre-set at the pumps.

    Support Input Message?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policyenables message button in the POS options screen.The message button

    allows the cashier to choose from list of message box to track the various events in the store. This option is available for stores using DVRS technology.

    YesIf Yes, message button will be enabled on POS system.
    NoIf No, message button will be disabled on POS system.

    Stock Item Settings are based on a Vendor

    Purpose of PolicyThispolicy prompts the system to link the stock items to vendors. All items shouldbe

    linked to a vendor.

    YesIf Yes, each item must be linked to vendors.
    NoIf No, not required to link item to vendors.

    Time is printed in _ format

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to setup time printed on the receipt is based on either 12 hours

    or 24 hours format.

    12 HourIf 12 Hour, time printed on the receipt will be 12 hour.
    24 HourIf 24 Hour, time printed on the receipt will be 24 hour.

    Bank System (select from the list)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to accept credit and debit cards with the POS system using pinpad.
    GlobalIf Global, the bank system must be global payments.
    MonerisIf Moneris, the bank system must be Moneris solutions.
    DesjardinsIf Desjardin, the bank system must be Desjardin.
    DataPacIf DataPac, the bank system must be DataPac.
    NoneIf None, there is no integrated banking connection to process credit and debit cards.

    Allow Minimize POS Window?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enable/disable minimize button on the POS screen.
    YesIf Yes, enable minimize button on the POS screen
    NoIf No, disable minimize button on the POS screen

    Daily Sales Report Entry Mode

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to generate the daily sales report in report menu from the option

    you choose. Each option has unique feature in displaying daily sales report. Input Screen allows you to modify the data from live data whereas excel reports needs to be manually entered.

    ExcelIf Excel, daily sales report format will be based on excel spread sheet.
    InputIf Input Screen, daily sales report format will be based on Input screen.

    IP Address of Tidel Server

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to configure Tidel server through IP address. Tidel Server is a

    cash management to streamline, automate, and secure stores cash environment.

    SettingEnter the IP address of back office that connects to Tidel Safe.

    TCP Listening Port of Tidel Server

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows TCP port for Tidel Server to connect.
    SettingValue ranges from 1 – 999999;

    Note: Tidel Server is a cash management to streamline, automate, and secure stores cash environment.

    Tidel Communication Time Out

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup Tidel communication timeout.
    SettingValue ranges from 1- 99;

    Tidel User ID

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to enter Tidel user id.
    SettingEnter the appropriate Tidel User ID assigned.

    Tidel Markup File Location?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to setup Marker file location for Tidel Safe.
    SettingEnter the complete path of file location. Example: C:\SIP\Logs

    Shift Number for Automatic Fuel Management Reading

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to generate automatic totalizer reading from the Fuel

    Management application at shift number specified. 0 refers to end of each shift. Fuel Management is the process used to track and report fuel tank contents.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-5;

    Note: The Fuel Management module is designed to provide robust fuel management features for a single site operation to combine both manual processes (tank setup, deliveries) as well as automatic information (tank dips, totalizers).

    Display DTS Error Message?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy display DTS transfer error messages in the BackOffice application DTS

    transfer screen.

    YesIf Yes, displays DTS transfer error message.
    NoIf No, DTS transfer error message will not be displayed.

    Support Store & Forward (SAF) Transactions?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enable/disable store and forward for Non-EMV transaction.
    YesIf Yes, enable store and forward for Non-EMV transaction
    NoIf No, disable store and forward for Non-EMV transaction

    Allow only “Call Bank Authorization” to SAF Transactions?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policyrestrict only call bank option for store and forward transactions. Automatic

    SAF is not supported. Needs authorization number from Bank to complete transactions?

    YesIf Yes, authorization message will pop-up to approve
    NoIf No, authorization message will not be displayed.

    Support EMV Compliance?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to supports EMV compliance for POS. EMV is a global standard

    for transactions made on chip-based debit, prepaid and credit cards.

    YesIf Yes, supports EMV compliance for POS.
    NoIf No, does not support EMV compliance for POS.

    Corporate Location

    Purpose of PolicyTo identify the store as a corporate or non-corporate location. It helps in getting daily

    sales report.

    YesIf Yes, site will be reported as corporate.
    NoIf No, site will be reported as non-corporate.

    Support KITS in Purchasing & Receiving?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to supports kits in purchasing/receiving tab.
    YesIf Yes, can create kits in purchasing/receiving tab.
    NoIf No, cannot create kits in purchasing/receiving tab.

    Note: Once this policy is enable then Purchasing and Receiving module will be enabled on Back Office computer to save stock receiving inventory.

    Purchasing Support Price Change along with Cost Update?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy changes the price when you change the cost in purchasing/receiving tab.
    YesIf Yes, prices changes when cost of product updates.
    NoIf No, price and cost will not be same.

    Maximum Amount to Qualify for Tax Rebate?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup maximum amount to qualify for Ontario tax rebate

    program. Ontario tax rebate is a tax credits and incentive program available to

    business incorporated in Ontario to help lower costs, hire/train workers, compete in the marketplace and support various sectors.

    Setting Value ranges from 1-100;

    Support Tax Rebate?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows your system to supportstax rebate program. A tax refund or tax

    rebate is a refund on taxes when the tax liability is less than the tax paid. In Canada each province has its own tax rebate program. For example British Columbia – SITE, Alberta – AITE, Quebec – QITE etc.

    YesIf Yes, supports tax rebate program.
    NoIf No, does not support tax rebate program.

    Allow both SAFE and ATM Cash Drop?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow SAFE and ATM cash drop.SAFE/ATM allows you to keep a daily

    history of all cash draw/ cash drop made by each cashier in order to protect cash flow. Once this policy is enabled then user must enter the reference number to reconcile the data.

    YesIf Yes, enables SAFE and ATM cash drop.
    NoIf No, disables SAFE and ATM cash drop.

    Do you want to track the count of coins in the safe?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to track count of coins in the SAFE Management system.
    YesIf Yes, enables you to track count of coins in the SAFE;
    NoIf No, disables you to track count of coins in the SAFE;

    Enter changes by item in Purchasing and Receiving?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to enter charges in the Purchasing/Receiving tab. To make

    changes in entering Purchasing and Receiving: BO > Purchase and Receiving > Select Status “Open” followed by PO > Click Detail button > Enter Charges. You will be allowed to enter charges once this policy is enabled.

    YesIf Yes, enabled entering charges.
    NoIf No, disabled entering charges.

    Notify about Fuel Price changes from Head Office _ times

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup number of timeabout notifying fuel pricechanges

    between head office and each site per day.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-10

    Notify about Fuel Price changes from Head Office every_ min

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup time interval in minutes about notifying fuel price changes

    between head office and each site.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-10

    Support EMV Compliance at the Pump?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports EMV compliance at the pump.
    YesIf Yes, enables pay @ pump EMV transactions.
    NoIf No, disables pay @ pump EMV transactions.

    Create PO and PD files for Purchasing and Receiving

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to create PO and PD files.PO and PD files are portable

    files used to facilitate transferring inventory data using purchasing and receiving tab.

    YesIf Yes, enables to create PO and PD files.
    NoIf No, disables to create PO and PD files.

    Use rollback in Back Office to Head Office Transfer

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enable roll back for DTS. Rollback allows store to send sales, fuel information

    to head office.

    YesIf Yes, enable roll back for DTS.
    NoIf No, disable roll back for DTS.

    Bank Transaction Timeout in Seconds

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup timeout for bank communication. Upon enabling this policy

    user who uses Pinpad must enter their credentials within the timeframe set to process the transaction smoothly.

    Setting Value ranges from 1-10000;

    Login using Windows User

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to use windows user for login back office.
    YesIf Yes, enables to use windows user for login.
    NoIf No, disables to use windows user for login.

    Allow Manual Credit Card Processing

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support manual credit card processing. It helps in processing

    manual credit card processing in the event of not recognizing card information.

    YesIf Yes, manual credit card will be displayed upon Accept Tender button to enter

    credit/debit card information followed by expiry date.

    NoIf No, doesn’t not support manual entry of card information.

    Pinpad Type

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to choose the type of pinpad. InfoNet C-Store Commander

    integrates with Global Payment (ICT-250) and Moneris Solutions (IPP320, VX810) payment terminals.

    VX810If VX810, the pinpad model must be VS810;
    IPP320If IPP320, the pinpad model must be IPP320;

    Enable Wex Fleet Card

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to processWEX fleet cards. WEX Inc. is a provider of payment

    processing and information management services to the United States commercial and government vehicle fleet industry.

    YesIf Yes, supports WEX Fleet cards.
    NoIf No, doesn’t not support WEX.

    Wex Fleet TPS Server IP

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to enter IP address for Wex server to process fleet cards.
    SettingEnter IP address the WEX Fleet TPS Server IP. Example: 192.168.85.100

    Wex Fleet TPS Server Port

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables you to connect to Wex server port
    SettingValue ranges from 1-10000

    Is this a Dealer Location?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to identify whether the store is a dealer location.
    YesIf Yes, site will be a dealer site.
    NoIf No, site will be a non-dealer site.

    Calculate Transaction Fee for Credit and Debit Cards?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to calculate transaction fee for credit and debit cards.
    YesIf Yes, transaction fee for credit can debit cards will be charged.
    NoIf No, no transaction fee will be charged.

    Carwash

    Carwash is a facility used to clean the exterior and, in some cases, the interior of motor vehicles. Car washes can be self-serve, fully automated, or full-service with attendants who wash the vehicle.

    Carwash Server IP Address

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to connect carwash system such as Kesseltronics, Ryko, Unitec,

    and Exacta though IP address.

    SettingEnter the IP address of carwash server.

    TCP Listening Port of Carwash Server

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to connect listening port for carwash server.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-999999;

    Carwash Communication Timeout (sec)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup communication timeout in seconds.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-2000;

    Is Carwash Integrated?

    Purpose of PolicyIs car wash integrated (Ryko, Kesseltronics, Unitec) or manual.
    YesIf Yes, carwash setup as integrated.
    NoIf No, carwash setup as manual.

    Carwash Category Name

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to setup carwash department ID for reporting purpose.
    SettingSelect from the list available in Department and Name format;

    Support Carwash at the Pump?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy support carwash at the pumps.
    YesIf Yes, enables carwash at the pumps.
    NoIf No, disable carwash at the pumps

    Customer

    A customer is an individual or business that purchases the goods or services produced by a stores having setup customer account to get discount on certain products. Attracting customers is the primary goal of most public- facing businesses, because it is the customer who creates demand for goods and services.

    Use Customers

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to enable customer button on POS screen. If Loyalty program is

    used then Loyalty button appears. This policy allows you to get AR Account Payment reports and all customer related reports.

    YesIf Yes, customer button appears on POS screen along with customer id on receipt.
    NoIf No, customer does not appear and no customer ID on receipt.

    Note: This policy interacts with the following policies: Do you offer credit terms? Do you use loyalty system? Use customer codes? Give sale price if lower?

    Do You Offer Credit Terms?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enable AR button on point of sale computer to select and offer

    credit to customer. Prior issuing, AR account must be setup in back office computer.

    YesIf Yes, AR account payment appears in options. “Account” Tender appears on tender

    screen.

    NoIf No, AR button will be non-functional.

    Use AR Customers

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to identify the AR customer on point of sale when cashier

    proceed to accept tender.

    YesIf Yes, the POS allows customer to be identified as an AR customer.
    NoIf No, AR Customers are not used.

    Show Customer Account Balance in Receipt?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to display customer balance on the AR payment receipt.
    YesIf Yes, customer balance will be display on AR payment receipt.
    NoIf No, prints the receipt with no customer balance on AR payment receipt.

    Show Customer Note if the Customer is Over Limit (Y/N)?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to display customer note on point of sale screen when the

    customer is over limit of purchase.

    YesIf Yes, enables popup message on point of sale screen.
    NoIf No, disables popup message on point of sale screen.

    Display Parent Account if Customer Card is Linked (Y/N)?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to display parent account if the customer card linked to any

    customer or customer group on the back office.

    YesIf Yes, display customer account if customer cards are linked to the customer.
    NoIf No, then customer details will not be displayed on the customer screen. Cashier has to

    swipe the customer card to pick up the customer information.

    Use Default Customer

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to set the default customer on POS computer automatically,

    otherwise default customer will be "Cash".

    YesIf Yes, you will be able to select customer to apply promotional settings.
    NoIf No, default customer will be cash.

    Default Customer is

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to select the default customer from customer list automatically for

    each transaction.

    YesIf Yes, enables to select from customer button.
    NoIf No, default customer will be “Cash”.

    Charge the Customer Account when using Customer Card?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides and optionto charge a specific AR tender. This way incumbent

    customer will be billed monthly.

    YesIf Yes, enables charging customer account when using customer card.
    NoIf No, disables charging customer account when using customer card.

    Tender Name for Customer AR Account

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to create tender name for customer AR account.
    YesIf Yes, enables creating tender name for customer AR account.
    NoIf No, disables creating tender name for customer AR account.

    Allow Member Code Entry at the Pump?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support member code entry at the pump. It prompts a message on

    pumps to enter customer member code to get rewards.

    YesIf Yes, enables member code entry at the pumps.
    NoIf No, disables member code entry at the pumps.

    Scan Customer Cards in POS to identify the Customer?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to scan customer cards in the point of sale customer screen

    to select customer.

    YesIf Yes, enables scanning customer cards.
    NoIf No, disables scanning customer cards.

    Swipe Customer Cards in POS to identify the Customer?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to swipe customer cards in POS customer screen to select

    customer.

    YesIf Yes, enables to read customer information when swiping the customer card.
    NoIf No, disables to read customer information when swiping the customer card.

    Verify Expiry Date on Customer Cards?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enable an option to verify expiry date on customer cards.
    YesIf Yes, enables expiry date verification of customer cards.
    NoIf No, disables expiry date verification of customer cards.

    Use Customer or Member in Back Office Definition Screen?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to change the label for customer in the back office data

    definition screen. Default is ‘Customer’.

    YesIf Yes, Customer will be changed to Member.
    NoIf No, default as “Customer”.

    Use A/R Invoice Menu

    Purpose of PolicyThis is a customised policy to support Key lock pricing for A/R invoicing.
    YesIf Yes, supports use of A/R invoice menu.
    NoIf No, does not support use of A/R invoice menu.

    Discounts

    Discounts can be applied to an entire sale or individual item whether it is dollar value or percentage value. This section gives an idea about discount policies and its each features.

    Do You Offer Discounts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to apply discount on inventory sales performed on POS system.
    YesIf Yes, discount button appears and options and discount column is accessible.
    NoIf No, discount button does not appear and discount column is not accessible.
    Set at LevelsLevel refers to range of products you would like to apply discount. Example Company,

    Department, Sub Department Level, Sub-Detail level etc.

    Value Value refers to range of value you would like to apply discount. Example: 0-10; if 1 is

    set the discount applies to department -1;

    Alert: If policy is set to ‘No’, then other discount policies will have no effect.

    Note: If discounts are allowed for some items but not others, the discount text box will be enabled for items allowed discounts and disabled for all others. If the Discount button is to be enabled then the ‘Company’ setting should be set to ‘Yes’ and all other categories not receiving discounts should be set to ‘No’. These settings only control what the user can perform on the system.

    Maximum Discount Percent

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to apply maximum allowable discount given by the system

    (percentage)

    SettingValue ranges from 0-100;

    Maximum Discount in Dollars

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to apply maximum allowable discount given by the system.
    Setting Value ranges from 0-999999;

    Dollar Discounts on a line are

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to apply dollar discount on range of products. If line total is

    selected the discount applies to whole sale line of products whereas each refers to discount being apply to single product.

    On Line Total If Line Total, Discount calculated is: (Quantity * Price) - Discount
    On Each ItemIf Each Item, Discount calculated is: Quantity * (Price – Discount)

    Use Customer Discount codes

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables to apply customer with a discount code purchasing a product with a

    product discount code will automatically receive the discount specified in the discount table.

    YesIf Yes, customer with a discount code purchasing a product with a product discount code

    will automatically receive the discount specified in the discount table.

    NoIf No, product is assumed to have a zero discount.

    Use Product Discount Codes

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables to apply customer with a discount code purchasing a product with a

    product discount code will automatically receive the discount specified in the discount table

    YesIf Yes, customer with a discount code purchasing a product with a product discount code

    will automatically receive the discount specified in the discount table.

    NoIf No, product is assumed to have a zero discount.

    Override User Discount from Table

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy overrides user discount from table by applying discount calculated from the

    discount table will replace the discount entered by the user unless the calculated discount is zero

    YesIf Yes, discount calculated from the discount table will replace the discount entered by

    the user unless the calculated discount is zero.

    NoIf No, discounts entered by user remain zero.

    Give Automatic Discounts on Sale Items

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows automatic discounts on each items of sale line.
    YesIf Yes, a discount calculated from the discount table will be applied to every item where

    a discount applies.

    NoIf No, a discount calculated from the discount table will be applied to an item if it is not

    currently on sale.

    Use Reasons for Discounts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to select the reason for discount when user would like to give

    discount.

    YesIf Yes, reason is required when giving a discount.
    NoIf No, no reason is required when giving a discount.

    Support Customer Fuel Rebate?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enable fuel rebate based on customer (AFFDA/TEFU program in Alberta).
    YesIf Yes, supports customer fuel rebate.
    No, If No, doesn’t not support customer fuel rebate.

    Enable Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print grocery coupon for pay @ pump and prepay transactions

    based on tender type. Print receipt with Grocery Coupon details based on the tender used for Pay@Pump sales.

    YesIf Yes, enable fuel sale grocery coupon feature.
    No, If No, disable fuel sale grocery coupon feature.

    Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon – Rate Calculated From

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to change coupon discount rate based on either amount or quantity.

    Default is quantity.

    AmountIf Amount, enables to change coupon discount rate based on either amount.
    QuantityIf Quantity No, default will be quantity.

    Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon – Value for Debit and Others

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to change grocery coupon discount value for debit and other tenders.
    Setting Enter the value.

    Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon – Value for Credit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to change grocery coupon discount value for credit tenders. Grocery

    coupon discount value for credit.

    SettingEnter the value.

    Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon – PLU Number

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to use default PLU number used for the fuel sale grocery coupon.
    SettingEnter the value.

    Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon – Expiration Period in Days

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup expiry date for the fuel sale grocery coupon.
    SettingEnter the value.

    Print Fuel Sale Grocery Coupon for AR Tender

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to enable/disable grocery coupon for AR tender.
    YesIf Yes, enables fuel sale grocery coupon for AR tender.
    No, If No, disables fuel sale grocery coupon for AR tender.

    Support Cash/Debit Fuel Discounts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to apply cash/debit discount after selecting accept tender

    button. User must follow certain prompts to get discount.

    YesIf Yes, appropriate discount applies when cash/debit card used.
    No, If No, regular pricing applies when credit or other cards used.

    Display Fleet and Credit for Cash/Debit Fuel Discounts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup display of credit and debit on tender.When support

    cash/debit fuel discount policy is enabled then this policy allows to display appropriate button on tender.

    YesIf Yes, enables display of credit and debit on tender.
    No, If No, disables display of credit and debit on tender.

    Display Fleet and Credit for Customer Group ID

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to apply discount to customer group where we can apply discount.
    SettingSelect the appropriate option from the list.

    Features

    A feature is a distinctive functionality that delivers business value. Features can include additions or changes to existing functionality.

    Pay Inside Credit/Debit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support pay inside store credit/debit card. Once this policy is

    enabled then system process debit/credit card on point of sale system.

    YesIf Yes, enables pay inside credit/debit cards.
    No, If No, disables pay inside credit/debit cards.

    Support Third Party Card

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support third party cards. A third-party transaction is a business

    deal involving a buyer, a seller and a third party. The third party's involvement varies with the type of business transaction. For example, an online payment portal, such as PayPal, acts as a third party in a retail transaction.

    YesIf Yes, supports third party cards.
    No, If No, does not supports third party cards.

    Support Fleet Card

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support fleet cards. A fuel card or fleet card is used as a

    payment card most commonly for gasoline, diesel, and other fuels at gas stations. Fleet cards can also be used to pay for vehicle maintenance and expenses at the discretion of the fleet owner or manager.

    YesIf Yes, supports fleet cards.
    No, If No, does not supports fleet cards.

    Support Loyalty System

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support loyalty cards. A loyalty program is a rewards program

    offered by a company to customers who frequently make purchases. A loyalty program

    may give a customer advanced access to new products, special sales coupons or free merchandise.

    YesIf Yes, supports loyalty cards.
    No, If No, does not support loyalty cards.

    Support A/R

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to support AR cards on point of sale system. The AR Credit Cards

    feature allows you to "bulk transfer" credit card transactions from Front Office guest folios and partial payments to credit card company AR accounts. At the time they are transferred to AR, the transactions you select are consolidated into a single AR invoice for each credit card issuer. From AR, you can keep track of outstanding amounts and amounts that have been paid based on the batches that were forwarded to the credit card companies.

    YesIf Yes, supports A/R cards.
    No, If No, does not support A/R cards.

    Enhanced Reports

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to generate enhanced reports. While using InfoNet C-Store

    Commander back office, user will initially provide with lite version of reports. Upon enabling user has access to all reports of back office.

    YesIf Yes, access to enhanced reports.
    No, If No, access to lite version of reports.

    Multi POS Support

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to supports more than one POS till computer.
    YesIf Yes, you can use more than POS.
    No, If No, you can use only one POS.

    Supports Head Office System

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports sharing data from head office systemto individual store. Data

    Transfer System DTS allows the users to transfer data from head office and to send sales data to head office.

    YesIf Yes, enable to transfer data to head office.
    No, If No, disable to transfer data to head office.

    Pump Control

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports pump control.Pump Control or Fuel Dispenser is a machine that is

    used to pump gasoline. Example: Wayne, Gilbarco, PIE.

    YesIf Yes, then system supports pump control.
    No, If No, then system does not supports pump control.

    Pay at the Pump Credit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports credit cards on Pay@Pump. Credit cards is payment issued to users

    to enable the cardholder to pay a fuel retailer for fuel item.

    YesIf Yes, supports credit card payments at pay @ pump.
    No, If No, does not supports credit card payments at pay @ pump.

    Pay at the Pump Debit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports debitcards on Pay@Pump.A payment card that deducts money

    directly from a consumer's checking/saving account to pay for a purchase of fuel.

    YesIf Yes, supports debit card payments at Pay@Pump.
    No, If No, system does not supports debit card payments at Pay@Pump.

    Do You Use Fuel Price Display?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you the system to supports fuel price displays like Novyc and

    Daktronics. Fuel Price display provide you with customized LED display solutions whenever an oil prices changes.

    YesIf Yes, supports fuel display price change.
    No, If No, system does not supports fuel display price change

    Support Tax Exempt

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables you to supports tax exempt. All other related policies are configured

    in the tax exempt policy class of your back office computer.

    YesIf Yes, enables tax exempt feature.
    No, If No, disables tax exempt feature.

    Support Promotional Sale

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to do a promotional sale. Sales promotion is one level or type of

    marketing aimed either at the consumer or at the distribution channel (in the form of sales-incentives). It is used to introduce new product, clear out inventories, attract traffic, and to lift sales temporarily.

    YesIf Yes, enables sales promotion.
    No, If No, disables sales promotion.

    Supports Third Party Card?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables you to supports third party cards like Milliplein.
    YesIf Yes, enables supporting third party cards.
    No, If No, disables supporting third party cards.

    Support Shift Reconciliation?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables you to supports shift reconciliation.Shift Reconciliation means

    counting the funds in a specific till and comparing with the expected amount. A till can be reconciled at any time during the day.

    YesIf Yes, system support shift reconciliation.
    No, If No, system does not support shift reconciliation.

    Support Purchasing and Receiving?

    Purpose of PolicySupports Purchasing and Receiving.C-Store Commander can manage and track your

    inventory by continually recording changes to increases or decreases in your on-hand quantities enabling purchasing and receiving tab in inventory menu.

    YesIf Yes, enables purchasing and receiving tab.
    No, If No, disables purchasing and receiving tab.

    Support Tank Gauge System Integration?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables you to supports tank gauge integration to INCON, Veeder-Root and

    OPW. Tank gauging means liquid measurement in large storage tanks to determine product volume. Tank Gauging System ensures accurate level, temperature, and pressure measurements for inventory control, oil movement and overfill prevention, securing efficient operations.

    YesIf Yes, supports tank gauge system integration.
    No, If No, does not support tank gauge integration.

    Support Price Book Management at Store?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports price book management at the store level (not from head office). Y

    Price Book Management set up your pricing structure to ensure that the margins you set are maintained and the pricing is consistent.

    YesIf Yes, enables using store level price book management.
    No, If No, disable using store level price book management.

    Use Tidel Safe (Y/N)?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables you to supports Tidel safe management system.
    YesIf Yes, supports the use of Tidel safe.
    No, If No, does not supports the use of Tidel safe.

    Support Check –In/Check-Out System and Timesheet Reports

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to supports timesheet check-in/check-out system.
    YesIf Yes, support check –in/check-out system and timesheet reports
    No, If No, does not support check –in/check-out system and timesheet reports

    Support EZiPin?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support EZiPin phone card system. EZiPin is a retailers with prepaid

    products and payment solutions.

    YesIf Yes, enable processing EZiPin cards.
    No, If No, disable processing EZiPin cards.

    Support Private Card Restriction Profiles?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to supportprivate card restriction profiles.Private card restriction

    allows you setup restriction on certain cards such as Fleet Cards, AR Card customer to purchase certain products like cigarettes, non-fuel products.

    YesIf Yes, enables tendering private card restrictions on customer profiles.
    No, If No, disable tendering private card restrictions on customer profiles.

    Support Carwash?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to supports Carwash interfaces like Ryko, Unitec, and Kesseltronics. By

    allowing this policy the system integrate with above discussed equipment to facilitate car wash system.

    YesIf Yes, enables facilitating carwash.
    No, If No, disable facilitating carwash.

    Support Private Card Restriction Profiles for Pay Pump?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support private card restriction profiles for Pay@Pump.
    YesIf Yes, enables tendering private card restrictions on customer profiles for Pay@Pump.
    No, If No, disable tendering private card restrictions on customer profiles for Pay@Pump.

    Support Fuel Price Change from Head Office?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support fuel price change from head office.
    YesIf Yes, enables fuel price change from head office.
    No, If No, disables fuel price change from head office.

    Enable EXACTA Carwash

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support EXACTA carwash. EXACTA provides electronic control

    systems for companies in the carwash and other retail industries.

    YesIf Yes, enables integrating EXACTA carwash.
    No, If No, disables integrating EXACTA carwash.

    Fuel Management

    Fuel Management is a process used to track and report fuel tank contents. This is designed to provide fuel management features for an operation that manually records fuel deliveries, adjustments and tank dips.

    Do You Use Fuel System?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support fuel dispensers to dispense fuel.
    YesIf Yes, pump control program runs and fuel screen becomes active
    NoIf No, pump control does not run, pumps shown as idle.

    Units of Measure for Fuel

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to set units of measure for fuel to be printed on receipt. Depending

    on provincial regulatory standard customer defines appropriate units of measure.

    Setting. Unit (Litres, Gallons, Kilograms, Pounds) to be specified on receipt.

    Do You Use Fuel Group Price?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows use of tier, level, and grade pricing. Examples are day/night pricing,

    full/self-service pricing, and regular/mid-grade/premium pricing.

    YesIf Yes, set for different pricing such as day/night pricing, full/self-service pricing, and

    regular/mid-grade/premium pricing

    NoIf No, set for a single type pricing only.

    Do You Sell Propane?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to sell propane tank.
    YesIf Yes, your facilities sell propane.
    NoIf No, your facilities do not sell propane.

    Support Cash/Credit Pricing

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support cash/credit pricing on fuel items. Once this policy is enabled

    pricing on fuel item will effect on mode of payment whether its cash or credit.

    YesIf Yes, different pricing depending on cash or credit use is enabled.
    NoIf No, different pricing depending on cash or credit use is disabled. Cash pricing only.

    Allow Post Pay?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enable/disable Post Pay. Post-Pay is for gas customers who prefer

    to pump first- then pay inside.

    YesIf Yes, post pay is enabled.
    NoIf No, post pay is disabled.

    Allow Prepay?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enable/disable PrePay. Prepay is for gas customers who prefer to

    pay inside first then pump the gas.

    YesIf Yes, prepay is enabled.
    NoIf No, prepay is disabled.

    Support Cashier Authorize for Pay@Pump
