    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables an option to activate cashier authorization for Pay@Pump.
    YesIf Yes, enables cashier authorization for Pay@Pump.
    NoIf No, disables cashier authorization for Pay@Pump.

    Display Confirm Message to Stop Pump?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enablesconfirmation message to stop pump.Confirmation message will be

    displayed on POS screen prior proceeding to stop pump activity.

    YesIf Yes, display message will be enabled.
    NoIf No, display message will be disabled.

    Allow Stack Fuel Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allowsstack fuel sale. C-Store Commander supports 2 stack fuels sales for

    better user experience.

    YesIf Yes, allows stack fuel sale.
    NoIf No, does not allow stock fuel sale.

    Default to Read Totalizer while Setting Fuel Price

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to take totalizer reading by default while setting fuel price.

    Automatically checks Totalizer option on fuel price display screen.

    YesIf Yes, automatically select totalizer option while setting fuel price.
    NoIf No, manually must select totalizer option while setting fuel price.

    Default to Process Tank Dip Reading when setting Fuel Price

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy process tank dip by default while setting the fuel price.
    YesIf Yes, automatically process tank dip reading while setting fuel price.
    NoIf No, manually must process tank dip reading while setting fuel price.

    Remain in Fuel Price Screen until Prices are set?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy set an option to remain in the fuel price screen until prices are set. Otherwise,

    after selecting the "SET" option, POS main screen will be displayed.

    YesIf Yes, enables to remain on fuel price screen until prices are successfully set.
    NoIf No, disables to remain on fuel price screen until prices are successfully set.

    Authorize Pump by POS ID?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy setsan option to authorize pump by POS ID. You can specify which POS

    computer can authorize which pump using the back office "Set Pump Authorization" option.

    YesIf Yes, enables pump authorization by POS ID.
    NoIf No, disables pump authorization by POS ID.

    Process Totalizer Reading during Pay at Pump Till Close?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy automatically process totalizer reading while closing the pay at the pump

    till.

    YesIf Yes, enables automatic process of totalizer reading while closing pay @ pump.
    NoIf No, disables automatic process of totalizer reading while closing pay @ pump.

    Need to Record the Product Cost as part of Fuel Delivery

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy sets an option to record product cost as part of the Fuel Delivery in the Fuel

    management module.

    YesIf Yes, enables to record product cost as part of fuel delivery.
    NoIf No, disables to record product cost as part of fuel delivery.

    Fuel Only System?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supportscustomizedfuel only systemdisplay on POS. Usedfor third party

    POS integration.

    YesIf Yes, only fuel display will be available on POS screen.
    NoIf No, complete POS display will be available on POS screen.

    IP Address for Integrated System?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter IP address for the third party software running on POS.
    SettingEnter the IP address value

    TCP Listening Port for Integrated System

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter TCP listening port for the third party POS software.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-99999;

    Integrated System Communication Timeout

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter timeout for integrated POS communication.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-99;

    Auto Deactivate Post Pay?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy set an option to auto deactivate post pay.
    YesIf Yes, supports auto deactivation of post pay.
    NoIf No, does not support auto deactivation of post pay.

    Post Pay Deactivation Time

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter time for post pay deactivation.
    SettingEnter the time in HH:MM:SS format.

    Post Pay Activation Time

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter time for post pay activation.
    SettingEnter the time in HH:MM:SS format.

    Prompt User to Dip Readings in POS Till Close

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy set an option to enter DIP readings in the POS during till close.
    YesIf Yes, enables to enter dip reading in POS till close in the event when automatic dip

    recording system is not available.

    NoIf No, disables to enter dip reading without any dip reading screen during till close.

    Maximum Threshold for Fuel Price Change is _

    Purpose of PolicyMaximum threshold for fuel price change. You can only increase or decrease fuel price

    based on the threshold limit.

    SettingValue ranges from 0.001-1.000;

    Do You Use Fuel Reconciliation Report

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports fuel reconciliation report feature.Fuel reconciliation tracks all the

    fuel that enters or exits in each of your underground tanks, regardless of delivery or dispensing activity. Then, it automatically collects metered sales information from

    electronic and mechanical dispensers and gives you accurate delivery and reconciliation reports on a shift-by-shift basis.

    YesIf Yes, fuel reconciliation feature will be enabled.
    NoIf No, fuel reconciliation feature will be disabled.

    Gift and Loyalty Programs

    Manage and implement specific loyalty programs with points, discounts at the product level, category or store- wide level, and see how the program impacted sales through detailed reports tied to each loyalty promotion. All Loyalty promotion start and stop dates are automatic ensuring maximum profitability.

    Enable ACKROO Loyalty and Gift Card

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables to support Ackroo Loyalty and Gift card. Ackroo provides gift card

    and loyalty processing solutions to help small to medium sized businesses attract, retain and grow their customers and their revenues.

    YesIf Yes, supports Ackroo loyalty and gift card
    NoIf No, does not supports Ackroo loyalty and gift card

    Customized Message

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define the customized message for Ackroo.
    SettingEnter message; Example: Does Customer have a Loyalty Card?

    Exclude from Redemption

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to exclude certain gift cards from redemption. Excluding can be done

    by Company, Stock, Sub-Detail, Sub-Department, and Department.

    YesIf Yes, excludes certain product categories from redemption.
    NoIf No, includes certain product categories from redemption.

    ACKROO TPS Server IP

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter Ackroo TPS server IP
    SettingEnter the IP address of Ackroo TPS Server. Example: 192.168.85.142

    ACKROO TPS Server Port

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter TPS server port
    SettingValue ranges from 1-10000;

    ACKROO Gift Cards

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose type for Ackroo gift card
    NoneIf None, no gift card is allowed to process transaction.
    AckrooGiftIf AckrooGift, gift card is enabled to process its transaction.

    ACKROO TPS Communication Timeout

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup Ackroo communication timeout for POS
    SettingValue ranges from 1-99;

    Rewards Caption on the Receipt

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup customized message on the receipt about reward points.
    SettingEnter the customized message; Example: Cash Plus $$$ Earned

    Loyalty waiting Timeout

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup processing time for loyalty transactions.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-10;

    Max# of no Loyalty Scanning

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to enter maximum number of loyalty scanning’s per day.
    Setting Value ranges from 1-10;

    Message when Max of No Loyalty Scanning

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display message when maximum number of loyalty scanning’s

    reached per day.

    SettingEnter message: Example: Ask every customer for CASH PLUS card.

    Gift Certificates

    Sell, redeem and manage gift vouchers as an additional revenue generating tool across all locations in your chain, with no additional third party vendor costs.

    Do you sell Gift Certificates?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to sell gift certificates on point of sale. This is a primary policy to be

    setup when you would like sell gift certificates.

    YesIf Yes, reserved code ‘GC’ invokes sale of gift certificate
    NoIf No, entering ‘GC’ will not permit sale of gift certificate.

    Alert: If policy is set to ‘No’, then all other Gift Certificates policies will have no effect.

    Are Gift Certificates Numbered?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy requires entering ‘GC’ requires entry of gift certificate number for first on

    being sold, number sold, and value of certificate

    YesIf Yes, entering ‘GC’ requires entry of gift certificate number for first on being sold,

    number sold, and value of certificate.

    NoIf No, ‘GC’ will create only one certificate with default number and value.

    Force Gift Certificates Check Off?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy requires payment of ‘GC’ will prompt display list of certificate to be checked

    off. One or more certificates may be chosen with total credited to sale.

    YesIf Yes, payment of ‘GC’ will prompt display list of certificate to be checked off. One or

    more certificates may be chosen with total credited to sale.

    NoIf No, dollar value for ‘GC’ redeemed may be entered on tender screen.

    Allow discounts on Gift Certificates

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to allow discount on gift certificate sale.
    YesIf Yes, percentage or dollar discount can be applied to gift certificates either from

    screen or from group discount function on the option screen.

    NoIf No, discount column is disabled for ‘GC’ and group discount do not apply to gift

    certificates.

    Note: If set to ‘Yes’, a user could apply a discount to Gift Certificates if policies “Do You Give Discounts” and “User Can Give Discounts” are set to ‘Yes’. (See Discount and User Authorization policies, respectively)

    Give a Credit as Change on a Gift Cert.

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose from list of options to select whether you wish to give credit

    as change on a gift certificate sale.

    NeverIf Never, a change is required on a sale involving a GC then the system issues cash for

    the change amount.

    AlwaysIf Always, a change is required on a sale involving a GC then the system may issues

    Store Credit for the change amount.

    ChoiceIf Choice, message of “Give Change as a Store Credit” is display. A ‘YES’ will issue

    change as Store Credit. A ‘NO’ will issue change as cash.

    Note: If set to ‘ALWAYS’ or ‘CHOICE’, then policy “Do You Issue Store Credits” must be set to ‘YES’, otherwise cash is issued regardless of setting.

    Report Gift Certificates in Department

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows toinclude gift certificate sales in reports of back office along with

    other items in the specified category.

    SettingSelect the department from the list.

    Gift Certificates expire in _ days

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows toset validity of gift certificate. Assigning ‘Zero’ assumes gift

    certificates are always valid. Greater than zero defines a gift certificate must be redeemed within the number of days from sale date to be valid.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-1000;

    Default Gift Certificates Amount

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set default value of gift certificate when sold. The user may enter

    any value desired at the time of sale.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-1000;

    What kind of Gift Certificates do you support?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose from kind of gift certificates the store supports. The system

    doesn’t accept any the below listed gift card if it’s not selected.

    NoneIf None, gift certificate is not supported.
    LocalGiftIf LocalGift, supports localized gift certificate.
    GivexIf Givex, supports GiveX gift cards.
    MillipleinIf Milliplein, supports Milliplein gift cards.

    Take Gift Card from Scanner?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports scanning the gift cards.
    YesIf Yes, enables scanning the gift cards.
    NoIf No, disables scanning the gift cards.

    IP Address of GiveX TPS

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter IP address for Givex TPS. Typically, this will be the

    BackOffice computer IP.

    SettingEnter the value. Example: 192.168.85.101

    GiveX TPS Communication Time Out

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter communication timeout for Givex.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-99;

    GiveX User ID

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter Givex user id. It will be unique for each site provided by

    Givex.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-10;

    GiveX User Password

    Purpose of PolicyGivex user password. Provided by Givex.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-10;

    GiveX Merchant ID

    Purpose of PolicyGivex Merchant ID. Provided by Givex.
    SettingEnter the value.

    Allow Adjustment for GiveX

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to adjust the value on GiveXcard. It helps the cashier

    when he prefer to enter the amount rather default amount.

    YesIf Yes, enables GiveX amount adjustment.
    NoIf No, disables GiveX amount adjustment.

    Gift Certificate Type for Tender

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy support tender for gift certificate. Used for EKO Milliplein interface.
    LocalGiftIf LocalGift, supports local gift cards.
    EKOIf EKO, supports EKO Milliplein cards.

    Integrated Loyalty Programs

    Manage and implement specific loyalty programs with points, discounts at the product level, category or store- wide level, and see how the program impacted sales through detailed reports tied to each loyalty promotion. All Loyalty promotion start and stop dates are automatic ensuring maximum profitability.

    Support KickBack Reward Points?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support KickBack reward points. This allows an option to swipe

    KickBack on MSR to get reward points. Earn points that spend like cash! Each point = $0.01. Earn KickBack® Points on the things you buy!

    YesIf Yes, supports KickBack reward points.
    NoIf No, does not support KickBack reward points.

    Customized Message

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup customized message to display for KickBack loyalty.
    SettingEnter the value; Example: Ask for KickBack Card or Enter Phone Number.

    IP Address of KickBack TPS

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter IP address for KickBack server.
    SettingEnter the value; Example: 173.195.232.2

    TCP Listening Port of KickBack TPS

    Purpose of PolicyTCP port for KickBack server communication.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-999999;

    KickBack TPS Communication Timeout

    Purpose of PolicyTimeout for KickBack communication.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-99;

    Minimum num. of points need to display redemption message

    Purpose of Policy0 means no limit. Otherwise, specify the minimum number of points required for

    processing redemption.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-999999;

    Support KickBack Reward Points at the Pump?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support KickBack cards at the pump. Once this policy is enable then

    it allows option to swipe the KickBack card to collect points at the Pay@Pump.

    YesIf Yes, supports kickback reward points at the pumps.
    NoIf No, does not support kickback reward points at the pumps.

    Inventory Management

    Manage incoming flow of products into the backroom. Measure the quantity of products received, produced, and determine product on hand in bulk.

    Allow Negative Stock Available?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy permits the point of sale computer to sell the product even the inventory levels

    of the product is negative. When stock receiving and stock takes are not done properly this event occurs.

    YesIf Yes, sales of a stock item are permitted even if the system stock level for the item

    goes negative

    NoIf No, message appears saying “Not Enough Stock to Sell That Quantity”.

    Do you accept product returns?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy permits negative value in quantity tab of point of sale allowing product

    return.

    YesIf Yes, negative values are allowed in quantity. Returns of previous sale are allowed.
    NoIf No, negative quantities are not allowed. An attempt to return a previous sale will

    include will include only those items that can be returned.

    Decimal place in Quantities

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows the product quantities displayed on POS screen and on receipts to

    show the specified number of decimal places.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-3;

    Decimal places in sell price

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows the product quantities displayed on POS screen and on receipts to

    show the specified number of decimal places.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-4;

    Product Management System is

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup product management system which is used for file extraction.

    Product management software lets you quickly load products, tag and organise them, track inventory levels, reduce the occurrence of out-of-stock items and keep shrinkage to a minimum.

    GeneralIf General, file extraction will be txt format.
    JDEIf JDE, file extraction will be in JDE format.

    Add returned stock to

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to manage inventory levels when stock item is return without

    incrementing the stock quantity.

    HoldIf Hold, place an item back in stock without incrementing stock amount. Item remains

    hidden until properly processed.

    In StockIf In Stock, Item returns in re-sellable condition are returned to stock with stock amount

    incremented.

    WasteIf Waste, Items returned in a condition that are not re-sellable are not added to stock.

    Allow Manual Entry

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to enter manual entry of barcode. This policy can be enabled in

    the event of improper functional state of scanners.

    YesIf Yes, stock codes or alternate codes for item may be manually keyed or scanned.
    NoIf No, an attempt to manually enter item will display message, “That Item cannot be

    Manually Entered”.

    Use reason for returns?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to select the reason when product is returned.
    YesIf Yes, a reason is requiring when returning an item.
    NoIf No, no reason is required.

    Restrict Sales to Authorization Level (0-10)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup customized message when a restricted product is sold, the

    cashier will be requested to confirm the condition associated with that restriction level.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-10;

    Keep Data for Returning Sale for _Days (0-365)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to store sales data in the database for specified number of day when

    items are returned.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-365;

    Type of cost used_

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides to choose type of cost used for inventory product pricing.
    StandardIf Standard, standard inventory pricing considered.
    AverageIf Average, average inventory pricing considered.

    Format to save Crystal Reports

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables an option to save crystal report in html or excel format.
    YesIf Yes, enables to save crystal reports in html or excel format.
    NoIf No, default crystal report screen will be available.

    Exempted tax for stock receiving

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to exempt tax from stock receiving.
    YesIf Yes, enables tax exemption from stock receiving.
    NoIf No, disables tax exemption from stock receiving.

    Eligible for Loyalty set to True by Default

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to change loyalty properties of an inventory item. Store can setup

    loyalty programs on certain products by default.

    YesIf Yes, enables loyalty eligibility of an item by default.
    NoIf No, disables loyalty eligibility of an item by default.

    Use Package Cost

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy considers package cost of inventory product.
    YesIf Yes, supports use of package cost.
    NoIf No, does not support use of package cost.

    Calculate Margin Based on _

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to calculate margin based on cost markup or profit markup.
    Cost MarkupIf Cost Markup, margin is based on cost markup profile.
    Profit MarkupIf Profit Markup, margin is based on profit markup profile.

    Note: The markup percentage can best be defined as the increase on the original selling price. For example, if a product costs $100, the selling price with a 25% markup would be $125. Gross Profit Margin = Sales Price – Unit Cost = $125 – $100 = $25.Markup Percentage = Gross Profit Margin/Unit Cost = $25/$100 = 25%. Sales Price = Cost X Markup Percentage + Cost = $100 X 25% + $100 = $125. The gross profit ratio or the gross margin ratio expresses the gross profit or gross margin amount as a percentage of sales. In our example the gross margin ratio is 20% ($2 divided by $10).

    Category in which all items that are supplies

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy displays specific category in which all items are supplies. In otherward it links

    department with supplies.

    YesIf Yes, enables display category in which all items that are supplies.
    NoIf No, disables display category in which all items that are supplies.

    Add Stock Item that is not found in the search list

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to add a stock item if not found in point of sale system.

    Add Stock item button will be enabled add the details.

    YesIf Yes, enable add stock item when item not found.
    NoIf No, disable add stock item when item not found.

    Excepted Stock Items for Inventory Valuation Report

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy exclude stock items from inventory evaluation report by department or stocks.
    YesIf Yes, enables exclusion of stock items from inventory valuation report.
    NoIf No, disables exclusion of stock items from inventory valuation report.

    Head Office has Priority for Cost and Vendor Transfer

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy changes priority settings by changing head office has priority for cost and

    vendor transfer.

    YesIf Yes, enables cost and vendor transfer priority for head office.
    NoIf No, disables cost and vendor transfer priority for head office.

    Excepted Stock Items for Stock Takes History

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to exclude stock items from stock takes history.
    YesIf Yes, enables exclusion of stock items from stock takes history.
    NoIf No, disables exclusion of stock items from stock takes history.

    Automatically Import Inventory Data

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to automatically load inventory based on the patch specified

    in the datapath.ini.

    YesIf Yes, enables automatically importing inventory data.
    NoIf No, disables automatically importing inventory data.

    Update all Vendors when Vendor Network values are updated

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to update all vendors when the stock item network values are updated.

    Once enabled all vendor prices will be changed.

    YesIf Yes, enables vendor price change for all list of price for an item.
    NoIf No, displays different price for all list of price for an item.

    Decimal Point for Cost in the Inventory Screens?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display decimal point for cost in the inventory screen.
    YesIf Yes, enables decimal point for cost in the inventory display.
    NoIf No, disables decimal point for cost in the inventory display.

    Excepted Stock Item for Shift Rec. Return Screen

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to exclude certain stock items from shift reconciliation report.
    YesIf Yes, enables exclusion of certain stock items from shift reconciliation report.
    NoIf No, disables exclusion of certain stock items from shift reconciliation report.

    Update Network Values when Active Vendor Values are updated

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy update network values when the active vendor values are updated.
    YesIf Yes, enables price update when active vendor values are updated.
    NoIf No, disables price update when active vendor values are updated.

    Select Which Department EZiPin Products belong to

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to select department for EZiPin.
    YesIf Yes, enables EZiPin department selection.
    NoIf No, disables EZiPin department selection.

    Check any Sales, before Deleting the Product?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts message to check for sales before deleting a stock item.
    Yes If Yes, prompts message before deleting a stock item.
    NoIf No, no warning message will be prompted.

    Print Barcode on Labels?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print barcode on labels. It requires certain requirements prior

    operating on the system.

    YesIf Yes, enables to print barcode on labels.
    NoIf No, disables to print barcode on labels.

    Files Structure to Export Date to a Handheld Device

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose file format for handheld device - access or text file
    Access DatabaseIf Access, file will be exported in access database format.
    Text FileIf Text, file will be exported in text format.

    Stock Ration for Inventory Levels Calculation is

    Purpose of PolicyStock ratio for inventory levels calculation. It’s used in back office inventory application.
    YesIf Yes, enables stock ratio for inventory levels calculations.
    NoIf No, disables stock ratio for inventory levels calculations.

    New Items are Added in the Last _ days

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy used by delete stock item from the back officein appropriate number of

    days.

    SettingValue ranges from 10 – 9999;

    Loyalty Programs

    Manage and implement specific loyalty programs with points, discounts at the product level, category or store- wide level, and see how the program impacted sales through detailed reports tied to each loyalty promotion. All Loyalty promotion start and stop dates are automatic ensuring maximum profitability.

    Do you use the Loyalty System?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows the system to support loyalty system on the point of sale computer.

    Once enabled loyalty buttons are available for loyalty pricing. A loyalty program is a rewards program offered by a company to customers who frequently make purchases. A loyalty program may give a customer advanced access to new products, special sales coupons or free merchandise.

    YesIf Yes, all other loyalty polices will be enabled including loyalty buttons on POS and

    loyalty discount or points printed on receipts.

    NoIf No, loyalty options are disabled with no loyalty buttons and no receipt information.

    Note: If this policy is set to ‘NO’, all other loyalty policies will have no effect. If “Use Customer” policy is set to ‘NO’, the ‘Customer’ button becomes disabled.

    Loyalty Type is

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy set to choose from list of loyalty type discussed in detail below.
    PointsIf Points, loyalty customers are awarded points for purchases. A “points” tender appears

    on the Tender screen. Receipts show “points earned and “points redeemed”.

    PricesIf Prices, loyalty customers receive the price designated by policy “Loyalty Price is”

    unless the policy is defined as zero, of which, the customer receives the regular price. Receipt will show a “Savings” line that displays amount of money saved. This is calculated as Quantity * (Regular Price – Loyalty Price).

    DiscountsIf Discounts, loyalty customers are assigned the Customer Discount Code defined by the

    Loyalty Discount Code is policy. Receipt shows a “Savings” line that displays amount of money saved. This is calculated as the sum of the all discounts from each item.

    Loyalty Price is (2-10)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup loyalty code used to reference to a specified loyalty price.
    SettingValue ranges from 2-10;

    Loyalty Discount Code is (0-99)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define customer discount code from the discount table that provides

    customers with discounts under a discount loyalty program. Active only if policy loyalty type is defined as discount, otherwise ignored.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-99;

    Loyalty Program Name

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define text string printed on receipt followed by word “member”.
    SettingEnter Loyalty program name. Example: Loyalty Club

    Point per Dollar Purchased

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define number of loyalty points awarded for each dollar of eligible

    goods purchased by the customer. Active only if policy Loyalty type is defined as points, otherwise ignored.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-1000;

    Exclude from Loyalty Program

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to exclude certain items from loyalty programs such as

    lottery etc.

    YesIf Yes, specified items are not included in the loyalty program. Sale of these items to a

    loyalty customer does not earn any special royalties regardless if specified.

    NoIf No, specified items are eligible to earn points, loyalty pricing/discounts.

    Note: active only if policy “loyalty type is” defined as points, otherwise ignored.

    Number of Points can be Redeemed / Transaction (0-No Limit)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to restrict number of loyalty points that may be redeemed in a single

    transaction. Zero (Default) is equal to no limit of points. Active only if policy “Loyalty Type Is” defined as points, otherwise ignored.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-100000000;

    Exclude from Redeemed Points

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to determine how many points the customer may use to pay for the

    sale when items are purchase.

    YesIf Yes, value of the total items purchased are deducted from the sale total before

    determining how many points the customer may use to pay for the sale.

    NoIf No, value of the total items purchased is included in the sale total before determining

    how many points the customer may use to pay for the sale. Active only if policy “Loyalty Type Is” is points, otherwise ignored.

    Award Points on Quantity

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy awards points based on the quantity of items purchased. Usually points are

    based on total amount of product purchase.

    YesIf Yes, points are awarded based on the quantity of items purchased.
    NoIf No, points are awarded based on the dollar value of item purchased. Active only if

    policy “Loyalty Type Is” defined as Points otherwise ignored.

    Points per Unit Purchased (0-1000)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows todefine number of points awarded for each unit of quantity

    purchased. Active only if policy “Loyalty Type Is” defined as points, otherwise ignored.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-1000;

    Show Total Points on Receipts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prints total points on receipt when product purchased.
    YesIf Yes, customer accumulated points are printed on each receipt.
    NoIf No, points earned and used are displayed on receipt but accumulated point total is

    omitted.

    Note: Active only if policy “Loyalty Type Is” defined as Points, otherwise ignored.

    Allow use of Current Sale Points in Payment

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to use current sale points as mode of payment.
    YesIf Yes, add points earned in current sale to the number of points available for

    redemption.

    NoIf No, use previous accumulated points available for redemption.

    Note: Active only if policy “Loyalty Type Is” defined as Points, otherwise ignored.

    Give Points on Points Purchases

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows customer to buy points. Store allows the customer to buy points and

    use those point at a later date.

    YesIf Yes, add points earned in current sale to the number of points available for

    redemption.

    NoIf No, uses historical accumulated points only to determine the number of points that may

    be redeemed

    Support Fuel Loyalty?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports localized fuel loyalty. Fuel loyalty lets you earn points at one place

    for every gallon of fuel purchased. To avail this points members simply enter their phone number or swipe a card at the pump. Coupon cannot be used at other site.

    YesIf Yes, adds points for fuel purchased and must redeem the coupon in same location.
    NoIf No, no changes observed and can redeem at any other location.

    Coupon Tender Name for Fuel Loyalty

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows tender name used for the coupon for fuel loyalty.
    SettingEnter the coupon tender name.

    Take Loyalty Card from Scanner?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports scanning of the loyalty card on point of sale computer.
    YesIf Yes, allows scanning loyalty card on point of sale system.
    NoIf No, does not allow scanning loyalty card on point of sale system.

    Support Cash Bonus Program?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports cash bonus program (co-op).
    YesIf Yes, supports cash bonus program (co-op).
    NoIf No, does not supports cash bonus program (co-op)

    Note: Cash Bonus is the functionality to give loyalty cash bonus coupons {equivalent to cash, but they can use in the next transactions as a tender} to the customer for fuel sales. (E.g. coupons like Atlantic co-op cash, Canadian tire money etc.). Cash bonus \ liter is configurable using the customer cash bonus chart. Different thresholds can be defined in the cash bonus chart. We can define either in different volume range threshold and \or in the multiple of liters. Member customers who are eligible for the cash bonus can be controlled using customer and customer group settings.

    Cash Bonus Name

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides name of the cash bonus program.
    SettingEnter Cash Bonus Name of program.

    Do you provide Till Cash Bonus Float?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports cash bonus float.
    YesIf Yes, supports cash bonus float.
    NoIf No, does not supports cash bonus float.

    Do you support Till Cash Bonus Draw/Drop?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy supports cash bonus draw and drop
    YesIf Yes, support cash bonus draw and drop.
    NoIf No, does not support cash bonus draw and drop.

    Exclude from Cash Bonus Program

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to exclude certain products from cash bonus program.
    YesIf Yes, support excluding from cash bonus program.
    NoIf No, does not support excluding from cash bonus program.

    Payouts

    Manages payouts to balance the cash drawer when adding / removing money during an open shift.

    Do you allow Payouts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to tender payouts on POS system. Payouts can be of any type

    such as lottery, merchant order etc.

    Yes If Yes, payout button appears on the options screen to process payment.
    NoIf No, payout button does not appear on options screen.

    Use Reason for Payouts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows toselect the reason while processing payouts. Prior enabling this

    policy, store manager should enter the reasons on data maintenance tab to select the reason.

    YesIf Yes, reason is required when payout is processed.
    NoIf No, reason is not required when a payout is processed.

    Pricing Policies

    Manage production of items and the dollar value of each product priced for the retail store. Provide real-time feedback to price’s on average pricing goals.

    Number of Pre-Defined Prices in use

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows maximum number of prices that may be defined for a product.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-10;

    Alert: This number is restricted to a maximum of 99 and must also be set in “Loyalty Price Number” otherwise

    the price based loyalty function will not operate properly.

    Strictly Enforce X-For Price

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to apply X-for pricing strategy for each item sold.
    YesIf Yes, X-for priced products revert to the regular price when the quantity purchased

    exceeds the X-amount.

    NoIf No, additional items purchased above the X-for amount are sold at the same price as

    the X-for price.

    Use Reasons for Price Changes?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to select the reason when product price has changed. In the event

    user must select one of the reason during price change. Also Price Change report can be available in crystal report.

    YesIf Yes, reason box appears on the POS screen when the user changes the price of an

    item.

    NoIf No, no reason screen appears.

    Group Pricing has Priority

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to apply group pricing as priority. Customer price group pricing

    allows you to price the items on an order based on the customer price group assigned to the sold to customer on the order whereas special pricing authorization enables partners to request special pricing on competitive deals.

    YesIf Yes, if both item special pricing and group special pricing apply to an item the latter

    shall be used.

    NoIf No, if both item special pricing and group special pricing apply to an item then item

    special pricing shall be used.

    Note: This is only a Company level policy, therefore, there can only be one setting.

    Strictly Enforce Incremental Prices

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enforce incremental pricing. It is the method of pricing a product

    based on incremental cost. In this type of pricing, the selling price of a product is

    determined by the variable cost, and not kept according to the overall cost of creating the product.

    YesIf Yes, Incremental pricing is applied in repeating groups with each group receiving the

    same price treatment.

    NoIf No, Incremental pricing is applied once and all units beyond the maximum defined

    are sold at regular price.

    Track Price Change?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to track price changes in POS and BO.
    YesIf Yes, enables price change tracking.
    NoIf No, disables price change tracking.

    Require Confirmation for Price Change (Y/N)?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to display a pop-up message that require confirmation for price

    change. Click Yes, if you would like to confirm price change. No, if you prefer not to change the price of an item.

    YesIf Yes, enables pop-up message for confirmation.
    NoIf No, disables pop-up message for confirmation.

    Allow Negative Price Entry

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to allow negative price. It helps in product return by

    setting up a hot button on point of sale computer.

    YesIf Yes, allows negative price.
    NoIf No, doesn’t allow negative price.

    Default Price Change based on Margin

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts change price based on margin by default. A margin pricing strategy

    allows you to define the pricing strategy for items based on margin targets.

    YesIf Yes, enables margin pricing strategy.
    NoIf No, disables margin pricing strategy.

    Display Warning Message in Stock Item Screen if Price is 0?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy display a warning message on the point of sale computer if stock item price

    is zero dollar amount.

    YesIf Yes, enables warning message if stock item price is 0.
    NoIf No, disables warning message if stock item price is 0.

    Set Price Equal to Cost by Default

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to set price equal to cost. When this policy enabled the

    price and cost of an item will be same with no profit margin.

    YesIf Yes, price equals to cost of an item default.
    NoIf No, varied pricing will be applied.

    Printing Options

    Manages various options to configure printing properties on C-Store Commander Point of sale and back office operations.

    Force Receipt Printing

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy force receipt printing automatically for every transaction made on POS. No

    Receipt option will be disabled.

    YesIf Yes, enables force receipt printing.
    NoIf No, disables force receipt printing.

    Expand Kits on Receipts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display expanded Kit information on the receipts.
    YesIf Yes, enables to display kit information on the receipts.
    NoIf No, disables to display kit information on the receipts.

    Show Receipt Till Close _ days

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to show previous till close reports for number of days specified here.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-365;

    Show Receipt Close Batch _ days

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to show previous close batch reports for number of days specified

    here.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-365;

    Print Company Name on Receipts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print company name on receipts. To enable, C-Store commander

    must register with InfoNet with appropriate information to get it display.

    YesIf Yes, name of company is printed on top of receipt.
    NoIf No, company name does not appear on receipt.

    Print Company Address on Receipts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print company address on receipts. To enable, C-Store commander

    must register with InfoNet with appropriate information to get it display.

    YesIf Yes, company address (2 Street lines, City, Province, and Postal Code) is printed on

    the receipt.

    NoIf No, company address does not appear on receipt.

    Print Phone & Fax Numbers on Receipts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print company phone & fax number on receipts. To enable, C-Store

    commander must register with InfoNet with appropriate information to get it display.

    YesIf Yes, company phone and fax numbers are printed on receipt.
    NoIf No, company phone and fax numbers do not appear on receipt.

    Receipt Header Justification is

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to adjust alignment of header information to be printed on the receipt.

    It can be Left, Right, and Centred on receipt.

    LeftIf Left, text alignment from left.
    RightIf Right, text alignment from right.
    CenteredIf Centered, text alignment from centre.

    Lines to Advance after Printing

    Purpose of PolicyReceipt is advanced specified number of lines after the last line is printed.
    SettingValue ranges from 0-20;

    Show Prices on Receipts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to show regular and sales price of product on the receipt. If both

    prices are the same then only the regular price appears.

    YesIf Yes, regular and sale price of product appear on receipt.
    NoIf No, unit prices do not appear on receipt.

    Show Stock Codes on Receipts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to show stock codes on receipt.
    YesIf Yes, each item will appear on two lines. The first will show the product code followed

    by the product description. The second line will show quantity, price, and amount information.

    NoIf No, Item will appear on one line showing description, quantity, amount, and price.

    Mask the Credit Card Number

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to mask the credit card number where the last 4 digits of card number

    will be printed by protecting customer information.

    YesIf Yes, credit or debit card number is printed on receipt as the first 4 digits followed by

    asterisks and the last 4 digits of the number.

    NoIf No, complete credit or debit card number is printed.

    Print Logo?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print company logo on the receipt.
    YesIf Yes, logo will be printed.
    NoIf No, logo will not be printed.

    How many copies do you want to Print Receipt?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow an option to print number of copies based on Sale Type (Sale, Refund,

    Payment, Payout etc.).

    SettingValue ranges from 1-9;

    Fuel Sales in Daily Sales are based on Totalizer Readings?

    Purpose of PolicyFuel sale in the daily sales report is based on totalizer reading or fuel sales.Pump

    reading are integrated with daily sales report. Fuel sales on POS instead.

    YesIf Yes, enables considering fuel sales in daily sales based on totalizer reading.
    NoIf No, enables considering fuel sales in daily sales based on POS data entry.

    Print Receipt for Void and Return?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print receipt automatically for void or return.
    YesIf Yes, enables print receipt for void and returns automatically.
    NoIf No, disables print receipt for void and returns automatically.

    Print Signature for Refund Sales

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy print signature for refunds.
    YesIf Yes, enables print signature for refunds.
    NoIf No, disables print signature for refunds.

    Print Card Holder Agreement for Fleet Card?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to prints card holder agreement for fleet cards.
    YesIf Yes, prints card holder agreement for fleet cards.
    NoIf No, no changes are observed.

    Display Printer list for Reports?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display printer list for reports on the back office computer. When

    this policy is enabled then while printing it will list out the choice to print. If you would like to save as soft copy then you choose “Cute PDF” from the list.

    YesIf Yes, enable display list of printers for reports.
    NoIf No, disable display list of printers for reports.

    Print User Name on Receipts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print username of cashier on the receipts.
    YesIf Yes, enables to print username of cashier on the receipts.
    NoIf No, disables to print username of cashier on the receipts.

    Print Coupon on a separate Receipts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to print coupon on a separate receipt.
    YesIf Yes, enable print coupon on a separate receipt.
    NoIf No, disable print coupon on a separate receipt.

    Print Store Code on Receipts?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to print store code on receipt.
    YesIf Yes, enable to print store code on receipt.
    NoIf No, disable to print store code on receipt.

    Print Receipts for Prepay Overpayment?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to print receipt for prepay over payment.
    YesIf Yes, enables to print receipt for prepay overpayment.
    NoIf No, disables to print receipt for prepay overpayment.

    Shift Number to Reprint Till Close Report for the day

    Purpose of PolicyShift number to print all shift report. If the shift is 0, then POS will not print all shift

    report.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-5;

    Force Fuel Receipt Printing

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print receipt for every fuel transaction automatically without any

    prompt message.

    YesIf Yes, enables force fuel receipt printing.
    NoIf No, disables force fuel receipt printing.

    Print Till Close Report on Two Columns

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print till close report on two columns.
    YesIf Yes, prints till close on two columns.
    NoIf No, prints till close on one columns.

    Export Daily Sheet to Excel?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to export daily sales sheet to excel format. To get Daily Sales report,

    BO > Reports > Daily Sales Report.

    YesIf Yes, enables exporting to excel format.
    NoIf No, disables exporting to excel format.

    Sounds

    One of the attractions of C-Store Commander is playing unique sound for each point of sale activity whether it’s inside store or outside. Playing different sound on each activity serves better user experience.

    Sound On Device Event

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enable sound on each point of sale event.
    YesIf Yes, produces sound on each event.
    NoIf No, produce no sound.

    Sound On Different Application Event

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables system for every other application that runs.
    YesIf Yes, produce a sound on the system for every other application that runs.
    NoIf No, produce no sound

    Sound On Pump Event

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables sound on the system for every pump activity.
    YesIf Yes, produce a sound on the system for every pump activity.
    NoIf No, produce no sound

    Alarm Sound Repeat Interval (min)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables to setup alarm sound repeat interval in minutes.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-60;

    Store Credits

    A simple and automated way of accepting returns with proof of purchase for an in-store credit printed directly from the receipt printer, and redeemable chain-wide.

    Do you issue Store Credits?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display store credits as tender and may be issued as change or as

    refund. This must be set to ‘YES’ if policy “Give Change on Gift Certificate Sale” is set to “Store Credit” or “Choice”. If not, then change should always be in cash regardless of that policy setting.

    YesIf Yes, store credits appear as tender and may be issued as change or as refund.
    NoIf No, store credits can neither be issued nor redeemed.

    Do you validate Redeemed Credits?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display list of store credits on tender screen display and the cashier

    may select one or more to complete the sale.

    YesIf Yes, selection of store credit on the tender screen will display a list of available store

    credits and the cashier may select one or more to complete the sale.

    NoIf No, value may be entered for the store credit amount with no validation of the store

    credit being performed.

    Give a Credit as Change on a Credits?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to choose list of options available for
    NeverIf Never, If change is computed on a sale involving store credit, the system issues cash

    for the change amount.

    AlwaysIf Always, If change is computed on a sale involving Store Credit, the system issues Store

    Credit for the change amount.

    ChoiceIf Choice, If change is computed on a sale involving Store Credit, you have a choice of

    issuing cash or credit. Selecting ‘YES’ will issue Store Credit. Selecting ‘NO’ will issue cash.

    Store Credits Expire in _ days

    Purpose of PolicyIf value is zero, Store Credits do not expire and the message “Valid Until Redeemed”

    appears on printed store credit. If value is greater than zero, that value is how many days credit is valid for. Message will appear on Store Credit saying that credit is valid for that number of days from issue date.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-1000;

    Tax Exempt Options

    This section allows to define monetary exemption which reduces taxable income. Tax exempt status can provide complete relief from taxes, reduced tax rates, or tax on only a portion of items.

    Use Override Code in Tax Exempt?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to override codes in the tax exempt module. In the event

    when customer would like to buy bulk quantity of tax exempted product then this policy allows to proceed to tender.

    YesIf Yes, enables override code in tax exempt sale.
    NoIf No, disables override code in tax exempt sale.

    Calculate Price Based on Tax Rate (AITE/QITE-Yes, SITE-No)?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows tax exempt price calculation is based on tax rate - rules are different

    for AITE/QITE/SITE.

    YesIf Yes, price calculated based on tax rate selected.
    NoIf No, regular price calculation will be applied.

    Tax Exempt Type

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose tax exempt type - SITE, AITE, QITE.
    SITEIf SITE, back office integrate with SITE application.
    AITEIf AITE, back office integrate with AITE application.
    QITEIf QITE, back office integrate with QITE application.

    How many copies do you want to print Tax Exempt Voucher?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup number of copies for tax exempt voucher to get print.
    SettingValue ranges 1-10;

    What’s the Age Restriction for Tobacco Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup age restriction for tobacco sale. Each province in Canada has

    its own regulations when it comes to tobacco sale to customers. Customers must be at least 19 years of age to be sold tobacco products. Before selling tobacco to any person who appears to be less than 25 years old, you must request identification (ID) and determine that the person is at least 19 years old.

    SettingValue ranges from 15-25;

    Note: There is no age requirement to sell tobacco products. Under the Smoke-Free Ontario Act an employee selling tobacco can be any age. However, the Ministry of Labour regulations under the Occupational Health and Safety Act set the minimum age of 14 years old to be eligible to work in Canadian workplaces.

    Reject the sale if the Card Holder is under 18?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to reject the sale if cardholder is under 18.
    YesIf Yes, reject the sale when card holder is under age 18.
    NoIf No, process the sale.

    Use Automation for TRA Files Processing

    Purpose of PolicyUse automation for TRA file processing.
    YesIf Yes, enables automation of TRA files processing.
    NoIf No, disables automation of TRA files processing.

    Authorize to use Tax Exempt Direct System

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to permit authorized user to use tax exempt direct application. InfoNet

    back office integrate with tax exempted software such as SITE, AITE, and QITE to process tax exempt transactions.

    YesIf Yes, enables to use tax exempt direct system.
    NoIf No, disables to use tax exempt direct system.

    How many digits of Site Treaty Number Card?

    Purpose of PolicyNumber of digits for SITE treaty number.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-15;

    Print Exempted Tax in Receipt?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display tax exempted amount on the receipt.
    YesIf Yes, enables tax exempted amount on the receipt.
    NoIf No, prints as a regular receipt.

    Required to collect Tax Exempt Customer Name

    Purpose of PolicyIf this policy is enabled, cashier has to enter the tax exempt customer name in the Tax

    exempt screen (for new customers).

    YesIf Yes, prompts to enter tax exempt customer name.
    NoIf No, no changes on the tender prompt.

    Required to collect Tax Exempt Customer Signature

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to collect British Columbiatax exempt customer’s signature. Prior

    enabling this policy store must integrate with signature pad.

    YesIf Yes, prompts signature pad window to collect tax exempt customer signature.
    NoIf No, no changes on the tender prompt.

    Mode for Collecting Tax Exempt Customer Signature

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose mode ofcollecting signature. Topaz signature capture is

    used with the Reader option.

    ManualIf Manual, customer must sign on soft copy.
    ReaderIf Reader, customer must sign on signature pad.

    Track Tax Exempt Inventory Separately?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to track tax exempt inventory items separately.
    YesIf Yes, enables to track tax exemption inventory.
    NoIf No, disables to track tax exemption inventory.

    Do you support Pay at the Pump Tax Exempt Sales?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows an option to support tax exempt sales at the pump (AITE only).
    YesIf Yes, supports Pay@Pump tax exempt sales.
    NoIf No, does not supports Pay@Pump tax exempt sales.

    Fuel Purchase for Band use only?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to process AITE transaction on pumps.
    YesIf Yes, supports tax exemptions tenders on pumps. It requires user to follow certain

    prompt along with regular display screen prior fueling.

    NoIf No, does not support tax exemptions tenders on pumps.

    Fuel Exception Reason Code for Band use

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup default fuel exemptions reason code for band use. Province

    such as Saskatchewan, when customer would like to have bulk gas then customer needs to select reason. In the event of failure then store might not able to get reward.

    YesIf Yes, enables fuel exception reason on Pay@Pump.
    NoIf No, disables fuel exception reason on Pay@Pump.

    Supports Real-Time Validation for SITE?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to supportreal time validation for SITE (RTVP-Saskatchewan). It

    requires treaty number from Saskatchewan Indian Tax Exemption body to configure it.

    YesIf Yes, support real time validation for SITE.
    NoIf No, does not support real time validation for SITE.

    Do you support Band Account for Tax Exempt Sales?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to support band account for tax exempt sales.
    YesIf Yes, support band account for tax exemption sales.
    NoIf No, does not support band account for tax exemption sales.

    Customer Settings Linked to Band Member

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup customer settings to band members.
    SettingEnter the value you wish to link band members.

    Customer Settings Linked to Non-Band Members

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows customer setting linked to non-band member todifferentiate band

    and non-band members.

    SettingEnter the value you wish to link band members.

    Band Account Number to identify the Band Member

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define starting band account number to identify band number.
    SettingEnter the value. Example: 555;

    Automatically identify the Band Member using Band Account?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to automatically identify band number using band

    account.

    YesIf Yes, enable identifying band member using band account.
    NoIf No, disable identifying band member using band account.

    Support Tax Exemption for Government Agencies

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support Tax Exemption for Government Agencies. Option to select

    the tax exempt customer from the customer screen and to enter the document number.

    YesIf Yes, supports tax exemption for government agencies.
    NoIf No, does not support tax exemption for government agencies.

    Use Ontario Gas Tax Refund E-Services

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support FNGTR - Ontario gas tax refund E-Services.
    YesIf Yes, supports Ontario gas tax refund e-services.
    NoIf No, does not support Ontario gas tax refund e-services.

    Collected Tax for Tax Exempt Customers

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to collect specific tax for tax exempt customers. Certain

    native Indian bands will collect GST for certain products. Certain bands want to apply PST for certain products.

    AddeIf Adde, value added tax will be applied.
    GSTIf GST, federal government tax will be applied.
    HSTIf HST, blended combination of PST and GST will be applied.
    PSTIf PST, provincial taxes will be applied

    Third Party Cards

    Easy connection to credit card clearing facilities for simple and inexpensive integration, providing payment choices for your customers and flexibility for your business.

    Third Party Loyalty Programs Name

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support third party loyalty cards such as Milliplein. A third-party

    source is independent of the first party and second party in processing transaction.

    MillipleinIf Milliplein, enable processing Milliplein card.

    IP Address of Third Party Card TPS

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enter IP address for TPS Milliplein.
    SettingEnter the third party IP address.

    TCP Listening Port of Third Party Card TPS

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enter TCP listening port of third party cards.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-999999;

    Third Party Card TPS Communication Time Out

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup timeout settings for third party cards such as Milliplein.
    SettingValue ranges from 1-99 seconds;

    Tender Screen Display only One Third Party Card?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display third party card option on tender screen display. Any third

    party card can be processed through this enabled tender option.

    YesIf Yes, enables tender screen display for processing third party cards.
    NoIf No, disables tender screen display for processing third party cards.

    Accumulate Loyalty Points?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows customer earn points on each purchase through third party cards such

    as Milliplein.

    YesIf Yes, enable loyalty point accumulation.
    NoIf No, disable loyalty point accumulation.

    Tender Screen Display Expiry Date for Loyalty Card?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display expiry date for loyalty card on the tender screen.
    YesIf Yes, display expiry date for loyalty card on tender screen.
    NoIf No, does not display expiry date for loyalty card on tender screen.

    Third Party Product Extraction Code

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy definesproduct extraction code for fuel products.Fuel products will be

    defined in the database based on third party programming requirements.

    3If 3, product extraction code is set to 3;
    5If 5, product extraction code is set to 5;
    6If 6, product extraction code is set to 6;
    7If 7, product extraction code is set to 7;
    10If 10, product extraction code is set to 10;

    Till End of Day Options

    This section allows to change polices related to end of day steps such as close the terminals, import financials to the back office and print the end of day reports.

    End of Day Display Method

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to display end of day –till close screen you must follow certain

    prompts to successfully close a till.

    YesIf Yes, till close screen shows system totals and allows entry of customer counts
    NoIf No, till close screen allows entry of customer totals only, does not display system totals

    or differences.

    Enter Cash Count when Closing

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enter tender count when closing a till. It will allow to balance system

    count and safe count.

    YesIf Yes, allows entry of tender counts when closing a till. “Show Tender Amounts For” and

    “Include in Close Tender” policies are ignored.

    NoIf No, closes till without user entry of tender counts.

    Copies of End of Day to Print

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup number of till close copies to be printed when till is closed.
    Setting Value ranges from 1-5;

    Show Codes in Product Sales Report

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print category, sub-category, and sub-detail codes in the till close

    report.

    YesIf Yes, category, sub-category, and sub-detail codes precede the names in the till close

    report

    NoIf No, category, sub-category, and sub-detail codes do not precede the names in the

    till close report

    Use Store Shifts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to select shift while opening a new till.
    YesIf Yes, shift definition enabled in back office. User will be asked to selectshift when

    opening a new till. ‘Close reports by shift’ can be generated.

    NoIf No, shift definition disabled in back office. User is not asked for shift when opening a

    new till. ‘Close reports by close number’ or date range can be generated.

    Allow Close with Suspended Sales

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to close till when transactions are suspended.
    YesIf Yes, till can still be closed if there are suspended sales for that till.
    NoIf No, till cannot be closed if till has any suspended sales.

    Enter Tender Amount For

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define the tender display. All tender together?
    Each TenderIf Each Tender, each tender is displayed separately and a count may be entered for it.
    All TenderIf All Tender, total of all tenders is displayed and a single count covering all tenders

    may be entered.

    Show only ‘Include in Close’ Tenders?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display include in close transactions when a till is close.
    YesIf Yes, only tenders marked as, “Include in Close” will be displayed when a till is closed.

    All other tenders are assumed to be balance with system totals.

    NoIf No, all tenders used will appear and counts can be entered for them.

    Shift 1 Starts in the _Day

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to system to choose automatic shift time based on start date and time

    of the shift falls.

    CurrentIf Current, shift 1 starts the day in which the start time falls. Applies only shift 1 spans

    midnight.

    NextIf Next, shift 1 starts the day in which the end time falls. Applies only shift 1 spans

    midnight.

    Allow Grouping by ‘EOD Groups’ in the Till Close Report?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display EOD groups in the till close report.
    YesIf Yes, supports displaying EOD group in the till close report.
    NoIf No, does not support displaying EOD group in the till close report.

    Close Batch when Till Close?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to do a batch close automatically along with till close. Batch processing

    refers to closing or settling an entire batch of transactions at one time.

    YesIf Yes, enable close batch along with till close.
    NoIf No, disable close batch along with till close.

    Support Automatic EOD?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support automatic EOD for Pay@Pump.This policy facilitates in

    supporting automatic end of day for Pay@Pump.

    YesIf Yes, supports automatic EOD for Pay@Pump.
    NoIf No, does not support automatic EOD for Pay@Pump.

    Automatic EOD Time

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup automatic end of day time. Shift will close automatically at

    the time specified here.

    SettingValue must in TT:MM:SS format;

    Change shift after Auto EOD?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to pick the change shift automatically after end of day.
    YesIf Yes, enable shift change after end of day.
    NoIf No, disable shift change after end of day.

    Automatically pick the Next Shift (it’s not based on time)?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to pick the next shift automatically. Shift selection will not be based on

    the shift time.

    YesIf Yes, enable automatic shift selection.
    NoIf No, disable automatic shift selection.

    Be able to Authorize Pumps when Closing the Till?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows cashier to authorize pump while closing the till. Normally while doing

    a till close no pump activity must be allowed as system reject processing till close.

    YesIf Yes, enable pump authorization while closing till.
    NoIf No, disable pump authorization while closing till.

    Automatic EOD Retry Limit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup automatic EOD(End of Day)retry limit in case of a failure

    (pumps active or bank connection error). Default is 5.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-10;

    Is it 24 Hours Store?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to setup your store as 24 hour store.
    YesIf Yes, enables 24 hours store timings.
    NoIf No, disables 24 hours store timings.

    Till Sales Options

    This class of policy defines objectives with respect to handling receiving and depositing cash or cards.

    Combine Common Lines in a Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to have multiple items of same UPC scanned to a single line with

    quantities incremented by one. Lines will not be combined if the price of the pre-existing line has been changed or a discount was given.

    YesIf Yes, multiple items will be combined into a single line with quantities incremented by

    one.

    NoIf No, multiple entries of the same item will produce multiple lines in the sale.

    Do you provide a Till Float?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts you to enter till float amount when new shift is started. Till float are

    pre-defined for each till to speed up start and end of day.

    YesIf Yes, opening a new till will request a till float amount to be entered.
    NoIf No, no till float is requested. Value is set to zero internally.

    Suspend Empty Sales

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to suspend an empty sale. Suspend Sale can be used if a customer

    forgot an item or forgot his money in the car. Instead of letting the other customers wait, the cashier can simply suspend the sale and then carry on with the next customer.

    YesIf Yes, the “Suspend” button can suspend a sale with no items.
    NoIf No, message displays as “Cannot Suspend. No lines in Sale” appears when “Suspend”

    button is touched. Sale is not suspended.

    Delay after Printing Receipts (seconds)

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set number of seconds to display “Change Screen” at the end of

    each sale. Touching the screen will close it sooner that the time specified here. If this value is zero, the screen disappears as soon as processing is complete.

    SettingValue ranges from 0-60;

    Swipe Credit Cards?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to swipe the credit cards on MSR.
    YesIf Yes, when a credit or debit card is selected on the tender screen a textbox and

    button are displayed. Card may be swiped or manually entered. If a card is swiped, the box and button disappear. If manually entered, the box closes and card processing commences by touching “Done” or “Enter”. The box will be re-displayed if there was an invalid card swipe or number. If the “Cancel” button is touched, the box & buttons disappear and no money appears in the “Amount” box. If the card selected does not match the card entered, an error is message is displayed.

    NoIf No, cards are not capable of being entered on the tender screen.

    User Clear Till Warning

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to display warning message to alert the cashier to clear till.
    YesIf Yes, display warning message if amount in till exceeds specified amount.
    NoIf No, no warning message displayed

    Clear Till if Currency is more than _ Dollars

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup maximum amount allowed in till before “Clear Till Warning”

    message is displayed.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-10000;

    Show Keypads

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allow to display numeric keypad when required. For instance when you would

    like to do price change, show keypad facilitates enabling keypad on POS screen.

    YesIf Yes, numeric keypads are displayed on all screens where needed.
    NoIf No, keypads do not appear.

    Numeric Entry Style

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display number of decimal places after a value keypad.
    DecimalIf Decimal, enables decimal place for numeric entry.
    0If 0, no decimals are allowed. ex:5
    1If 1, 1 decimals are allowed. ex:5.0
    2If 2, 2 decimals are allowed. ex:5.00
    3If 3, 3 decimals are allowed. ex:5.000

    Credit Card Mode is

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to validate credit and debit cards.
    Cross-RingIf Cross-Ring, No credit or debit card validation occurs. Amounts are entered on tender

    screen but no card information is collected.

    ValidateIf Validate, full credit and debit card processing occurs.

    Allow Item Price Change

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to change price of an item on point of sale computer. In the event

    when wrong price is entered or discount is not applied or damaged product, user change product price.

    YesIf Yes, cashier can change price for the specified item. Policy “user can change prices”

    must be true for this policy to work.

    NoIf No, cashier cannot change price of item.

    Allow Item Quantity Change

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to change quantityof an item on point of sale system. In the

    event when quantity must be multiplied manually, user change product quantity.

    YesIf Yes, cashier can change the quantity of specified item. Policy “User can Change

    Quantity” must be true for this policy to work.

    NoIf No, cashier cannot change quantity of item

    Open Drawer On

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to open cash drawer as required.
    Every SaleIf Every Sale, cash drawer will open at end of every sale.
    As NeededIf As Needed, cash drawer will open only if there is change to be paid out or the sale

    involves a tender kept in cash drawer.

    Show a Drawer Open button

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enable cash drawer button point of sale screen.
    YesIf Yes, “Cash Drawer” button appear on screen.
    NoIf No, “Cash Drawer button does not appear on screen.

    Confirm Deletes of Lines in a Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display warning message to delete
    YesIf Yes, message will confirm whether or not to delete line. This also applies to voiding

    a sale except for message referring to ‘sale’ instead of ‘line’.

    NoIf No, lines or sales are deleted without seeking confirmation.

    Share Suspended Sale

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to cancel suspended transaction from any till. If policy “Allow

    Close with Suspended Sales” is set to ‘NO’, then no tills may be closed in the Back Office while there are suspended sales.

    YesIf Yes, all till generating a suspended sale may be unsuspended by that or any other

    till.

    NoIf No, each till has its own suspended sale and can be unsuspended by that till only. If

    policy “Allow Close with Suspended Sale” is set to ‘NO’ then that specific till cannot be closed while it has a suspended sale.

    Use Reasons for Void Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to select reason for void a sale.
    YesIf Yes, selection of a reason is required when voiding a sale.
    NoIf No, no reason is required to void a sale.

    Use Reasons for Open Cash Drawer?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to select reason to open cash drawer. Reason to open cash drawer

    is not required at the end of a sale.

    YesIf Yes, Selection of a reason is required to open the cash drawer.
    NoIf No, Reason is not required to open cash drawer.

    Use Reasons for Cash Draw?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to select reason for cash draw. User must select the reason when

    cash draw is required.

    YesIf Yes, enables use reason for cash draw.
    NoIf No, disables use reason for cash draw.

    Tender Screen Display only One Fleet Card

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display only one fleet card option on tender screen. When customer

    uses different credit card, this options allows to process every fleet card that InfoNet supports.

    YesIf Yes, supports one fleet card button on tender screen display.
    NoIf No, does not support fleet card button on tender screen display.

    Tender Screen Display only One Credit Card

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to display only one credit card option on tender screen. It allows to

    process all credit, master card, visa card using single button.

    YesIf Yes, supports one credit card button on tender screen display.
    NoIf No, does not support credit card button on tender screen display.

    Warn User when only _ invoice numbers are left

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to warn user only when the specified number of invoices left.C-

    Store Commander ensure to have 10 digit number for invoice.

    SettingValue ranges from 500 – 500000;

    Display Fuel Coupon Message?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to display fuel coupon message.
    YesIf Yes, enables display of fuel coupon message.
    NoIf No, disables display of fuel coupon message.

    Fuel Coupon Message Threshold Value

    Purpose of PolicyThreshold value to display fuel coupon message
    SettingValue ranges from 1-1000;

    Fuel Coupon Type

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to choose fuel coupon type. Alerts the cashier to explain about

    promotion on fuel.

    Fuel CouponIf Fuel Coupon, fuel coupon prompt settings will be displayed.
    Fuel StampIf Fuel Stamp, fuel stamp prompt settings will be displayed.
    Litre LogIf Litre Log, litre log prompt settings will be displayed.

    Till is Locked Automatically after Inactivity?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy lock the till computer (POS application)automatically after inactivity.

    Inactivity refers to not functioning or not being used any user.

    YesIf Yes, till computer gets locked after inactivity.
    NoIf No, till computer remains same even after inactivity.

    Till/Back Office is locked automatically after _ minutes

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup inactivity time frame for locking till/back office applications
    SettingValue ranges from 1-144 minutes;

    Back Office is locked automatically after Inactivity?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy lock the back office application automatically after inactivity. Inactivity refers

    to not functioning or not being used any user.

    YesIf Yes, back office gets locked after inactivity.
    NoIf No, back office remains same even after inactivity.

    Use Envelope Number for Cash Drops?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter envelope number for cash drop.
    YesIf Yes, enables display message when cash drop buttons is touched on point of sale.
    NoIf No, disables display message when cash drop buttons is touched on point of sale.

    Checkup-sell Sales?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows you to track upsell sales (Sales after tender). This is to track

    commissions for employees.

    YesIf Yes, tracks upsell sales information.
    NoIf No, does not track upsell sales information.

    Input Serial Number for Vendor Coupon?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter serial number for vendor coupon.
    YesIf Yes, enable to enter serial number for vendor coupon.
    NoIf No, disabled to enter serial number for vendor coupon.

    Support Scale Item and Lotto-Quebec?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support scalableitem and/orLotto-Quebec. When this policy is

    enabled then system supports both lotto Quebec and scalable items if it is based on price.

    YesIf Yes, supports scale item and lotto Quebec.
    NoIf No, does not support both scale item and lotto Quebec.

    What’s the UPC Format System?
