    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to choose UPC format for scalable item. The Universal Product Code

    symbology is used throughout the U.S. and Canadian retail. UPC-A barcodes carry GTIN-12 numbers as defined by GS1 US. In its standard version (UPC-A), the bar code consists of a five digit manufacturer number and a five digit product number.

    UPC-AIf UPC-A,UPC-A format is enabled for scanning. The barcode identifies the

    manufacturer and specific product so point-of-sale cash register systems can automatically look up the price. Note that the UPC-A and EAN-13 codes are valid worldwide, so products marked with a UPC-A code can be sold outside the USA. The UPC-A Code and the assignment of manufacturer ID numbers is administered by GS1.

    EAN-13IfEAN-13,EAN-13 format is enabled for scanning. January 1, 2005 is the date by

    which all retail scanning systems in the USA were required to accept the EAN-13 symbol as well as the standard UPC-A. This change will eliminate the need for manufacturers who export goods to the US and Canada to double-label their products.

    UPC-A10If UPC-A10, UPC-A10 format is enabled for scanning.

    How many digits of the item code for Scalable Items?

    Purpose of PolicyNumber of digits for scalable item code
    SettingValue ranges from 1-6;

    How many digits of Amount/Weight for Scalable Items?

    Purpose of PolicyNumber of digits for scalable item amount/weight
    SettingValue ranges from 1-7;

    Do you print weight in Scalable Item UPC?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to print weight for scalable item UPC. For instance if store uses Weight,

    POS takes quantity from the UPC and get the price from inventory setting, then calculate the total amount (Amount = Quantity * Price) for the item. If the store uses Amount, POS takes the line total amount from the UPC and get the price from inventory setting, then calculate the quantity (Quantity = Amount / Price).

    YesIf Yes, POS takes quantity from the UPC and get the price from inventory setting, then

    calculate the total amount (Amount = Quantity * Price) for the item.

    NoIf No, no changes will be observed.

    Note: 1. For scalable items, Quantity field and Price field are disabled in the POS sale screen to prevent cashier changing quantity/price and messing up the amount. 2. The digit between Item code and Amount/Weight is never consistent. It's actually thecheck digit for the Amount/Weight. So basically POS has no way to tell the UPCincludes Amount or Weight. Most of the stores will use Amount and even though somestores may use Weight but they won't use Amount and Weight both format at the samestore Sample UPC: Barcode format for scalable item and Lotto Quebec is the same. In your bar code, you have the PLU code and price/weight of the product. Barcode format - 2DDDDDCPPPPC The first number 2 is the KEY, to identify the type of bar code. DDDDD is the PLU number. C is the check digit.PPPP is the price/weight of item. C is the check digit. For bar code: 200999505759, PLU is 00999 and quantity is 0.575.

    Expand all the Barcodes (UPC-A, UPC-E, Other) to 13 Digits?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to expand barcodes to EAN 13 digit format.UPC-EAN conversion.

    When product has 6 digits on the barcode then system will automatically converts to 13 unique digits.

    YesIf Yes, enables barcode extension to 13 digits.
    NoIf No, disables barcode extension to 13 digits.

    Sell Inactive Item?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to sell inactive item from the inventory. In the event when product is

    not available, store managers change items to “Inactive” to avoid selling the product.

    YesIf Yes, enables selling inactive item from inventory.
    NoIf No, disables selling inactive item from inventory

    Use the leading Zero if you have UPC-A Standard?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy use leading 0 for UPC-A standard for scalable item; When this policy is set

    then every scanned item will initially start with ‘0’.

    YesIf Yes, enable leading zero if you have UPC-A standard.
    NoIf No, disable leading zero if you have UPC-A standard.

    Use the check digit if you have UPC-A Standard?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy provides an option to use check digit for UPC-A standard scalable item.
    YesIf Yes, enables to use check digit if you have UPC-A standard.
    NoIf No, disables to use check digit if you have UPC-A standard.

    Keypad format in Login Screen

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to choose keypad format for login screen.
    DIf D, decimal format will be enabled in login screen.
    2If 2, two digit decimal format functionality will be enabled.

    Use Predefined Till number

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to use predefined till number. Till number has to specified in the

    Datapath.ini file.

    YesIf Yes, enables predefined till number. When enabled the user cannot be able to choose

    any till number while login to the system.

    NoIf No, disables predefined till number.

    Use Penny Adjustment

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to support penny adjustment to round the penny.
    YesIf Yes, supports penny adjustment to round the penny.
    NoIf No, does not support penny adjustment to round the penny.

    Time Sheets

    An employee timesheet is a method for recording and tracking the amount of an employee's time spent working. The employee timesheet can report total hours worked or time spent working on a specific task or job

    What is your Pay Period?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set payroll type for employees working in a store. A pay period is

    a recurring length of time over which employee time is recorded and paid. Examples of pay periods are weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, and monthly.

    Bi-WeeklyIf Bi-Weekly, then pay period type is set to bi-weekly.
    Semi-MonthlyIf Semi-Monthly, then pay period type is set to semi-monthly.

    Specify the Interval you want to use to round hours in min.

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to define interval time in rounding the minutes to hours. For example

    if employee spent 7 hours 15 minutes then system will calculate to 7 hours 30 minutes.

    YesIf Yes, rounding hours in minutes will be enabled.
    NoIf No, rounding hours in minutes will be disabled.

    User have User ID in addition to User Code and User Name

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to have user ID along with user code and user name. This options for

    reporting purpose only.

    YesIf Yes, supports combination of user id and user code or username.
    NoIf No, supports only user id.

    Initial Value for User ID

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set initial value for user id for accounting purpose.
    YesIf Yes, supports initial value for user id.
    NoIf No, does not support initial value for user id.

    Can Users view all Timesheet Reports?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to view timesheet reports.Timesheet reports includes payroll

    information, overtime hours etc.

    YesIf Yes, enables to view all timesheet reports.
    NoIf No, disables to view all timesheet reports.

    Can Users edit and Post Timesheet Reports?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to edit and post timesheet. In the event when timesheets reports

    to be corrected then this policy prompts to update the employee’s information.

    YesIf Yes, enables users edit and post timesheet reports.
    NoIf No, disables users edit and post timesheet reports.

    Can Users change Check-In/Check-Out time for Employees?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to change check-in/check-out time for employees. This policy

    must be enabled for Managers or operators of store.

    YesIf Yes, enables user change settings for employees.
    NoIf No, disables user change settings for employees.

    Create PY file for Timesheet

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to create PY file (txt file) to process payroll information of an

    employee to send it to head office. Prior enabling this policy, accounting system is required to integrate with C-Store commander.

    YesIf Yes, enable PY file for payroll process.
    NoIf No, disable PY file for payroll process.

    Hours to calculated overtime for Salaried Employees

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows set number of hours for each salaried employee must work per week

    to be eligible for overtime.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-100; example: 40 hours per week.

    Hours to calculated overtime for Hourly Paid Employees

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set number of hours for each hourly paid employee must work per

    week to be eligible for overtime payment

    SettingValue ranges from 1-100; example: 44 hours per week.

    Use Signature Pad in Timesheet

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to capture signature of each employee to approve individual’s

    timesheet report.

    YesIf Yes, system prompts to capture signature of employee to approve timesheet report.
    NoIf No, no prompt message will be displayed.

    Unattended Fuelling

    Unattended Fuelling defined as self-service motor fuel dispensing facilities. Unattended Fueling save time and money helping your fleet customers get on the road faster.

    Training Mode enabled or not

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup training mode for unattended fuelling store. Once this policy

    enabled the user prompts to follow the screen prior fuelling.

    YesIf Yes, training mode is enabled.
    NoIf No, training mode is disabled.

    Training mode Start Time

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter training mode start time for unattended fuelling.
    SettingEnter the start time in HH:MM:SS format.

    Training mode End Time

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter training mode end time for unattended fuelling.
    SettingEnter the start time in HH:MM:SS format.

    Training ID Length

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set training ID length.
    Setting Value ranges from 3-6;

    Maximum Training Steps

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup number of training steps for unattended fuelling. If this policy

    is set to 4 then user prompts to answer 4 different question prior fuelling.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-12;

    Expiry Period of Training ID, based on Month

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup expiry period for training id in months
    Setting Value ranges from 1-99;

    User Authorizations

    Manages users (Owner, Manages, Cashiers) security, administrative settings. Creating user profiles on back office allows to apply this user authorization policies.

    User can Maintain Data

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to store data in the back office. In user authorization policy class, you

    can setup the policy in which how it should be applied to user levels. You can set the implementation level of policy to Company, User Group, User etc. If the policy is set to user then the policy applies to selective users.

    YesIf Yes, user can maintain information in the back office including Customers, User,

    Registers, Tills, and Shifts.

    NoIf No, user cannot maintain data.

    User can Maintain Inventory

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to store inventory information such as stock items, stock code allowing

    them store it as categories, subcategories in back office.

    YesIf Yes, you can maintain information for stock item and categories in back office.
    NoIf No, you cannot maintain inventory data.

    User can Receive Data

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows toreceive data from various stores. Chain stores when connected to

    head office able to received data. This data includes inventory list, promotion, fuel price etc.

    YesIf Yes, you can enter stock receiving information in back office.
    NoIf No, you cannot enter data pertaining to stock receiving.

    Note: store receiving menu must be available to receive stock receiving information.

    User can Sell Products

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to sell products. It is one of the basic policy to set in retail store.
    YesIf Yes, you can sell products that are available in the inventory.
    NoIf No, you cannot sell the products.

    User can Perform Bank Close

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables to do a close batch whenever it’s required. Usually store set a policy

    to do a close batch at end of day shift.

    YesIf Yes, you can do a bank close or close batch or end of day shift.
    NoIf No, you cannot perform end of day shift.

    User can Change Prices

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows any authorized user to change the price. It includes fuel and inventory

    item prices.

    YesIf Yes, you change product prices, This will only work if changing prices on a product is

    allowed.

    NoIf No, you cannot change product price.

    User can Change Quantity

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows any authorized user to change the product quantity.
    YesIf Yes, you can change product quantities. This will only work if changing quantities on

    a product is allowed.

    NoIf No, you cannot change product quantities.

    User can give Refunds

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows any authorized user to give refunds. Refund must be product refund.
    YesIf Yes, you can process refunds.
    NoIf No, you cannot process refund.

    User can give Discounts

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows any authorized user to give product discounts on POS system.
    YesIf Yes, you can give discounts.
    NoIf No, you cannot give discounts.

    Note: Keep in mind product discount is different from fuel discount. Discount can be in dollars and percentage.Get to know about your stores discount policy prior activating this policy.

    User can Override AR Limit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to overrides AR Limit.
    YesIf Yes, you can authorize AR customers balance
    NoIf No, you cannot override the AR customer’s credit limit.

    User can process A/R Sales

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to process A/R sales.
    YesIf Yes, you can make sales charged to an AR account.
    NoIf No, you cannot make sales to an AR account.

    User can do Cash Draws

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to perform cash draw from POS cash drawer.
    YesIf Yes, you can perform a cash draw.
    NoIf No, you cannot do a cash draw.

    User can Cash Drops

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to perform cash drop from POS cash drawer. To perform this

    action, store must have ‘Safe Management’.

    YesIf Yes, you can do a cash drop.
    NoIf No, you cannot do a cash drop.

    Note: While enabling this policy, ensure you also set ‘Enter Reference Number’ = Yes, under Policy by Class > Business Practices. This way you can able to track the cash drop by reference number.

    User can Print Reports

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables user to print reports from back office.
    YesIf Yes, you can print reports if you have a data pertaining to your required report.
    NoIf No, you cannot print any reports.

    User can Void Sales

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to maintain information in the database in back office including

    customers, users, registers, tills and shifts.

    YesIf Yes, you can do a void sale.
    NoIf No, you cannot do a void sale.

    User can Setup POS Screen

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts users to define screen layouts and content for the hot buttons and

    cash draw screens. Prior to this, stock items must be available in the database to setup.

    YesIf Yes, you can setup POS screens.
    NoIf No, you cannot setup POS screens.

    User can Change Policy Settings

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to manage policy settings in back office.
    YesIf Yes, you can change policy settings.
    NoIf No, you cannot change policy settings.

    User can perform Till Audit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to perform Till Audit.
    YesIf Yes, you can perform a till audit.
    NoIf No, you cannot perform a till audit.

    User can Change Customer Status

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows authorized users to change customer’s status. Customer status….
    YesIf Yes, you can change a customer’s status.
    NoIf No, you cannot change a customer’s status.

    User Sales Authorization Level is

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users set authorization level of restriction on certain restricted products

    being sold must be at least equal to the restriction level otherwise the user is not allowed to sell the product.

    SettingValue ranges from 1-10;

    User can Purge the Transaction Data

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables authorized users to purge data.
    YesIf Yes, you can purge data.
    NoIf No, you cannot purge data.

    User can Change the Password

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables authorized users to change the password on POS.
    YesIf Yes, you can change password.
    NoIf No, you cannot change password.

    User can Change Fuel Price

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables authorized users to change fuel price.
    YesIf Yes, you can change fuel price.
    NoIf No, you cannot change fuel price.

    User can perform Fuel Setup

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to setup pumps and tanks.
    YesIf Yes, you can perform fuel setup.
    NoIf No, you cannot perform fuel setup.

    Use Pin Pad Entry

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables to use Pinpad.
    YesIf Yes, you can use Pinpad.
    NoIf No, you cannot use Pinpad.

    User can do Write Offs

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to do write offs. Once enabled the “Write Off” button will be

    enabled to perform write off action, if not ‘Write Off” button will be disabled.

    YesIf Yes, you can perform write off.
    NoIf No, you cannot perform write off.

    User can change Price Increment for Fuel?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy enables users to change price increment for fuel.
    YesIf Yes, you can change price increment for fuel.
    NoIf No, you cannot change price increment for fuel

    User can perform Till Close

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to perform till close.
    YesIf Yes, you can perform till close.
    NoIf No, you cannot perform till close.

    User can print A/R Closing Report

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to print A/R closing report. Keep in mind that AR closing reports

    will be generated on monthly basis.

    YesIf Yes, you can print AR closing report.
    NoIf No, you cannot print AR closing report.

    User can close A/R Account

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to close A/R closing account. Keep in mind that prior closing AR

    closing account the balance needs must be paid.

    YesIf Yes, you can close AR account.
    NoIf No, you cannot close AR account.

    User can process Totalizer Reading

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to process pump totalizer reading while closing the till.
    YesIf Yes, you can process pump totalizer readings when closing till
    NoIf No, you cannot process totalizer readings

    User can go over the Price Limit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to increase the price limit of product.
    YesIf Yes, you can increase the price of an item.
    NoIf No, you cannot increase the price of an item.

    User can do TM Audit

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to access TM audit feature of fuel management application. It’s

    typically used by territory managers to audit a site.

    YesIf Yes, you enable TM audit feature.
    NoIf No, you disable TM audit feature.

    User can sell Manual Fuel Sale?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows the users to sell manual fuel sale. Manual fuel sale must be allowed

    when the InfoNet system and/or banking is down.

    YesIf Yes, you can process manual fuel sale.
    NoIf No, you cannot process manual fuel sale.

    User can process Run Away?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows the users to process run away. Run Away referred to customer who

    dispense the fuel without pay. This is considered a Run Away transaction. To complete the transaction, bring the sale from the basket and place it into the sale line. Then simply click on the run away button.

    YesIf Yes, you can process run away.
    NoIf No, you cannot process run away.

    Note: User must enter run away time, vehicle number and identification detail for regulatory purpose.

    User can do Bottle Return

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to process the bottle return in the event when customer would like to

    return the bottle.

    YesIf Yes, you can process bottle return.
    NoIf No, you cannot process bottle return.

    Bottle Return Limit for User

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to set limit of bottle returns for each users.
    SettingValue ranges from 0-1000; if value is set to 1 for each user ‘X’ then user ‘X’ can process

    only one bottle return.

    User can Add Stock in POS?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to add inventory items on point of sale computer.
    YesIf Yes, you can add stocks in POS.
    NoIf No, you cannot add stocks in POS.

    User can Manually change Fuel Price?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to change the fuel price manually.
    YesIf Yes, you can process manual fuel price.
    NoIf No, you cannot process manual fuel price.

    User can change Stock Item settings

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to change stock item settings that includes
    YesIf Yes, you can change stock item settings.
    NoIf No, you cannot change stock item settings.

    User can process Tank Dip Reading?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to process tank dip reading. Dip tank means a container holding

    a gasoline that is used for dipping or coating. An object may be immersed (or partially immersed) in a dip tank or it may be suspended in a vapor coming from the tank to measure its level of volume.

    YesIf Yes, you can process tank dip reading.
    NoIf No, you cannot process tank dip reading.

    User can Suspend and Unsuspend Sales?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to suspend and unsuspend sale. A transaction will be suspended

    usually when you have a lineup of customers and the person being served has not gathered all the items they wish to purchase. By suspending the transaction you can serve the next customer without having to re-enter any of the items already scanned into the system. As a user you cannot have any suspended transactions at the end of the day. All sales must be completed. Unsuspend option allows to serve which is previously put on hold.

    YesIf Yes, you can suspend and unsuspend sale.
    NoIf No, you cannot suspend and unsuspend sale.

    Activate the ‘Exact Change’ button on the POS?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to enable Exact Change button on the POS. Exact Change Button

    prompts you to quickly complete a sale that is paid in Cash with no change required. When completing a sale with exact change there will be no receipt printed.

    YesIf Yes, you can enable exact change button.
    NoIf No, you cannot enable exact change button.

    User can accept Fleet Payments

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to accept fleet payments. Fleet Cards is a credit card that allows

    businesses to manage expenses associated with the vehicles that they own and operate. Fleet cards are used to pay for the fuel, repair, and maintenance of vehicles, and are provided by a business to personnel who operate those vehicles.

    YesIf Yes, you can process fleet payments
    NoIf No, you cannot process fleet payments.

    User can Open Cash Drawer?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to open cash drawer whenever immediate cash is required. Cash

    Drawer prompts you to select the reason for cash drawer opening.

    YesIf Yes, you can open cash drawer whenever it’s required.
    NoIf No, you cannot open cash drawer whenever it’s required.

    User can process Pump Test?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to process pump test. A pump test is a procedure carried out by

    a pump manufacturer, supplier, installer, or owner to confirm one or more aspects of pump design or performance.

    YesIf Yes, you can process pump test.
    NoIf No, you cannot process pump test.

    User can process Init Debit?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to initialized debit machine. Initialization of debit must be done

    in the event when store received a new pinpad from the bank.

    YesIf Yes, you can process debit initialization.
    NoIf No, you cannot process debit initialization.

    User can make Products Inactive?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to make a products inactive.
    YesIf Yes, you can make a products inactive.
    NoIf No, you cannot make a products inactive.

    User that can Print the Last Receipt with Voucher

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to print the last receipt with voucher from the last print option of

    point of sale. While generating EZiPin voucher, this policy allows to get approval code in the last receipt.

    YesIf Yes, you can print the last receipt with voucher.
    NoIf No, you cannot print the last receipt with voucher.

    User can Activate/Deactivate Post Pay?

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to activate/deactivate post pays option on point of sale. Upon

    enabling user can activate or deactivate postpay whenever it’s required.

    YesIf Yes, you can activate or deactivate postpay whenever it’s required.
    NoIf No, you cannot activate or deactivate postpay whenever it’s required.

    User can reject Fuel Price Changes from Head Office

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to reject fuel price change from head office. In the event when

    cashier is taking the customer while fuel price change message pop up, this policy helps in not changing the policy.

    YesIf Yes, you can reject the price change from head office.
    NoIf No, you cannot reject the price change from head office.

    User can edit Total Section in PO Details

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows users to update purchase order total section information in purchase

    and receiving section of inventory tab.

    YesIf Yes, you can edit total section in PO details of purchase and receiving.
    NoIf No, you cannot edit total section in PO details of purchase and receiving.

    User can Override Cigarette Sale Quota

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows user to override cigarette sale quota to tax exempted customers. Tax

    exemption store prefer to activate this policy in event when they want to override the cigarette quota.

    YesIf Yes, you can override the cigarette sale quota.
    NoIf No, you cannot override the cigarette sale quota.

    User System Settings

    User System Settings allows system administrators and users several levels of control over configuration of POS. Administrator can easily navigate, modify permissions and settings allowing right users to operate and use POS and system resources.

    Force User ID on Every Sale

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows toenter user id on every sale.Stores with more security procedures

    enable this policy to protect each transaction.

    YesIf Yes, the system requires to enter user id before each sale begins.
    NoIf No, the system uses current user id on each new sale.

    Default User ID to

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows tohave current user as user for next sale when the system is forcing

    the entry of user id on each sale.

    YesIf Yes, the current user idas the user for the next sale when the system is forcing the

    entry of a user id on each sale.

    NoIf No, Displays a blank user id when the system is forcing the entry of a user ID on each

    sale.

    Require Password on User Change

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to enter password when new user starts the shift. Usually after every

    8 hours of store shift, employee logins with new user id and password.

    YesIf Yes, requires a password when the system is forcing entry of a user id on each sale.
    NoIf No, No password required.

    Force Authorization on VOID sales

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy prompts to sign in authorized user on voiding a sale.
    YesIf Yes, requires an authorized user to sign on to void a sale if the current user is not

    authorized.

    NoIf No, displays message, “You are not authorized to void sale”.

    User can log on more than One Till

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to access more than one till. Administrator/Managers prefer to choose

    this policy if they wish to troubleshoot certain times.

    YesIf Yes, users can log on more than one till.
    NoIf No, users cannot log on more than one till.

    Default User for Fuel Only System

    Purpose of PolicyThis policy allows to setup fuel sales screen on the point of sale system.
    YesIf Yes, only pump icons are displayed on the POS screen.
    NoIf No, only pump icons along with sales screen displayed on the POS screen.

    Policies by Entity

    Policies by entity have the same effect as policies by class and allow for making changes by Department, Stock Item, Sub-Department, Sub-Detail, User and User Group. This ability gives an owner/manager in making required changes on a global basis to specific products, groups of products, users etc. in an efficient and effective way.

    📷 Figure 6-2 Policies by Entity Window

    Chapter 7

    🔒 End of Day Operations

    CHAPTER 7

    Routine Maintenance

    The Routine Maintenance tab in Back Office provides access to job schedules and parameters. The routine maintenance functionality allows you, if authorized, to create and schedule jobs, edit currently scheduled jobs, or remove jobs.

    The routine maintenance function allows you to create and schedule data movement jobs such as parameter distributions, file transfers, and the run of reports. A job is a system process that can be scheduled to perform an operation in Back Office. When a job runs, the system writes the data from an imported file to the database in the system. For example, the Job Manager can choose to import a file of price promotions and then schedule the system to execute those price promotions at a specific time. The job manager function allows you to add jobs, edit existing jobs, select registers as recipients of jobs, enter notifications of failure and success of jobs, and view and edit job settings.

    📷 Figure 7-1 Routine Maintenance menu window

  • Click the Back Office > Routine Maintenance > Till Close tab.
  • Select one of the available options:
  • Close a Specific Till
  • Close a Shift
  • Close all Tills
  • Select a store shift, enter all of the bills and coin information.
  • Select Complete Till Close button to close the till.

    • 📷 Figure 7-2 Routine Maintenance menu window

    📷 Figure 7-3 Shift Reconciliation Window

    📷 Figure 7-4 Shift Reconciliation Window

    📷 Figure 7-5 Shift Reconciliation Window

    Note: Overtime calculations are based on 44 hours a week. Statutory holidays is time and half for hourly employees and regular hours for salary employees (salary employee will take a day off in the next 3 weeks). Bi-weekly method of pay is fully implemented. Semi-monthly is implemented with overtime being more than 88 hours in a pay period. Statutory holiday is the same as for bi-weekly.

    To Check-In or Check-Out:

    1. Click the Back Office > Routine Maintenance > Check-In and Check-Out tab.
    2. Once the users are checked in then users will be available followed by clicking View Current or ViewHistory button of Timesheets dialog box. View Current displays employees login details, hours, commission etc. If you would like to view historical employee hours, click View History.
    3. Click Check-In button if you would like to login to system.

    📷 Figure 7-6 Check-In or Check-Out window

    Safe Management

    Safe management process includes the following procedures:

    • Setup opening balance
    • Receiving
    • Loan
    • Pickup
    • Bank Deposit
    • Safe Management Report

    Figure 7-8 Safe Management window

    Price Book Update

    Price Book Update allows manage multiple stores having C-Store head office running on your computer by transferring product related changes to stores. This option is used to create and send product related information to stores inclusive of price changes, product cost, price groups, regions or vendors. Various options such as new products, cost changes, promotions, department structures and hot buttons can also be sent. Price book update allows to setup price change, stocks, cost change, commodity, promotions, and hot buttons operation.

    To do a price book update:

    1. Click the Back Office > Routine Maintenance >Price Book Update.
    2. Select where you want to send the changes to by Region, Store, PriceGroup or Vendor.
    3. Select Stocks in the Data Transfer Type box.
    4. Click on the Stock View tab followed by Recent Changes.

    📷 Figure 7-9 Price book update window

  • Click on Sell for each item that you want to send or click on Select all.
    1. Click on Save as Pending Transfer button.
    2. Click Ok button in the Records are save for Stock Transfer.
    3. Click on the Stock List tab.
    4. On the right side of the screen, you will have a list of stores who will receive the communication. If you want to cancel a transfer to a store, select the store and click on Delete Store Data.
    5. Click on Reports, this will give you a report of all changes that will be sent.
    6. When you’re ready to send the changes, click on Transfer button.

    Accounting Integration

    InfoNet C-Store Commander integrates with various accounting software’s such as Simply Accounting, ACCPAC, SAGE 50 etc. This integration allows use the same data files and information for real time updating. The data is consistent across all modules, offering maximum integrity so that you save time and avoid double entry. It allows you to send sales, purchases and inventory adjustment transactions from point of sale computer to the accounting software.

    GL Interface – Simply Accounting Integration

    The GL interface allows for the mapping of accounts (“SimplyAccts”) between the C-Store Commander’s BackOffice program and Simply Accounting. In order to use SimplyAccts from Simply Accounting a user must use the DDE export option in Simply Accounting and create a text file named “Accounts.txt”. In this file, the first field must be an account number and the second field the description of the account. All other fields will be ignored. WARNING: If other fields are selected instead of the account number and description, BackOffice will consider the first field as the account number and the second field as an account description.

    If the first field does not have a numeric value, the record will be rejected; otherwise, it will be considered as a valid Simply Accounting account and it will then be loaded into the BackOffice database. The second field is assumed to be the account description. Only the first four (4) characters from the line will be considered as an account number and the rest of the line will be the account description (50 characters maximum).

    Sample data (Accounts.txt):

    1000 Current Assets 1020 Cash to be deposited 1030 Cash Draws 1050 Petty Cash 1055 Saving Bank Account 1060 Chequing Bank Account

    1067 Foreign Currency Bank 1070 Total Cash 1080 Visa 1083 MasterCard You then must copy the “Accounts.txt” file to “C:\C-CtoreCommander\In” folder. Use the BackOffice- RoutineMaintenance-Accouting Integration-Account Mapping option to import the SimplyAccts. If the file is found then the user will see a prompt to import the data. If the answer is “Yes”, all SimplyAccts will be imported from the txt file into an Accounts table in our database. If the answer is “No”, the file import will not be processed.

    Mapping the SimplyAccts

    You must map the SimplyAccts that are related for transaction types such as "Sales", "Tenders", “Taxes”, and Charges etc. You have the option to link each dept., tender or tax to a different SimplyAccts. For all other transactions only one account is allowed. The only validation that is done is that the SimplyAccts has to be valid in the Accounts table (imported from Simply Accounting). If the user links an incorrect but valid SimplyAccts, to a transaction type and/or dept. or tender, the data export will be processed using that SimplyAccts and sent to Simply Accounting for import and if only all transactions imported from the BackOffice are balanced.

    📷 Figure 7-10 Back Office and Accounts Mapping window

    want to import accounts from Simply Accounting at all or if you’re going to define the account in Simply Accounting after processing the file export from the BackOffice).

    Processing data for Exporting to Simply Accounting

    If any links exist between transactions in Back Office and Accounts (i.e. there are records in the BackOffice AccLink table), the “See Balance” button will be enabled. Once this button is clicked, data will be processed based on the closed tills to that point.

    Alert: you must maintain a consistent close period for processing. All tills that have been closed and have yet to

    be processed will be part of the new balance. You will see both the last processed close till number and last

    available till close number to be processed (this will be the last till close number displayed in the Close Tills in the

    BackOffice).

    📷 Figure 7-11 Simply Accounting window

    button you will be able to enter an adjustment amount. In the last line of the grid, you can then enter adjustments as either a debit or credit and assign n account number for these adjustments as well as a description of the adjustment (optional). More than one adjustment can be entered using either the same or different accounts. After balancing occurs the “Process Data” button will be enabled. Once this button is clicked, all data from the Simply Accounting Back Office screen will then be populated to produce a txt file that can then be imported into Simply Accounting. The file is named Balance.txt and will be created in C:\C-StoreCommander\Out folder.

    Sample data - Balance.txt:

    09-14-12, "435",

    1000,-303.37,

    1055,868.73,

    1070, 8769.52,

    1089, 13.75,

    3300,-838.02,

    4200,-8510.61,

    You can then use the Simply Accounting import option to import the “Balance.txt” file into the Simply Accounting system.

    ACCPAC Integration

    ACCPAC Integration for C-Store Commander allows the user to post Sales, and other financial activities from C- Store Commander to ACCPAC. The information that can be posted over is upon based on either of the following:

    • Till Close Number,
    • Shift Date or
    • Date Range

    The data generates an accounting journal entry and then transfer the data to a journal entry for posting into ACCPAC. This process eliminates the need to enter Sales and other data from C-Store manually into ACCPAC. It also helps to eliminate the possibility of entry errors when posting on a daily, weekly or monthly basis. ACCPAC Integration is found in the Back Office Main screen under ‘Routine Maintenance’. When the user clicks on ‘ACCPAC Integration’ on the main screen, all sales data that have not yet been processed to ACCPAC, is displayed. If there is no sales data then the message ‘No data available!’ will display, and ‘Last close number was already processed.’ is displayed.

    📷 Figure 7-12 Initial Screen with Sales Data Loaded Window

  • Till Close Number,
  • Shift Date or
  • Date Range

    • Integration by Till Close Number

    When the till is closed, it is given a Till Close Number. If ‘By Till Close Number’ is selected as a criteria, the till information is displayed to assist the user to select the right record.

    Shift Date

    During a given day there can be more than one shift and during one shift there can be more than one till operating. When ‘Shift Date’ is chosen as criteria, the user needs to select the date for which he/she wants to generate a journal entry, as well as the actual shift. When selecting a shift date, the user is not able to select either the date that was already processed or the date for which the till has not yet closed. If there is only one shift then that shift is automatically selected. If there is more than one shift, then ‘All Shifts’ is selected by default.

    Date Range

    The user needs to select the starting and ending dates inclusively, for which the journal entry will be generated. Once again the user cannot select dates that were processed nor that have not happened yet. When the needed criteria is selected, the user needs to click ‘See Balance’ to generate a journal entry. Depending on the criteria chosen, this process may take a while. Once the journal entry is generated, the user needs to make sure that the Totals in the journal entry are balanced.

    📷 Figure 7-12 Integration by Till Close Number Window

    Note ‘Enter Adjustment’ is not available when the totals are in balance.

    Once clicked on ‘Enter Adjustment’, the user needs to enter the ACCPAC Account and Description of that account. Also, the user needs to enter the amount, depending on where the user clicks (Debit or Credit) the amount will be generated for the user, so there is no need to calculate the difference between debits and credits. If the user is happy with the entry, clicking ‘Save’ will adjust the Totals. If the user needs to add two entries, then the amount generated will not be in balance, the user needs to enter it’s an adjusting amount to balance the entry and then press anywhere in the grid to save the amount entered. If satisfied with the entry, the user needs to click ‘Save’

    in order to post the second entry. Once again, depending whether the entry is a Debit or Credit, the required balancing entry will be generated.

    Integration - Adjusting Totals

    Once Totals are in balance, the user can view the Sales Detail Report by clicking on ‘Sales Detail Report’. Once the Report is displayed the user can click on “Print Report’ to print the displayed Sales Detail Report. To close the Sales Detail Report the user must click on ‘Close Sales Report’. The user cannot generate another journal entry until the Sales Detail Report is closed. However, the user can proceed with the processing of the journal entry when viewing the report. If the user only wishes to view the report and/or print and does not want to process the journal entry, then the user can click on ‘Close Balance’ to close the generated journal entry.

    📷 Figure 7-13 Integration by Adjusting Totals Window

    📷 Figure 7-13 Integration by Sales Detail Report Window

    ACCPAC Setup Requirements
    • Prior to generating and posting journal entry, the user needs to configure Back Office and ACCPAC so

    that they can work together. There are two things the user has to do.

    • First, the user needs to enter the required accounts from ACCPAC into BackOffice and second of all, user

    needs to map those accounts to the appropriate Back Office data.

    • To get ACCPAC Accounts, all the user needs to do is click on ‘Synchronize Accounts’. This process gets all

    the accounts from ACCPAC. Now that the user knows which accounts are used in ACCPAC, he/she can map them to the BackOffice data.

    • To map accounts the user needs to click on ‘Accounts Mapping”. Here there are six Transaction Types

    (Categories) that have to be mapped: Sales, Tenders, Taxes, Payouts, Write-offs, and Runaways.

    • Since sales are processed by departments, the user needs to map each department to the appropriate

    ACCPAC Account.

    📷 Figure 7-14: BackOffice and ACCPAC Accounts Mapping Window

    Send Data to HO & Get Data from HO

    C-Store Commander Head Office is a component of C-Store Commander Point of sale system that enables data replication from C-Store Commander Head Office and C-Store Commander POS system for retail operations. This section provides a detailed overview of the functionality of the system. It allows stores to exchange data between head office and POS systems in the store. It includes the following three components.

    📷 Figure 7-10 Get Data from HO (Head Office) window

  • C-Store Commander Back Office – It allows to send business sales information to head office system.
  • C-Store Commander Head Office – It allows to synchronize access the data between two systems that

    • performs change tracking of business data and the scheduling of the data transfer. Advanced features such as scheduled data transfer, automation can be configured.

    • C-Store DTS Transfer – It performs the actual data transfer connecting head office system and retail

    back office system.

    To send data to HO

    1. Click the Back Office > Routine Maintenance > Send Data to HO tab.
    2. Click Yes

    To get data from HO

    1. Click the Back Office > Routine Maintenance > Get Data to HO tab.
    2. Select from the list of Outstanding Data Files dialog box followed by click Get Data button on the right.
    3. Click Report button when you would like to add any information to head office team.
    4. Click Print button when you would like to print any report.
    1. Click Upload button when you want send sales data to head office.
    2. Click See Log Files button when you would like to see any error message while getting data from head office.
    Chapter 8

    📎 Appendix A

    CHAPTER 8

    Freeze

    The Freeze tab in Back Office provides you an option to lock your computer while away from it. This security measure is to prevent unauthorized user accessing your back office computer.

    📷 Figure 8-1 Freeze menu window

  • Click the Back Office > Freeze menu tab.
  • Upon selection your screen will be locked and prompt you to enter your credentials to unlock the terminal.
  • To unlock the computer, enter any key followed by password to unlock the computer.

    • 📷 Figure 8-1 Freeze menu window

    Chapter 9

    📋 Appendix B

    CHAPTER 9

    Exit

    The Exit menu tab in Back Office provides you an option to terminate your back office application.

    📷 Figure 9-1 Exit menu window

    POLICIES: these are the options selected in the Program for the required management of a store. These range from accounting integration to tax exemption support.

    POS PROGRAM: the C-Store Commander POS and pump control software system

    PRODUCT CATEGORIES: product classification involves the establishing of general product categories (fuel, tobacco, lottery etc.); sub-categories (premium, scratch, etc.) and details (litre, can, pack, carton etc.) as best fits and meets the needs of the store and the products it sells.

    PUMP AUTHORISATION: when a cash/attendant must authorize a pump through the POS Program main screen by pressing on the pump that is calling.

    REASONS & RESTRICTIONS: defined limitations for the sale of products and or the conveying of discounts, voided sales and other actions.

    REGISTER: see till

    STOCK CODE: code as defined in BO Program for those items without a UPC.

    STOCK ITEMS: all those products (items) sold using a product code and/or UPC

    STOCK RECEIVING: the process of receiving products delivered to a store into the store’s inventory through an inventory control process.

    STOCK TAKES: the process of taking (removing) products from a store out of the store’s inventory through an inventory control process.

    TENDERS: these are the defined methods of payment that are accepted at the store and each POS: cash (CDN, USD, Euro), credit (VISA, MC, AMEX, Diner’s Club etc.; Debit, Gift Card, Fleet card, In House account, AR customer etc. as accepted by a retailer.

    TILL: this is the POS computer system used to complete fuel and merchandise purchases in the store.

    TRAINING MODE: this is the ability to use a designated POS/Till for training allowing a cashier to train on the system without that sales activity to be recorded in the sales log with no effect upon inventory.

    UPC: Universal Product Code, see Bar code

    Note: Back Office computer, maintaining optimum performance. Please keep in mind that Windows is the underlying operating system of your Back Office computer and you need to follow normal Microsoft prescribed routines in order to maintain maximum operating efficiency. Please endeavour to re-start your computer at least weekly and do not use your computer for any other purpose than as a Back Office computer. If you must use it for any other purpose please ensure that you put in place anti-virus and other appropriate security software. Also please keep in mind the most recent requirements under PCI Data Security Standards as are required by merchants/retailers at http://www.pcicomplianceguide.org/
